    • 雁鹜 - Geese and ducks.
    • 干將 - Legendary swordsmith.

    Emperor Xuan of Han had hair on his feet, and wherever he lived, there was always a radiant glow.
    Yan Han of the Jin dynasty was known for his filial piety. His brother, who had taken too many medicines, died at home. Yan Han then opened the coffin and revived him. His mother, wife, and family members were all exhausted, but Yan Han completely withdrew from social affairs, serving his brother for thirteen years without ever showing signs of fatigue.
    Song Yan, who served as chancellor of Qi, was dismissed and returned home. He summoned the gatekeeper Tien Rao and twenty-two others, saying, "Are there any of you gentlemen who can help me join another lord?" Tien Rao and the others all remained silent and did not respond. Song Yan said, "Alas! Scholars and officials are easy to find but hard to use." Tien Rao replied, "It is not that scholars and officials are easy to find but hard to use; it is that you cannot use them. If you cannot use them, you will have an unfair heart, which means you are first examining yourself before criticizing others." Song Yan asked, "What does it mean to first examine oneself before criticizing others?" Tien Rao said, "Three dou of grain is not enough for scholars, but you have more than enough for your geese and ducks. The fruits and nuts from your garden are thrown around by the women in your harem, but scholars do not get a single taste. The luxurious silk and brocade are worn by your concubines, but scholars and officials do not get them as their salary. Wealth is something you take lightly, but death is something scholars take seriously. You cannot give up what you take lightly and make scholars achieve what they value. It is like having a dull knife stored away while expecting a sharp one to be used—how difficult is that!" Song Yan felt ashamed and left his seat, saying, "I have made a mistake." [59]

    楚王聘庄子，庄子曰：“吾闻神龟死三年，置巾藉之而藏之宗庙堂之上。此宁死为贵乎宁其生曳尾于泥中矣。”遂不赴楚聘。
    　　汲黯不乐为淮阳相，固辞之。帝曰：“卿可卧理之”。

    　　●卷下
    　　赵简子死而未葬，而中牟叛，五日，襄子举兵攻之。围未匝，而城自坏者十丈，襄子令退军，吏谏曰：“君殊有罪而城自坏者，天助也。曷为退”襄子曰：“吾闻于叔向曰：‘君子不承人之危，不扼人之险。’使其理城后攻之。”中牟闻之，请降。 [60]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [60]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 楚王聘庄子 - The King of Chu invited Zhuangzi.
    • 吾闻神龟死三年，置巾藉之而藏之宗庙堂之上 - I heard that a divine turtle, after dying for three years, was placed on a mat and then stored in the ancestral temple.
    • 此宁死为贵乎宁其生曳尾于泥中矣 - Is it better to die with honor or to live and drag one's tail in the mud?
    • 遂不赴楚聘 - And thus he did not go to Chu for the invitation.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 楚王 - The King of Chu, a powerful state during the Warring States period.
    • 庄子 - Zhuangzi, a famous philosopher and writer of the Daoist school.
    • 神龟 - Divine turtle, a symbol of longevity and wisdom in Chinese culture.
    • 宗庙堂 - Ancestral temple, a place where ancestors are honored and sacrifices are made.

    Proper Nouns

    • 楚王 - The King of Chu.
    • 庄子 - Zhuangzi.
    • 神龟 - Divine turtle.
    • 宗庙堂 - Ancestral temple.

    The King of Chu invited Zhuangzi, who replied, "I have heard that a divine turtle, after dying for three years, was placed on a mat and then stored in the ancestral temple. Is it better to die with honor or to live and drag one's tail in the mud?" Thus, he did not go to Chu for the invitation.

    Ji An was not happy to be the governor of Huaiyang and firmly refused the position. The emperor said, "You can govern while lying down."

    In the subsequent chapter,

    When Zhao Jianzi died but had not yet been buried, Zhongmou rebelled. After five days, Xiangzi raised an army to attack them. Before the siege was fully encircled, ten zhang of the city wall collapsed on its own. Xiangzi ordered the army to retreat. The officials advised, "Your lord has committed a grave sin, yet the city wall collapsed on its own. Isn't this a sign of divine assistance? Why should we retreat?" Xiangzi replied, "I heard from Shuxiang that a gentleman does not take advantage of another's peril, nor does he exploit another's danger. Let them repair the city wall before we attack." Zhongmou heard this and surrendered. [60]

    太康畋于洛之表，十日不返。其弟五人辇其母以从。终失国。
    　　汉王欲以赵王如意易太子，吕后问计于张良。曰：“南山有四皓，隐而不仕于秦。太子卑辞延之，若四老人到，扶太子，一助也。”于是东园公、夏黄公、角里先生、绮里季皆随太子入谒。高帝曰：“吾得天下，不到。今从吾儿游，何也？”四老曰：“陛下侮慢，臣等耻来。今太子贤明，臣故佐之。”于是太子乃定。高祖谓戚夫人曰：“羽翼已成，难动摇矣。”
    　　张嘉为相州刺史，至都，询故事。皆云前后太守多不生出郡城，苟不流死则贬。嘉按其图籍，自后周尉迟迥死王事始也。乃为迥立庙，四时享之。后三年，入拜大金吾。到吴竞加以冕服，而其后皆荣迁去。 [61]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [61]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 太康畋于洛之表 - 太康是传说中的中国皇帝，畋于洛之表意为在洛阳郊外打猎。
    • 十日不返 - 意为十天没有返回，表示长时间的缺席。
    • 辇其母以从 - 辇意为用车载，以从意为跟随，表示他的弟弟们用车载着母亲去跟随他。
    • 终失国 - 终意为最终，失国意为失去国家，表示他的行为最终导致国家的丧失。
    • 汉王欲以赵王如意易太子 - 汉王指刘邦，赵王如意是赵王，易太子意为更换太子，表示汉王想用赵王如意替换太子。
    • 吕后问计于张良 - 吕后是刘邦的皇后，问计于张良意为向张良询问计策，表示吕后寻求张良的建议。
    • 南山有四皓 - 南山指山名，四皓是四位隐士，表示南山有四位隐士。
    • 卑辞延之 - 卑辞意为谦卑的言辞，延之意为邀请，表示用谦卑的言辞邀请他们。
    • 若四老人到，扶太子，一助也 - 若四老人到意为如果四位老人到来，扶太子意为支持太子，表示四位老人的到来将支持太子。
    • 高帝曰：“吾得天下，不到。” - 高帝是刘邦的另一个称呼，吾得天下，不到意为我得到天下，但没有来，表示刘邦的缺席。
    • 今从吾儿游，何也 - 今从吾儿游意为现在跟随我的儿子，何也意为为什么，表示询问他们为何跟随。
    • 陛下侮慢，臣等耻来 - 陛下是皇帝的尊称，侮慢意为轻视，臣等耻来意为我们感到羞耻而来，表示他们对皇帝的轻视感到羞耻。
    • 今太子贤明，臣故佐之 - 今太子贤明意为现在的太子贤明，臣故佐之意为我们因此支持他，表示他们支持太子。
    • 高祖谓戚夫人曰：“羽翼已成，难动摇矣。” - 高祖是刘邦的另一个称呼，羽翼已成，难动摇矣意为翅膀已经长成，难以动摇，表示太子的地位稳固。
    • 张嘉为相州刺史，至都，询故事 - 张嘉是人名，为相州刺史意为担任相州刺史，至都，询故事意为到达都城，询问旧事，表示他询问历史惯例。
    • 皆云前后太守多不生出郡城 - 皆云意为都说，前后太守意为前后任太守，多不生出郡城意为大多不居住在郡城之外，表示一种普遍看法。
    • 苟不流死则贬 - 苟不流死则贬意为除非被流放或死亡，否则会被贬职，表示一种后果。
    • 嘉按其图籍 - 嘉是人名，按其图籍意为查阅其记录，表示他查阅了历史记录。
    • 自后周尉迟迥死王事始也 - 自后周尉迟迥死王事始也意为从后周尉迟迥为国事而死开始，表示一个历史参考。
    • 乃为迥立庙，四时享之 - 乃为迥立庙意为为尉迟迥建立庙宇，四时享之意为四季祭祀，表示一种祭祀仪式。
    • 后三年，入拜大金吾 - 后三年意为三年后，入拜大金吾意为被任命为大金吾，表示一种晋升。
    • 到吴竞加以冕服 - 到吴竞是人名，加以冕服意为赐予冕服，表示一种礼仪上的荣誉。
    • 而其后皆荣迁去 - 而其后意为后来，皆荣迁去意为都得到了晋升，表示一系列的晋升。

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 太康 - 传说中的中国皇帝，以狩猎闻名。
    • 洛阳 - 古代中国的首都，位于河南省。
    • 汉王 - 刘邦的另一个称呼，汉朝的创始人。
    • 赵王如意 - 汉朝的王子，常在历史文献中被提及。
    • 吕后 - 刘邦的皇后，汉朝初期的重要人物。
    • 张良 - 刘邦的著名谋士，以战略建议著称。
    • 四皓 - 四位隐士，隐居不仕于秦，受到尊敬。
    • 高帝 - 刘邦的另一个称呼，强调其帝王身份。
    • 戚夫人 - 刘邦的宠妃，以影响力和悲剧命运著称。
    • 相州 - 古代中国的省份，位于现在的河南省。
    • 尉迟迥 - 北周时期的著名人物，以忠诚和为国事而死著称。
    • 大金吾 - 京师卫戍司令的高级军职。
    • 吴竞 - 历史上的人物，可能是高级官员或学者。

    Emperor Taikang hunted in the outskirts of Luoyang for ten days without returning. His five younger brothers carried their mother to join him. Ultimately, he lost his kingdom.

    Emperor Gaozu of Han wanted to replace the crown prince with Prince Zhao. Empress Lu asked Zhang Liang for advice. Zhang Liang said, "There are four worthies in the South Mountain who lived in seclusion and did not serve Qin. The crown prince should humbly invite them. If the four elders come, they will support the crown prince, which will be a great help." Thus, Dongyuan Gong, Xialuangong, Jueli Master, and Qiliji all accompanied the crown prince to pay homage. Emperor Gaozu said, "I have gained the empire, but I have not been here. Now they are following my son, why?" The four elders said, "Your Majesty is disrespectful, and we are ashamed to come. The current crown prince is wise and virtuous, so we support him." Thus, the crown prince was secured. Emperor Gaozu told Consort Qi, "The wings are already formed, it is difficult to shake them."

    Zhang Jia served as the governor of Xiangzhou and inquired about past practices when he arrived in the capital. It was said that past and present governors mostly did not live outside the city walls, unless they died in exile, then they were demoted. Zhang Jia examined the records and found that starting from the death of Wei Zhi Jiong in service to the state during the Northern Zhou dynasty. He then built a temple for Wei Zhi Jiong and worshiped him four times a year. Three years later, he was appointed as the Grand Commandant of the Capital Garrison. When he reached Wu Jing, he was bestowed a ceremonial robe and hat, and later all received promotions. [61]

    汉耿寿昌置常平仓，贱时籴粟，贵时减价，恤民，以成为国之体也。
    　　唐崔群为相，清名甚重。元和中，自中书舍人知贡举。既罢，夫人李氏因暇日常劝其树庄田以为子孙之计。笑答曰：“余有三十所美庄良田遍天下，夫人复何忧”夫人曰：“不闻君有此业。”群曰：“吾前岁放春榜三十人，岂非良田耶”夫人曰：“若然者，君非陆相门生乎然往年君掌文柄，使人约其子简礼，不令就春闱之试。如君以为良田，则陆氏一庄荒矣。”群惭而退，累日不食。
    　　唐魏元忠神气刚直，初为洛阳令，有罪戮于都市，已坐讫。天后以元忠有平徐敬业之功，特敕免之。承制者走而传呼释元忠。传呼先至，执捉者扶令起，元忠曰：“敕未至，岂可求生”有顷方至。观者咸服其安闲神异也。 [62]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [62]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 置常平仓 - Establishing a granary system to regulate grain prices.
    • 贱时籴粟 - Buying grain at low prices.
    • 贵时减价 - Reducing the price when it is high.
    • 恤民 - Showing concern for the people.
    • 以成为国之体也 - To establish a model for the state.
    • 知贡举 - Being in charge of the imperial examinations.
    • 树庄田 - Establishing estates.
    • 美庄良田 - Beautiful estates and good farmland.
    • 春榜 - The list of successful candidates in the spring examination.
    • 陆相门生 - Students of the former prime minister Lu.
    • 掌文柄 - Holding the power of literary selection.
    • 使人约其子简礼 - Having someone tell his son to avoid the spring examination.
    • 陆氏一庄荒矣 - The Lu family's estate is now barren.
    • 戮于都市 - To be executed in the marketplace.
    • 坐讫 - To be seated for execution.
    • 天后 - Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
    • 特敕免之 - To specially order his release.
    • 承制者 - The person carrying out the imperial decree.
    • 传呼释元忠 - To announce the release of Yuanzhong.
    • 执捉者扶令起 - The executioner helped him stand up.
    • 敕未至，岂可求生 - The imperial decree has not arrived, how can one seek to live?
    • 观者咸服其安闲神异也 - The onlookers were all impressed by his calm and extraordinary demeanor.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 汉耿寿昌 - Geng Shouchang, a minister during the Han Dynasty.
    • 唐崔群 - Cui Qun, a prime minister during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 元和中 - The Yuanhe period of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 中书舍人 - A high-ranking official in charge of drafting imperial edicts.
    • 陆相 - The former prime minister Lu.
    • 天后 - Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
    • 魏元忠 - Wei Yuanzhong, a minister during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 洛阳令 - The governor of Luoyang.
    • 徐敬业 - Xu Jingye, a rebel leader during the Tang Dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 汉耿寿昌 - Geng Shouchang, a minister during the Han Dynasty.
    • 唐崔群 - Cui Qun, a prime minister during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 元和中 - The Yuanhe period of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 中书舍人 - A high-ranking official in charge of drafting imperial edicts.
    • 陆相 - The former prime minister Lu.
    • 天后 - Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
    • 魏元忠 - Wei Yuanzhong, a minister during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 洛阳令 - The governor of Luoyang.
    • 徐敬业 - Xu Jingye, a rebel leader during the Tang Dynasty.

    During the Han Dynasty, Geng Shouchang established the granary system, buying grain at low prices when it was cheap and reducing prices when it was expensive, showing concern for the people and thus setting an example for the state.

    During the Tang Dynasty, Cui Qun, a prime minister, was known for his integrity and prestige. In the Yuanhe period, he served as the imperial examiner in charge of the spring examinations. After his term ended, his wife, Lady Li, often advised him to establish estates as a provision for their descendants. Cui Qun smiled and replied, "I have thirty beautiful estates and good farmland spread throughout the land. What more could you possibly worry about?" Lady Li said, "I have not heard of such estates belonging to you." Cui Qun responded, "Last year, I selected thirty candidates for the spring examination. Are they not like good farmland?" Lady Li said, "If that is the case, you are not a student of the former prime minister Lu? But in the past, when you held the power of literary selection, you had someone tell his son to avoid the spring examination. If you consider these candidates as your estates, then the Lu family's estate is now barren." Cui Qun felt ashamed and withdrew, not eating for several days.

    Wei Yuanzhong of the Tang Dynasty had a noble and upright spirit. Initially serving as the governor of Luoyang, he was sentenced to execution in the marketplace. After he was seated for execution, Empress Wu Zetian, recognizing his merit in suppressing Xu Jingye's rebellion, specially ordered his release. The person carrying out the imperial decree hurriedly announced the release of Wei Yuanzhong. The announcement arrived first, and the executioner helped him stand up. Wei Yuanzhong said, "The imperial decree has not arrived; how can I seek to live?" After a while, the decree finally arrived. The onlookers were all impressed by his calm and extraordinary demeanor. [62]

    隋炀帝无道，杜伏威以齐州叛。炀帝遣陈棱击之。棱下偏裨射中伏威额。伏威怒曰：“不杀射我者，终不拔此箭。”由是奋击而入，获所射者，乃令拔箭毕，然后斩其首，携入棱军中，棱遂大败。
    　　汉惠帝时，叔孙通讽上曰：“古者春有献，今樱桃熟，愿陛下取之。”献果皆自此始。
    　　北齐高洋凶暴，贵嫔薛氏有小过，遽杀支解之。抱其股为琵琶弹之，复叹曰：“佳人难再得。”
    　　唐沧景节度李同捷叛，王智兴帅徐泗兵讨于棣州。时同捷遣一能言者披短褐坐于城上战棚骂智兴，军吏耻之，智兴蒙衣掩耳不忍闻。有一卒曰：“此可用抛石击去其首。”智兴喜曰：“若中，赏汝千万金。”乃具抛发一石，正中其首，随石迸落。军中欢叫，城上飞动。 [63]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [63]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 終不拔此箭 - A personal vow expressing determination to remove an arrow only after achieving a goal.
    • 佳人難再得 - A common phrase expressing the rarity and value of beautiful women.
    • 獻果皆自此始 - The practice of offering fruits began from this, marking the origin of a tradition.
    • 奮擊而入 - Charge in with great effort, describing a determined military action.
    • 蒙衣掩耳不忍聞 - Covered his ears and could not bear to hear it, indicating a reaction to the curse.
    • 此可用拋石擊去其首 - This can be used to throw a stone to hit his head, suggesting a practical solution.
    • 軍中歡叫，城上飛動 - The army cheered, and the city walls shook, indicating the impact of the event.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 隋煬帝 - Emperor Yang of Sui, a notorious ruler known for his tyrannical rule and the downfall of the Sui dynasty.
    • 杜伏威 - A prominent rebel leader during the Sui dynasty.
    • 陳棱 - A general under Emperor Yang of Sui.
    • 漢惠帝 - Emperor Hui of Han, a relatively peaceful ruler of the Han dynasty.
    • 叔孫通 - A famous courtier and advisor during the early Han dynasty.
    • 北齊高洋 - Emperor Gao Yang of Northern Qi, known for his cruel and violent rule.
    • 貴嬪薛氏 - An imperial consort of Emperor Gao Yang.
    • 唐滄景節度李同捷 - Li Tongjie, a governor who rebelled during the Tang dynasty.
    • 王智興 - A general during the Tang dynasty.
    • 棣州 - A prefecture in the Tang dynasty.
    • 徐泗兵 - Troops from the regions of Xu and Si.
    • 拋石 - A type of siege weapon used in ancient Chinese warfare.
    • 琵琶 - A traditional Chinese musical instrument.

    Proper Nouns

    • 隋煬帝 - Emperor Yang of Sui.
    • 杜伏威 - A prominent rebel leader.
    • 陳棱 - A general under Emperor Yang of Sui.
    • 漢惠帝 - Emperor Hui of Han.
    • 叔孫通 - A famous courtier and advisor.
    • 北齊高洋 - Emperor Gao Yang of Northern Qi.
    • 貴嬪薛氏 - An imperial consort.
    • 唐滄景節度李同捷 - Li Tongjie, a governor.
    • 王智興 - A general during the Tang dynasty.
    • 棣州 - A prefecture in the Tang dynasty.
    • 徐泗兵 - Troops from the regions of Xu and Si.
    • 琵琶 - A traditional Chinese musical instrument.

    Emperor Yang of Sui was tyrannical, and Du Fuwei rebelled in Qi Prefecture. Emperor Yang sent Chen Leng to attack him. Chen Leng's subordinate shot Du Fuwei in the forehead. Du Fuwei, enraged, said, "I will not remove this arrow until I kill the one who shot me." He then charged in with great effort, captured the shooter, ordered the arrow to be removed, and then beheaded the man, carrying his head into Chen Leng's camp, which led to Chen Leng's defeat.

    During the reign of Emperor Hui of Han, Shu Sun Tong indirectly advised the emperor, saying, "In ancient times, there were offerings in spring. Now that cherries are ripe, may Your Majesty pick them." The practice of offering fruits began from this event.

    Emperor Gao Yang of Northern Qi was cruel and violent. Imperial consort Xue committed a minor fault, and he immediately killed and dismembered her. He held her leg and played the pipa on it, then lamented, "A beautiful woman is hard to find again."

    Li Tongjie, the governor of Cangjing, rebelled during the Tang dynasty. Wang Zhixing led troops from Xu and Si to attack in Di Prefecture. At the time, Li Tongjie sent a skilled speaker dressed in a short robe to sit on the city wall and curse Wang Zhixing. The military officials were ashamed, and Wang Zhixing covered his ears and could not bear to hear it. One soldier said, "This can be used to throw a stone to hit his head." Wang Zhixing was pleased and said, "If it hits, I will reward you with ten thousand gold coins." He prepared to throw a stone, which hit his head and fell off with the stone. The army cheered, and the city walls shook. [63]

    汉承宫威名闻于匈奴，匈奴欲识，使人求见宫。宫启帝曰：“域外重人形状魁梧，臣貌丑陋，不如选瑰伟者示之。”帝以大鸿胪卿魏应代之。
    　　东汉宦者张让、赵忠持国权，引用屠沽人登清贵。灵帝语左右曰：“张常侍是我父，赵常侍是我母。”故卒以灭汉者，赵、张是也。
    　　张安世每举进贤达，不令其知。或有诣门谢者，安世亦不见。终身恨曰：“安有拜官公庭，谢恩私门乎！”
    　　虞氏，梁之富人也。起高楼临大道，日夕歌宴击博于上。博者胜，掩口而笑。适有三客过楼下，飞鸢衔腐鼠堕客⊥举面，值其笑。二客相与谋曰：“虞氏富乐久矣，我不侵犯，何为辱我”乃聚众灭其家。谚曰：“骄奢之灾，祸非一致。”
    　　汉邴丹曼容养志乐，外权势，仕至六百石，即免归，畏权而祸至也。
    　　张衡死，蔡邕生，时人以邕为张衡后身。 [64]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [64]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 威名 - Means 'fame and authority' or 'reputation and influence.'
    • 魁梧 - Refers to being tall and robust, often used to describe physical appearance.
    • 瑰伟 - Means 'magnificent' or 'splendid,' often used to describe something extraordinary.
    • 大鸿胪卿 - An official title in the Han dynasty, responsible for foreign affairs and protocol.
    • 持国权 - Means 'holding national power' or 'controlling the state.'
    • 屠沽人 - Refers to butchers and wine sellers, often used to describe lowly or vulgar people.
    • 登清贵 - Means 'ascending to high office' or 'reaching a position of honor.'
    • 拜官公庭 - Means 'receiving an official appointment in public' or 'being appointed in a formal setting.'
    • 谢恩私门 - Means 'expressing gratitude privately' or 'thanking in private.'
    • 骄奢之灾 - Refers to the disaster caused by pride and extravagance.
    • 祸非一致 - Means 'disaster is not singular' or 'disaster can come from multiple sources.'
    • 畏权而祸至 - Means 'fearing power leads to disaster' or 'disaster comes from fearing power.'
    • 张衡死，蔡邕生 - A metaphorical expression suggesting that Puyang succeeded Zhang Heng in some way, possibly in terms of talent or influence.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 匈奴 - Refers to the Xiongnu, a nomadic confederation that was a major power in northern China during the Han dynasty.
    • 张让、赵忠 - Two eunuchs who held significant power during the reign of Emperor Ling of Han.
    • 灵帝 - Refers to Emperor Ling of Han, known for his indulgence in luxury and the influence of eunuchs.
    • 邴丹曼容 - A figure known for his disdain for power and his preference for a simple life.
    • 张安世 - A historical figure known for his humility and refusal to acknowledge his own achievements.
    • 虞氏 - Refers to a wealthy family in the state of Liang.
    • 鸢 - A type of bird, often associated with carrion in Chinese literature.
    • 张衡 - A famous astronomer, mathematician, and inventor during the Eastern Han dynasty.
    • 蔡邕 - A renowned scholar, musician, and calligrapher during the Eastern Han dynasty, often compared to Zhang Heng.

    Proper Nouns

    • 匈奴 - The Xiongnu.
    • 张让、赵忠 - Eunuchs during the reign of Emperor Ling of Han.
    • 灵帝 - Emperor Ling of Han.
    • 邴丹曼容 - A historical figure known for his disdain for power.
    • 张安世 - A historical figure known for his humility.
    • 虞氏 - A wealthy family in the state of Liang.
    • 张衡 - A famous astronomer, mathematician, and inventor during the Eastern Han dynasty.
    • 蔡邕 - A renowned scholar, musician, and calligrapher during the Eastern Han dynasty.

    The Han official Palace had a reputation among the Xiongnu, who wished to meet him. They sent envoys to request an audience with Palace. Palace reported to the emperor, saying, "People in the outer regions value grand and imposing figures. I am unattractive, so it would be better to select someone magnificent to represent me." The emperor then sent the Grand Commandant Wei Ying to meet the Xiongnu.

    During the Eastern Han dynasty, eunuchs Zhang Rang and Zhao Zhong held the reins of power, elevating butchers and wine sellers to positions of honor. Emperor Ling told those around him, "Zhang the Attendant is my father, and Zhao the Attendant is my mother." Thus, it was these two eunuchs, Zhao and Zhang, who ultimately brought about the downfall of the Han dynasty.

    Zhang Anshi would always recommend virtuous and capable individuals without letting them know. Sometimes, people would come to his door to express their gratitude, but he would not receive them. He lamented throughout his life, saying, "How can one receive an official appointment in public and express thanks privately?"

    The Yu family was a wealthy clan in the state of Liang. They built a tall tower overlooking a major road, where they held nightly singing, banquets, and games. When a player won, they would cover their mouth and laugh. One day, three guests passed by the tower, and a kite carrying a dead rat fell on one of the guests' faces, landing on the person who was laughing. The two guests who saw this plotted together, saying, "The Yu family has been rich and happy for a long time. We have not trespassed on their wealth, so why should we be humiliated?" They then gathered a crowd to destroy the Yu family. The saying goes, "The disaster of pride and extravagance is not singular in its cause."

    Bing Dan Manrong of the Han dynasty cultivated his aspirations and distanced himself from power, holding a position of 600 stones and then resigning, fearing power and thus bringing disaster upon himself.

    Zhang Heng died, and Puyang was born; at the time, people considered Puyang to be the successor of Zhang Heng. [64]

    唐初有阚棱者，善用长刀，刀长丈余，每下刀，毙数人。莫有嗣者。
    　　薄昭，汉文帝舅。以其杀汉使，文帝不忍行法，乃令朝臣衣丧服哭之。昭遂自尽。或云：昭侍饮，酌酒不满，一郎纠之。昭既归，使人持刀杀纠者。帝闻之，怒，故有此。
    　　北方有匈奴，形质皆人，而足如马蹄，谓之“马蹄突厥”。
    　　窦滔久戍，其妻苏氏能词，织锦为回文诗，叙离间阻隔之意以寄之，其理纵横读之皆有旨义。
    　　贾达年五岁，姊抱听邻家读书，及长，俱能通经籍。姊问曰：“吾未尝教汝，何得致然”答曰：“姊抱听读书，皆省之。”及成人，更博群书。天下闻名，载粟帛受业，而家大富。时人以为贾达“舌耕”。
    　　钟繇年七十而纳正室。
    　　卫青，本平阳公主家参乘。驸马曹寿卒，敕令择国中贵居第一者尚之。青最贵，主问之曰：“是常为我参乘，如之何”使者答曰：“当世无如将军者。”遂尚平阳公主。 [65]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [65]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 唐初有阚棱者 - Classical introductory phrase meaning 'There was a man named Kan Leng in the early Tang dynasty.'
    • 善用长刀 - Means 'skilled in using a long sword,' where '长刀' refers to a long sword or saber.
    • 刀长丈余 - Means 'the sword was over ten feet long,' emphasizing the unusual length of the weapon.
    • 每下刀，毙数人 - Means 'each time he drew his sword, he killed several people,' highlighting the sword's effectiveness.
    • 莫有嗣者 - Means 'no one succeeded him,' indicating that there was no one who could match his skill.
    • 薄昭，汉文帝舅 - Means 'Bo Zhao, the maternal uncle of Emperor Wen of Han,' introducing a historical figure.
    • 以其杀汉使 - Means 'because he killed Han envoys,' explaining the reason for punishment.
    • 文帝不忍行法 - Means 'Emperor Wen could not bring himself to enforce the law,' showing the emperor's compassion.
    • 乃令朝臣衣丧服哭之 - Means 'he ordered the court officials to wear mourning clothes and mourn for him,' describing a symbolic act of justice.
    • 昭遂自尽 - Means 'Bo Zhao then committed suicide,' indicating the outcome of the situation.
    • 或云 - Means 'some say,' introducing an alternative account.
    • 昭侍饮，酌酒不满，一郎纠之 - Means 'Bo Zhao was attending a drinking party, where he poured wine that was not full, and a junior official criticized him,' providing details of the incident.
    • 帝闻之，怒，故有此 - Means 'the emperor heard about it, became angry, and thus had this [action taken],' explaining the emperor's reaction.
    • 北方有匈奴 - Means 'in the north, there were the Xiongnu,' introducing a historical group.
    • 形质皆人，而足如马蹄 - Means 'their appearance was like humans, but their feet were like horse hooves,' describing the unique physical characteristics of the Xiongnu.
    • 谓之“马蹄突厥” - Means 'they were called 'horse-footed Tujue,' providing the specific name for the group.
    • 窦滔久戍 - Means 'Diu Tao was on long-term military service,' introducing another historical figure.
    • 其妻苏氏能词 - Means 'his wife Su was skilled in poetry,' highlighting her talent.
    • 织锦为回文诗 - Means 'she wove a poem in a circular pattern,' describing the form of her work.
    • 叙离间阻隔之意以寄之 - Means 'to express the feelings of separation and distance,' explaining the theme of her poem.
    • 其理纵横读之皆有旨义 - Means 'when read in any direction, it all makes sense,' emphasizing the cleverness of the poem.
    • 贾达年五岁 - Means 'Jia Da was five years old,' introducing a young historical figure.
    • 姊抱听邻家读书 - Means 'his sister held him while he listened to the neighbors reading,' describing his early education.
    • 及长，俱能通经籍 - Means 'when he grew up, both of them were able to understand the classics,' showing their shared learning.
    • 姊问曰：“吾未尝教汝，何得致然” - Means 'his sister asked, 'I never taught you, how did you learn this?'' introducing a dialogue.
    • 答曰：“姊抱听读书，皆省之。” - Means 'he replied, 'My sister held me while I listened to the neighbors reading, and I understood everything.' explaining his learning process.
    • 及成人，更博群书 - Means 'when he grew up, he read more widely,' showing his continued education.
    • 天下闻名，载粟帛受业，而家大富 - Means 'he became famous throughout the land, people brought grain and silk to study under him, and his family became very wealthy,' describing his success.
    • 时人以为贾达“舌耕” - Means 'people of the time believed that Jia Da was 'tilling with his tongue,' using a metaphor to describe his success.
    • 钟繇年七十而纳正室 - Means 'Zhong Yao married his principal wife at the age of seventy,' introducing a historical figure.
    • 卫青，本平阳公主家参乘 - Means 'Wei Qing, originally a charioteer for Princess Pingyang,' introducing another historical figure.
    • 驸马曹寿卒，敕令择国中贵居第一者尚之 - Means 'after the death of Prince Cao Shou, the emperor ordered that the most prestigious person in the country should be chosen to marry her,' explaining the context.
    • 青最贵，主问之曰：“是常为我参乘，如之何” - Means 'Wei Qing was the most prestigious, and the princess asked, 'He was my charioteer, what should I do?'' introducing a dialogue.
    • 使者答曰：“当世无如将军者。” - Means 'the messenger replied, 'There is no one in the world like General Wei Qing.' explaining the messenger's response.
    • 遂尚平阳公主 - Means 'so he married Princess Pingyang,' concluding the story.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 唐初 - Refers to the early Tang dynasty, a period of significant cultural and political development in Chinese history.
    • 阚棱 - A historical figure known for his exceptional swordsmanship.
    • 汉文帝 - Emperor Wen of Han, a ruler known for his benevolence and reforms.
    • 薄昭 - A historical figure, the maternal uncle of Emperor Wen of Han, known for his involvement in a scandal.
    • 匈奴 - The Xiongnu, a nomadic people who were often in conflict with the Han dynasty.
    • 窦滔 - A historical figure, known for his military service.
    • 苏氏 - The wife of Diu Tao, known for her poetic skills.
    • 贾达 - A historical figure, known for his early education and later success.
    • 钟繇 - A historical figure, known for his longevity and marriage at an advanced age.
    • 卫青 - A historical figure, known for his military achievements and marriage to Princess Pingyang.

    Proper Nouns

    • 唐初 - Early Tang dynasty.
    • 阚棱 - Kan Leng.
    • 汉文帝 - Emperor Wen of Han.
    • 薄昭 - Bo Zhao.
    • 匈奴 - Xiongnu.
    • 窦滔 - Diu Tao.
    • 苏氏 - Su.
    • 贾达 - Jia Da.
    • 钟繇 - Zhong Yao.
    • 卫青 - Wei Qing.
    • 平阳公主 - Princess Pingyang.

    In the early Tang dynasty, there was a man named Kan Leng who was skilled in using a long sword. The sword was over ten feet long, and each time he drew it, he killed several people. No one succeeded him in his skill.

    Bo Zhao was the maternal uncle of Emperor Wen of Han. Because he killed Han envoys, Emperor Wen could not bring himself to enforce the law and ordered the court officials to wear mourning clothes and mourn for him. Bo Zhao then committed suicide. Some say that at a drinking party, Bo Zhao poured wine that was not full, and a junior official criticized him. After the incident, Bo Zhao had the official killed. When the emperor heard about it, he became angry, and thus had this action taken.

    In the north, there were the Xiongnu, whose appearance was like humans, but whose feet were like horse hooves. They were called "horse-footed Tujue."

    Diu Tao was on long-term military service, and his wife Su was skilled in poetry. She wove a poem in a circular pattern to express the feelings of separation and distance. When read in any direction, it all made sense.

    Jia Da was five years old, and his sister held him while he listened to the neighbors reading. When he grew up, both of them were able to understand the classics. His sister asked, "I never taught you, how did you learn this?" He replied, "My sister held me while I listened to the neighbors reading, and I understood everything." When he grew up, he read more widely. He became famous throughout the land, people brought grain and silk to study under him, and his family became very wealthy. People of the time believed that Jia Da was "tilling with his tongue."

    Zhong Yao married his principal wife at the age of seventy.

    Wei Qing, originally a charioteer for Princess Pingyang, was chosen to marry her after the death of Prince Cao Shou, as he was the most prestigious person in the country. When the princess asked, "He was my charioteer, what should I do?" the messenger replied, "There is no one in the world like General Wei Qing." So, he married Princess Pingyang. [65]

    汉武帝乳母恃恩，家人纵横。帝怒，乳母流于边，入辞帝。郭舍人谓曰：“母今出时，但屡顾我，当救母不行。”母如其言，乃顾舍人。舍人骂曰：“妪回顾，何为帝壮矣，岂假汝乳耶！”帝于是悦，遂不流乳母。
    　　秦优旃侍始皇，立殿上。秦法重，非有诏不得辄动。时天雨甚，武士被盾立于廷，优旃欲救之，戏曰：“被盾郎，汝虽长，立雨中；我虽短，立殿上。”始皇闻之，乃命徙立庑下。
    　　汉高祖微时，常与客过其丘嫂食⊥益羹，嫂厌叔，佯为羹尽，忧其釜。高祖怨其嫂。及为帝，封其子为忧羹侯。或问帝，帝曰：“其母不长者。”
    　　齐景公时，有一人犯罪，景公怒，令支解之，语曰：“有敢谏者诛！”晏子左手持其头，右手执刀，仰问景公曰：“自古圣主明王，支解人从何而始”景公遽舍之，曰：“罪在寡人。”
    　　梁元帝眇一目，宠徐姬。姬性妒，后怨帝，每召至，即妆半面见之，意者以帝一目，非为全面也。帝亲杀之。 [66]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [66]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 恃恩 - Means 'to rely on favor or privilege,' indicating the arrogance of the wet nurse.
    • 家人纵横 - Refers to the household's unruly behavior, suggesting disorder and lack of discipline.
    • 流于边 - Means 'to exile to the border,' a severe punishment in ancient China.
    • 顾我 - Means 'to look back at me,' a gesture that the wet nurse is instructed to make.
    • 何为帝壮矣，岂假汝乳耶 - A sarcastic remark implying that the emperor's strength does not come from the wet nurse's milk.
    • 微时 - Means 'in humble times,' referring to the early days of someone who later becomes famous.
    • 佯为羹尽 - Means 'pretended the soup was finished,' a deceptive act by the wet nurse.
    • 忧其釜 - Means 'worry about the pot,' suggesting that the wet nurse was concerned about the state of her cooking utensils.
    • 支解之 - Means 'to dismember,' a severe punishment.
    • 顾景公 - Means 'to look at King Jing,' a gesture that the court jester makes.
    • 罪在寡人 - Means 'the fault lies with me,' an admission of personal responsibility by King Jing.
    • 眇一目 - Means 'blind in one eye,' a physical characteristic of the emperor.
    • 宠徐姬 - Means 'to favor Lady Xu,' indicating a special relationship.
    • 饭牛牧羊 - Means 'to feed cattle and herd sheep,' a humble occupation.

    The wet nurse of Emperor Wu of Han relied on her favor, and her family acted recklessly. The emperor was angry and exiled the wet nurse to the border, where she came to bid farewell to him. Guo She-ren said to her, "When you leave now, just look back at me several times, and I will save you from being exiled." The wet nurse followed his advice and looked back at him. Guo She-ren then scolded, "Old woman, why did you look back? The emperor is now strong, did he really need your milk?" The emperor was pleased and decided not to exile the wet nurse.

    In the Qin dynasty, You Zhan served Emperor Qin Shi Huang, standing on the hall. The laws of Qin were strict, and without an imperial order, one could not move. During heavy rain, the guards stood under their shields in the courtyard. You Zhan wanted to help them, so he joked, "Men with shields, you are tall but standing in the rain; I am short but standing on the hall." Emperor Qin Shi Huang heard this and ordered them to move to the eaves.

    When Han Gaozu was in humble circumstances, he often visited his sister-in-law with guests for a meal. His sister-in-law was annoyed by him and pretended the soup was finished, worrying about her pot. Han Gaozu resented her. After becoming emperor, he bestowed a title on his son as the Lord of Worrying Soup. When someone asked the emperor about this, he replied, "Her mother was not a virtuous woman."

    During the reign of Duke Jing of Qi, a man was convicted of a crime, and the duke was angry, ordering him to be dismembered, saying, "Anyone who dares to remonstrate will be executed!" Yanzi held the man's head in his left hand and the knife in his right, asking the duke, "Since ancient times, when did sage rulers begin to dismember people?" Duke Jing immediately stopped the execution and said, "The fault lies with me."

    Emperor Yuan of Liang was blind in one eye and favored Lady Xu. Lady Xu was jealous, and later resented the emperor. Whenever he summoned her, she would only half-dress herself to see him, implying that since the emperor had only one eye, he was not fully whole. The emperor personally killed her. [66]

    尸乡有祝鸡翁，善养群鸡，皆有名呼之。贩卖鸡卵，获亿万。一旦，逃入山，不知所之。
    　　李怀光既叛于蒲，朝廷以法诛之。有子七人，其长曰。谓诸弟曰：“我兄弟不可死于兵卒之手，曾不自裁！”于是执剑俱斩弟首，堆积叠之，立剑于中，以心淬剑，乃洞于胸。闻者伤之。
    　　郑之管城，有居人郑虔章者，落魄杯酒间，年五十余，无闻焉。日醉归，寝宾署中。夕，引手取酒器，遂为鬼拽臂入坑，逡巡至膊，其人慌叫。亲戚举烛俱至，相与牵争而不能制。渐入，至胸臆，头遂入地，俄然全身陷没，若堕水者。乃合众将锹掘之，深丈余，得一枯骨，可长八九寸，又复旁搜，无所见。因出而葬之。
    　　王锷为辛京杲下偏裨，杲时帅长沙，甚易之。一旦击球，驰逐既酣，锷仰天呵气，气高数丈，若白练上衔。杲谓妻曰：“此极贵相。”遂以女弟配之。锷终为将相。 [67]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [67]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 為之一空 - Set phrase meaning completely emptied; fields were left barren.
    • 亿万 - Idiomatic expression meaning a very large sum of money.
    • 不知所之 - Meaning 'no one knew where he went,' indicating a sudden and mysterious disappearance.
    • 曾不自裁 - Meaning 'did not choose to take their own lives,' indicating a decision to avoid suicide.
    • 遂为鬼拽臂入坑 - Meaning 'was suddenly pulled into a pit by a ghost,' indicating a supernatural event.
    • 逡巡至膊 - Meaning 'struggled,' indicating a struggle.
    • 相与牵争而不能制 - Meaning 'they could not control the situation,' indicating a struggle.
    • 渐入，至胸臆 - Meaning 'gradually sank, until his chest and head,' indicating a gradual sinking.
    • 俄然全身陷没 - Meaning 'suddenly his entire body was submerged,' indicating a sudden event.
    • 甚易之 - Meaning 'greatly underestimated him,' indicating a lack of respect.
    • 此极贵相 - Meaning 'this is a sign of great fortune,' indicating a prediction of success.
    • 终为将相 - Meaning 'eventually became a general and a high-ranking official,' indicating a successful career.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 尸乡 - A place name, likely referring to a village or town in ancient China.
    • 祝鸡翁 - A legendary figure known for his ability to communicate with chickens.
    • 李怀光 - A historical figure who rebelled against the Tang Dynasty.
    • 蒲 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China.
    • 灵帝 - Emperor Ling of Han, a ruler of the late Eastern Han period.
    • 洛阳 - A city in ancient China, the capital of the Eastern Han Dynasty.
    • 王锷 - A historical figure who became a high-ranking official.
    • 辛京杲 - A historical figure who was the governor of Changsha.
    • 长沙 - A city in ancient China, known for its strategic importance.

    Proper Nouns

    • 紹興 - Era name of the Southern Song; rendered as Shaoxing.
    •  - Name of Li Huiguang's eldest son, kept consistent.
    • 鄭虔章 - Name of a man in Guancheng, Zheng, kept consistent.
    • 辛京杲 - Name of a historical figure who was the governor of Changsha, kept consistent.

    In the village of Shi, there was an old man named Zhu Ji, skilled at raising a flock of chickens, all of which had names. He sold chicken eggs and earned millions. One day, he fled into the mountains, and no one knew where he went.

    Li Huiguang, having rebelled in Pu, was punished by the court according to the law. He had seven sons, and his eldest was named . He told his younger brothers, "We brothers cannot die at the hands of soldiers, so we did not choose to take our own lives!" So they took up swords and cut off their brothers' heads, piling them up and placing the sword in the middle. They used their hearts to temper the sword, and it pierced through their chests. Those who heard about it were saddened.

    In the city of Guancheng in Zheng, there lived a man named Zheng Qianzhang. He was a disreputable person who often drank in taverns, over fifty years old, and unknown to anyone. He would return drunk and sleep in the guest quarters. At night, he reached out to take a wine vessel, and was suddenly pulled into a pit by a ghost. He struggled and called out in panic. His relatives lit candles and came to help, but they could not control the situation. He gradually sank, until his chest and head were in the ground, and suddenly his entire body was submerged. They gathered the crowd and dug with shovels, reaching a depth of over a meter, and found a dried bone about eight or nine inches long. They searched again, but found nothing else. They buried it.

    Wang E was a subordinate officer under Xin Jingguo. Xin was the governor of Changsha and greatly underestimated him. One day, they were playing ball, and the competition was intense. Wang lifted his head and exhaled, and his breath rose several meters high, like a white silk banner. Xin told his wife, "This is a sign of great fortune." So he married his younger sister to him. Wang eventually became a general and a high-ranking official. [67]

    西极有献续弦胶者，帝不信，即断而接之，使人挽拽，及他处断，而接者如故。
    　　魏国有女子，极美丽，逾时不嫁，以右膝上常患一疮，肿，脓水不绝。遇华佗过，其父问之，佗曰：“使人乘马，牵一栗色犬，走三十里。归而截犬右足挂之。”俄顷，一赤蛇从疮而出入犬足中，其疾遂愈。
    　　玄宗朝，有张果老先生者，不知岁数，出于邢州。帝迎于内，礼敬甚。问，无不知者。一旦，有道士叶静能，亦多知解，玄宗问：“果老何人？”静能答曰：“臣即知之。然臣言讫即死，臣不敢言。若陛下免冠跣足敕臣，臣即能活。”帝许之。静能曰：“此混沌初分白蝙蝠精。”言讫，七窍血流，偃仆于地。玄宗遽往，果老徐曰：“此小儿多口过，不谪之，败天地间事耳。”帝哀恳久之，果老以水巽其面，复生。其后果老辞归邢州所隐之处，俄然不知所往。 [68]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [68]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 西極有獻續弦膠者 - Someone from the far west presented a string-repairing glue; a metaphor for something that can mend or restore.
    • 帝不信，即斷而接之 - The emperor did not believe it, so he cut it and joined it; a literal test of the glue's efficacy.
    • 使人挽拽 - He had someone pull it; a forceful action to test the glue's strength.
    • 及他處斷，而接者如故 - When it broke elsewhere, the glued part remained as before; indicates the glue's effectiveness.
    • 逾时不嫁 - She remained unmarried beyond the usual time; '逾时' refers to exceeding the socially acceptable period for marriage.
    • 右膝上常患一疮，肿，脓水不绝 - She had a chronic wound on her right knee, swollen and oozing pus; '患' means 'suffer from,' and '脓水不绝' describes the wound's persistent discharge.
    • 华佗过 - Hua Tuo passed by; '过' means 'to pass by' or 'to visit.'
    • 牵一栗色犬，走三十里 - He had a red-brown dog tied to it and walked thirty miles; '栗色' means 'chestnut-colored,' and '走' means 'to walk' or 'to travel.'
    • 归而截犬右足挂之 - Upon returning, he cut off the dog's right foot and hung it; '截' means 'to cut off,' and '挂' means 'to hang.'
    • 赤蛇从疮而出入犬足中 - A red snake emerged from the wound and entered the dog's foot; describes a supernatural event.
    • 玄宗朝 - During the reign of Emperor Xuanzong; '朝' refers to the imperial court or reign period.
    • 有道士叶静能 - There was a Daoist named Ye Jingneng; '道士' refers to a Daoist practitioner.
    • 果老何人 - Who is Guo Lao?; '何人' means 'who.'
    • 此混沌初分白蝙蝠精 - This is the white bat spirit from the beginning of chaos; '混沌' refers to the primordial chaos, and '白蝙蝠精' is a mythical creature.
    • 七窍血流，偃仆于地 - Blood flowed from his seven orifices, and he fell to the ground; '七窍' refers to the seven orifices of the face, and '偃仆' means 'to fall down.'
    • 此小儿多口过，不谪之，败天地间事耳 - This child has too much talk, if not punished, it will ruin the affairs of the world; '多口过' means 'to talk too much,' and '谪' means 'to punish.'
    • 果老徐曰 - Guo Lao calmly said; '徐' means 'calmly' or 'slowly.'
    • 此小儿多口过，不谪之，败天地间事耳 - Repeated phrase from above.
    • 其后果老辞归邢州所隐之处，俄然不知所往 - Later, Guo Lao returned to his place of seclusion in Xingzhou, and suddenly disappeared; '俄然' means 'suddenly' or 'in a moment.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 西极 - Refers to the far west, often used in Chinese literature to denote distant or mythical places.
    • 续弦胶 - A mythical or extraordinary substance that can mend or restore, possibly alluding to a magical or legendary glue.
    • 华佗 - A famous physician in Chinese history, known for his medical skills and legendary cures.
    • 玄宗朝 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a significant period in Chinese history.
    • 叶静能 - A historical figure, a Daoist practitioner known for his knowledge and abilities.
    • 混沌 - Refers to the primordial chaos in Chinese cosmology, often associated with the creation of the world.
    • 白蝙蝠精 - A mythical creature, possibly a spirit or demon, often found in Chinese folklore and literature.
    • 邢州 - A historical region in northern China, known for its cultural and historical significance.

    Proper Nouns

    • 续弦胶 - A mythical or extraordinary substance.
    • 华佗 - A famous physician in Chinese history.
    • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
    • 叶静能 - A historical figure, a Daoist practitioner.
    • 混沌 - A concept in Chinese cosmology.
    • 白蝙蝠精 - A mythical creature.
    • 邢州 - A historical region in northern China.

    A man from the far west presented a string-repairing glue to the emperor. The emperor did not believe it, so he cut it and joined it, then had someone pull it. When it broke elsewhere, the glued part remained as before.

    In the state of Wei, there was a woman who was extremely beautiful but remained unmarried beyond the usual time because she had a chronic wound on her right knee, which was swollen and oozing pus. When Hua Tuo passed by, her father asked him about it. Hua Tuo said, "Have someone ride a horse, tie a chestnut-colored dog to it, and walk thirty miles. Upon returning, cut off the dog's right foot and hang it." Soon after, a red snake emerged from the wound and entered the dog's foot, and her illness was cured.

    During the reign of Emperor Xuanzong, there was a man named Mr. Zhang Guolao, whose age was unknown, and he came from Xingzhou. The emperor welcomed him into the palace and showed him great respect. He asked him anything, and Mr. Guolao knew the answers to all questions. One day, a Daoist named Ye Jingneng, who also had much knowledge, asked the emperor, "Who is Mr. Guolao?" Ye Jingneng replied, "I know him. But if I speak, I will die. I dare not speak. If Your Majesty removes your hat and shoes and commands me, I can live." The emperor agreed. Ye Jingneng said, "This is the white bat spirit from the beginning of chaos." After he finished speaking, blood flowed from his seven orifices, and he fell to the ground. The emperor rushed to him, and Mr. Guolao calmly said, "This child talks too much. If not punished, it will ruin the affairs of the world." The emperor pleaded with him for a long time, and Mr. Guolao wiped his face with water, and he revived. Later, Mr. Guolao returned to his place of seclusion in Xingzhou, and suddenly disappeared. [68]

    后汉刘焉，字君卿。灵帝时为太常，见王室多故，意求之交，欲避难。侍中董扶谓焉曰：“常见益州有天子气。”焉乃求益州。遂拜之。既而至蜀，思扶之言，咸造乘舆旌旗，一如王者之制。忽一旦，天火下烧，所造作物荡尽。焉疽背而死。
    　　始皇时，长安县忽有大水涨而欲没县。主簿全干入白，明府谓干曰：“今日卿何作鱼面”干曰：“明府亦作鱼头。”言讫，遂陷为湖。
    　　唐宰相王涯，奢豪其极。庭穿大井，合木为柜，严其锁阴，天下宝玉珍珠琼璧，投置水中，汲水供涯所饮。未几犯法，为天兵枭戮而赤族，涯骨肉色并如金。 [69]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [69]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 見王室多故 - Means 'saw that the imperial family was in turmoil' or 'observed that the royal house was in disarray.'
    • 意求之交，欲避難 - The phrase is somewhat ambiguous. '交' is likely a typo or corruption of '交州' (Jiaozhou), a region in southern China. Thus, it could mean 'intended to seek refuge in Jiaozhou to avoid disaster.'
    • 天火下燒，所造作物蕩盡 - Means 'heavenly fire descended and burned, resulting in the destruction of all the things made.'
    • 始皇時，長安縣忽有大水漲而欲沒縣 - Means 'during the reign of the First Emperor, a sudden flood in Chang'an County threatened to inundate the entire county.'
    • 唐宰相王涯，奢豪其極 - Means 'extremely extravagant and luxurious' or 'unusually opulent.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 後漢劉焉 - Refers to Liu Yan, a prominent figure during the Eastern Han Dynasty.
    • 靈帝 - Refers to Emperor Ling of Han, the ruler during Liu Yan's time.
    • 太常 - Refers to the position of Grand Master of Ceremonies, a high-ranking official in the Han Dynasty.
    • 益州 - Refers to Yizhou, a region in southwestern China.
    • 天子氣 - Refers to the aura or sign of a future ruler.
    • 始皇時 - Refers to the reign of Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China.
    • 長安縣 - Refers to Chang'an County, an area in the capital city of Chang'an during the Qin and Han Dynasties.
    • 唐宰相王涯 - Refers to Wang Ya, a Tang Dynasty chancellor known for his extravagance.
    • 唐 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty, a period of great cultural and political achievement in Chinese history.

    Proper Nouns

    • 後漢劉焉 - Liu Yan, a prominent figure during the Eastern Han Dynasty.
    • 靈帝 - Emperor Ling of Han.
    • 太常 - The position of Grand Master of Ceremonies.
    • 益州 - Yizhou, a region in southwestern China.
    • 天子氣 - The aura or sign of a future ruler.
    • 始皇時 - The reign of Qin Shi Huang.
    • 長安縣 - Chang'an County.
    • 主簿全幹 - Quan Gan, a county clerk.
    • 明府 - A respectful term for a county magistrate.
    • 唐宰相王涯 - Wang Ya, a Tang Dynasty chancellor.
    • 唐 - The Tang Dynasty.

    Liu Yan of the Eastern Han Dynasty, styled Junqing, served as the Grand Master of Ceremonies during the reign of Emperor Ling. Observing the turmoil in the imperial family, he intended to seek refuge in Jiaozhou to avoid disaster. The imperial censor Dong Fu told Liu Yan, "I have often seen that Yizhou has the aura of a sovereign." Liu Yan then sought to take control of Yizhou and was subsequently appointed. Upon arriving in Shu, he thought about what Dong Fu had said and created imperial carriages and flags, following exactly the standards of a king. Suddenly one day, a heavenly fire descended and burned, destroying all the things he had created. Liu Yan died of a boil on his back.

    During the reign of Qin Shi Huang, a sudden flood in Chang'an County threatened to inundate the entire county. The county clerk Quan Gan entered to report to the magistrate, who said, "Today, why are you acting like a fish?" Quan Gan replied, "You are also acting like a fish head." After speaking, the area sank and turned into a lake.

    Wang Ya, a Tang Dynasty chancellor, was extremely extravagant and luxurious. He dug a large well in his courtyard, made a wooden cabinet, and secured it tightly. He threw jewels, pearls, and precious stones from all over the world into the water, drawing water to supply his drinking. Shortly after, he committed a crime and was executed by divine forces, with his family being wiped out. His bones and flesh turned golden in color. [69]

    《神异记》注曰：西南大荒中，有兽，形如兔，人面而能言，心常欺人，言东即西，言南即北，其名曰“诡”。
    　　吴孙权猎于武昌，有神女见，曰：“今日当猎异兽。”忽然不见。俄顷，猎得一豹。女复见，曰：“可竖其尾于我处而立祠焉。”或曰，豹尾之设，自孙权始焉。
    　　《三十国春秋》：伪前梁张重华在梁州，欲诛西河张祚。祚厩马数十匹，同时皆无尾。未几祚遇祸。
    　　唐贾直言，德宗朝父泄漏禁中事，帝怒，赐鸩酒。直言白中使，请自执器以饮其父。中使然之。直言既持杯而自饮之，立死。酒自左足间出，复活。具奏，遂流其父于南海。遇恩归，还东平。以劲直名闻，拜谏议大夫。直言妻董氏，亦奇节。直言随父流所，谓董氏曰：“先死莫期，不得相见。”令其改适。董入室以绳缚发，取笔令直言封之，启曰：“非君不解，毕死不开。”其后二十二年再会，旧题宛然。以油沐之，其发俱堕。 [70]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [70]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 形如兔 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'shaped like a rabbit,' used to describe the appearance of the creature.
    • 言东即西，言南即北 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to say east means west, to say south means north,' indicating the creature's tendency to lie or mislead.
    • 竖其尾于我处而立祠焉 - Means 'set up its tail at my place and establish a shrine there,' indicating a ritual or act of worship.
    • 豹尾之设 - Refers to the setting up of a shrine with a tiger's tail, possibly a symbolic act.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 神异记 - A classical text that records strange or supernatural occurrences, often used in Chinese literature to describe mythical events.
    • 大荒 - A term often used in classical texts to describe remote or mythical places, often associated with the *Huainanzi* or *Shanhaijing*.
    • 武昌 - A city in present-day Hubei Province, China, historically significant during the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 神女 - A common motif in Chinese mythology and literature, often representing divine or supernatural beings.
    • 伪前梁张重华 - Zhang Zhonghua, a ruler of the Western Jin Dynasty, known for his political struggles.
    • 西河张祚 - Zhang Zu, a ruler of the Western Jin Dynasty, known for his conflicts with Zhang Zhonghua.
    • 唐贾直言 - A historical figure, possibly a loyal minister during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 东平 - A city in present-day Shandong Province, China, historically significant during the Han and Tang Dynasties.

    Proper Nouns

    • 神异记 - A classical text.
    • 大荒 - A term for remote or mythical places.
    • 武昌 - A city in present-day Hubei Province, China.
    • 神女 - A divine or supernatural woman.
    • 豹尾之设 - A ritual or act of worship.
    • 伪前梁张重华 - Zhang Zhonghua, a ruler of the Western Jin Dynasty.
    • 西河张祚 - Zhang Zu, a ruler of the Western Jin Dynasty.
    • 唐贾直言 - A historical figure, possibly a loyal minister during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 东平 - A city in present-day Shandong Province, China.
    • 董氏 - The surname of the woman, possibly referring to her husband's wife.

    The Shen Yi Ji notes: In the remote wilderness to the southwest, there is a beast that resembles a rabbit, has a human face, and can speak. Its heart is always deceitful, and it often lies, saying "east" when it means "west" and "south" when it means "north." Its name is "Gui."

    During the reign of Sun Quan of Wu, he was hunting in Wuchang when a divine woman appeared and said, "Today you will hunt a strange beast." She then disappeared. Shortly after, a leopard was caught. The woman reappeared and said, "Set up its tail at my place and establish a shrine there." Some say that the establishment of a shrine with a tiger's tail began with Sun Quan.

    The Thirty Kingdoms Spring and Autumn records: During the reign of Zhang Zhonghua of the Western Jin, he intended to eliminate Zhang Zu of Xihé. Suddenly, dozens of horses in Zhang Zu's stable lost their tails. Shortly after, Zhang Zu met his demise.

    **During the Tang Dynasty, Jia Zhiyan, a loyal minister of Emperor Dezong, was exiled to the South Sea because his father had leaked confidential matters. The emperor was furious and ordered Jia Zhiyan to be executed with poison. Jia Zhiyan approached the imperial messenger and asked to take the cup himself and drink it for his father. The messenger agreed. Jia Zhiyan then held the cup and drank it, dying immediately. The wine came out from his left foot, and he revived. He reported this to the emperor, and his father was subsequently exiled to the South Sea. Later, Jia Zhiyan returned to Dongping after receiving imperial favor. He became renowned for his integrity and was appointed as a Jianyi Daifu (Adviser to the Emperor). Jia Zhiyan's wife, Dong, was also a woman of extraordinary virtue. When Jia Zhiyan was exiled with his father, he told Dong, "Death may come at any time, and we may not meet again." He instructed her to remarry. Dong entered her room, tied her hair with a rope, took a brush, and had Jia Zhiyan seal the note, writing, "This will not be opened unless by you, and it will remain sealed until death." Twenty-two years later, they reunited, and the old note was still intact. When they washed their hair with oil, her hair fell out. [70]

    太宗朝罢归而含怒曰：“终须杀此田舍奴！”文献皇后问曰：“大家嗔怨谁也？”帝曰：“只是魏征老兵，对众辱我。”后入院，衣プ翟，下殿拜。帝惊问曰：“何也？”后曰：“妾闻主圣臣忠。征能直言，非大家圣德，不有忠臣。妾敢为贺。”帝大悦，益重魏征。
    　　曹操密语左右一人曰：“汝明日可挟一刃入吾室中，吾令人执汝，汝勿言，吾有重报于汝。”其人不悟，遂缄默至于死。操用此以惑众，能察人眉睫之用也。
    　　子贡一出，存鲁、乱齐、破吴、强晋而霸越。
    　　唐张文成七登科选，员半千云：“张子之文如青钱，万拣万中。”时人以文成为“青钱学士”。 [71]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [71]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 朝罢归而含怒曰 - Formal way to describe someone's emotional state after a meeting.
    • 终须杀此田舍奴 - Idiom expressing frustration or anger.
    • 大家嗔怨谁也 - Formal way to ask about someone's anger.
    • 只是魏征老兵，对众辱我 - Formal way to describe someone's actions.
    • 衣プ翟，下殿拜 - Formal description of a ritual action.
    • 主圣臣忠 - Classical saying about the relationship between rulers and ministers.
    • 征能直言，非大家圣德，不有忠臣 - Formal way to praise the ruler and the minister.
    • 妾敢为贺 - Formal way to express congratulations.
    • 密语左右一人 - Formal way to describe a private conversation.
    • 汝明日可挟一刃入吾室中 - Formal command.
    • 吾令人执汝，汝勿言，吾有重报于汝 - Formal way to give an order and promise a reward.
    • 用此以惑众 - Formal way to describe a deceptive action.
    • 能察人眉睫之用也 - Formal way to describe keen observation skills.
    • 子贡一出，存鲁、乱齐、破吴、强晋而霸越 - Formal way to describe Zilu's impact.
    • 七登科选 - Formal way to describe passing the exams multiple times.
    • 青钱万拣万中 - Idiom meaning something is always successful.
    • 青钱学士 - Nickname given to someone who is always successful in exams.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 太宗 - Refers to Emperor Taizong of Tang, a prominent ruler known for his military prowess and administrative reforms.
    • 文献皇后 - Refers to Empress Wenxuan, a respected and influential figure in the Tang court.
    • 魏征 - A famous minister known for his frank advice to Emperor Taizong.
    • 曹操 - A powerful warlord and later de facto ruler of the late Eastern Han period, known for his political and military strategies.
    • 子贡 - A prominent disciple of Confucius, known for his diplomatic and economic achievements.
    • 张文成 - Refers to Zhang Ji, a renowned poet and scholar of the Tang dynasty.
    • 员半千 - A contemporary of Zhang Ji, known for his poetry and scholarship.

    Proper Nouns

    • 太宗 - Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 文献皇后 - Empress Wenxuan.
    • 魏征 - Wei Zheng.
    • 曹操 - Cao Cao.
    • 子贡 - Zilu (Zigong).
    • 张文成 - Zhang Ji.
    • 员半千 - Yuan Banqian.

    After the court session, Emperor Taizong returned home in anger and said, "I must eventually kill this country bumpkin!" The Empress Wenxuan asked, "Who has the Great One become angry with?" The emperor replied, "It's just that old soldier Wei Zheng, who humiliated me in front of everyone." The empress then entered the palace, dressed in a ceremonial robe, and bowed down in the hall. The emperor was surprised and asked, "Why did you do that?" The empress said, "I have heard that when the ruler is wise, the ministers are loyal. Wei Zheng can speak frankly; it is because the Great One is wise that there are loyal ministers. I dare to congratulate you." The emperor was greatly pleased and valued Wei Zheng even more.

    Cao Cao secretly spoke to one of his attendants, saying, "You may carry a blade into my room tomorrow. I will have someone seize you; do not speak, and I will reward you greatly." The person did not understand and remained silent until he died. Cao Cao used this to deceive the people, demonstrating his ability to observe the subtlest expressions of others.

    When Zilu went out, he saved Lu, caused chaos in Qi, destroyed Wu, strengthened Jin, and made Yue dominant.

    During the Tang dynasty, Zhang Ji passed the imperial examinations seven times. Yuan Banqian commented, "Zhang's writing is like green coins, a million chosen, a million hit." At that time, people referred to Zhang as the "Green Coin Scholar." [71]

    唐狄仁杰为大理寺丞，申中上考功驳下，问：“有何劳绩”寺复执申曰：“岁凡断狱一万二千。”考功特升上下考。
    　　齐高洋残酷，其弟南阳王淖献计，令取群蝎置斛中，倮断一人为蝎所螫，哀号宛转，不胜其苦。洋笑，飞书谓其弟曰：“有此乐事，何不早言之”
    　　玄宗御勤政楼，于下百戏，坐安禄山于东阅看。肃宗谏曰：“历古今无臣下与君王同坐阅戏者。”上曰：“渠有异相，故禳之。”又尝与之夜宴，禄山醉，化为猪而龙头。左右遽告，帝曰：“渠龙首猪身，无能为也。”终不杀之，卒乱中国。
    　　河上公尝居河上，不知姓字，无营欲，不履城郭。文帝召之，不至，乃就见之。自云穷《老子》一经，甚侮侵于世。与帝语之，帝曰：“吾，君也；公，卿也。岂无敬君之礼乎”公遂跃起空中，曰：“吾上不在天，下不在地。帝虽尊，于我何加！”帝谢之，乃下。共论《老子》经之义，帝不能屈。 [72]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [72]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 歲凡斷獄一萬二千 - Literal statement indicating that the official handled 12,000 criminal cases in a year.
    • 帝雖尊，於我何加 - Defiant statement asserting that the emperor's power does not affect the speaker.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 狄仁杰 - Famous Tang Dynasty official known for his integrity and judicial expertise.
    • 安禄山 - Powerful general who later rebelled against the Tang Dynasty, causing the An Lushan Rebellion.
    • 河上公 - Legendary figure associated with Daoist teachings, often credited with interpreting the *Laozi*.

    Proper Nouns

    • 狄仁杰 - Historical figure and Tang Dynasty official.
    • 安禄山 - Historical figure and general who rebelled against the Tang Dynasty.
    • 河上公 - Legendary figure associated with Daoist teachings.

    During the Tang Dynasty, Di Renjie served as the Director of the Dali Temple, where he submitted his annual performance evaluation to the Ministry of Personnel, only to have it rejected. When asked, "What achievements do you have?" the temple responded, "He handles 12,000 criminal cases each year." The Ministry of Personnel then specially promoted him to a higher rank.

    Emperor Gao Yang of the Northern Qi was known for his cruelty. His younger brother, Prince Nanyang, suggested that they collect a group of scorpions and place them in a large container, then strip a person naked and let them be stung by the scorpions. The person cried out in pain, unable to endure the suffering. Emperor Gao Yang laughed and sent a letter to his brother, saying, "How could you not tell me about this delightful event earlier?"

    Emperor Xuanzong of Tang held a ceremony at the Qinzheng Pavilion, where various performances were held. He seated An Lushan to the east to watch. The Crown Prince advised, "Throughout history, there has never been a minister sitting beside the emperor to watch performances." The emperor replied, "He has an unusual appearance, so I am protecting him with rituals." On another occasion, during a night banquet, An Lushan became drunk and transformed into a creature with a pig's body and a dragon's head. The attendants quickly reported this to the emperor, who said, "He has a dragon's head and a pig's body; he cannot do anything." Despite this, the emperor did not kill him, and An Lushan eventually caused chaos in China.

    The River Master once lived by the river, unknown by his name or surname, with no desires for worldly affairs and never stepping into a city. Emperor Wen of Han summoned him, but he did not come. The emperor then went to see him. The River Master claimed to have thoroughly studied the Laozi and was greatly despised by the world. When the emperor spoke to him, he said, "I am your sovereign; you are my minister. Do you not have respect for your sovereign?" The River Master then leaped into the air and said, "I am not in the heavens above, nor in the earth below. Your majesty may be respected, but what can you do to me?" The emperor apologized, and the River Master descended. They discussed the meaning of the Laozi, but the emperor could not convince him. [72]

    陆云有笑癖，尝谒司空张华，华多须，以袋盛之，云见华，不及拜而笑倒。又尝自服上船，见水中影，笑而堕水，几至于死。
    　　太宗以魏征为人镜，谓左右曰：“以古为镜见成败，以铜为镜知美丑，以人为镜知善恶。吾用此三镜以辨兴衰。今魏征死，吾失一镜。”
    　　李衡，江陵种橘千树，岁收其利。谓其子曰：“吾有木奴千头，可为汝业，当终身衣食也。”
    　　祭彤为辽东太守三十年，帝嘉其功，赐钱百万。
    　　周亚夫平七国归，不得任用，怏怏不乐。帝觉之，因宴与肉，大不设筋。亚夫有怨色。帝付廷尉。饥食藁席九十日，至俄死。先时人相其有纵理入口，当饿死，果然。
    　　吕蒙随姊夫邓当击贼，年十六，呵叱而前。当不能禁止。归言于母曰：“贫贱谁可居设有功，富贵可致。”又曰：“不探虎穴，焉得虎子”遂成大名。
    　　隋文帝在位时，已无道。天下船长三丈，谓其既大，必能藏匿奸党，并令没入官。 [73]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [73]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 笑癖 - Indicates a habitual or compulsive tendency to laugh.
    • 笑倒 - To laugh so hard that one falls over, indicating extreme amusement or laughter.
    • 木奴 - A metaphorical term for trees, particularly fruit trees, suggesting that they can provide a livelihood.
    • 大不设筋 - A euphemism for a meal without meat, indicating a lack of luxury or attention to detail.
    • 探虎穴 - A metaphor for taking great risks, often with the implication of achieving something valuable.
    • 纵理入口 - A phrase indicating a fate where one's death is due to starvation, possibly because of a physical condition that prevents eating properly.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 陆云 - A famous scholar and official during the Western Jin Dynasty, known for his wit and literary talent.
    • 张华 - A prominent statesman and scholar during the Western Jin Dynasty, known for his wisdom and contributions to literature.
    • 太宗 - Refers to Emperor Taizong of Tang, a highly revered emperor known for his administrative reforms and patronage of culture.
    • 魏征 - A renowned chancellor and advisor to Emperor Taizong, known for his frank advice and moral integrity.
    • 李衡 - A historical figure known for his agricultural innovations and wealth.
    • 祭彤 - A military general during the Eastern Han Dynasty, known for his long service in the border regions.
    • 周亚夫 - A general during the Han Dynasty, known for his military prowess and later political downfall.
    • 吕蒙 - A general during the Three Kingdoms period, known for his strategic acumen and rise from a lowly background.
    • 隋文帝 - The founder of the Sui Dynasty, known for his efforts to reunify China and implement reforms.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • This phrase indicates that Lu Yun laughed so hard that he fell over before he could even bow to Zhang Hua, suggesting a spontaneous and uncontrollable reaction.
    • This phrase suggests that the trees will provide a lifelong source of food and clothing, emphasizing the long-term benefits of planting them.
    • This phrase indicates that Zhou Yafu died after 90 days of starvation, with '俄死' suggesting a sudden and unexpected death.
    • This phrase is a proverb meaning that one cannot achieve great things without taking significant risks.

    Proper Nouns

    • 陆云 - Lu Yun, a scholar and official.
    • 张华 - Zhang Hua, a statesman and scholar.
    • 太宗 - Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 魏征 - Wei Zheng, a chancellor and advisor.
    • 李衡 - Li Heng, a historical figure.
    • 祭彤 - Ji Tong, a military general.
    • 周亚夫 - Zhou Yafu, a general.
    • 吕蒙 - Lu Meng, a general.
    • 隋文帝 - Emperor Wen of Sui.

    Lu Yun had a penchant for laughter. Once, he visited the Grand Works Minister Zhang Hua, who had a lot of facial hair, which Zhang Hua kept in a bag. When Lu Yun saw Zhang Hua, he laughed so hard that he fell over before he could even bow. Another time, while he was on a boat, he saw his reflection in the water and laughed so much that he fell into the water, nearly drowning to death.

    Emperor Taizong considered Wei Zheng as his mirror, telling those around him: "By using the past as a mirror, we can see success and failure; by using bronze as a mirror, we can know beauty and ugliness; by using people as a mirror, we can know good and evil. I use these three mirrors to distinguish prosperity and decline. Now that Wei Zheng has died, I have lost one of my mirrors."

    Li Heng planted a thousand orange trees in Jingling, reaping their benefits every year. He told his son: "I have a thousand 'wooden slaves,' which can serve as your livelihood, providing you with food and clothing for life."

    Ji Tong served as the governor of Liaodong for thirty years. The emperor praised his achievements and granted him a million coins.

    After quelling the rebellion of the Seven States, Zhou Yafu returned but was not given a position of trust, leaving him unhappy and discontented. The emperor noticed this and, during a banquet, gave him meat without any bones. Zhou Yafu showed signs of resentment. The emperor then handed him over to the Imperial Censor. Zhou Yafu starved for ninety days on straw mats, eventually dying suddenly. It was predicted beforehand that he would die of starvation due to his physical condition, and this prediction came true.

    Lu Meng, at the age of sixteen, followed his brother-in-law Deng Dang to fight against bandits. He shouted and charged forward, and Deng Dang could not stop him. Upon returning home, he told his mother: "Who can live in poverty? With merit, one can achieve wealth and honor." He also said: "If one does not venture into the tiger's den, how can one catch the tiger's cub?" Thus, he became famous.

    During Emperor Wen of Sui's reign, he had already become tyrannical. Ships measuring three zhang in length were deemed too large and capable of hiding rebels, so they were confiscated by the government. [73]

    昔有人出行，逢一兔走，头击树而死，其人得之。后日日忘家失业，专坐此树下守兔。有过者见之，问：“汝何守株也如是”
    　　后汉窦武生时，其母并生一蛇，乃送山中。及武母死，有大蛇至母棺柩侧，蜿蜒磕头血流而去。
    　　郅都为济南太守，诛豪族三百余家，不顾妻子。
    　　后汉韦逞母宗氏，博究经典，置生徒一百二十人，隔纱窗授业。
    　　荀菘有女名灌。菘为襄阳太守，为杜鲁所围，菘欲求救于平南将军石览。灌年十三，率勇士千余人，逾城突围，且战且前，诣览乞救。览假兵救菘，贼遂走散。
    　　契何力，西番酋种太守，授右骁卫将军，早立功勋。太守征辽，至白雀城，为贼所围，腰中贼槊疮极重。帝因传令，及拔贼城，得行槊者高突鹁雏。太宗捉付何力，令自杀。曰：“犬马犹为其主，况于人乎彼为其主，用刃而刺臣者，是勇也。本不相识，岂是冤仇。”遂舍之。 [74]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [74]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 昔有人出行，逢一兔走，头击树而死，其人得之 - Narrative opening describing a man encountering a rabbit that hits a tree and dies.
    • 后日日忘家失业，专坐此树下守兔 - Describes the man neglecting his family and livelihood, focusing solely on waiting for the rabbit to reappear.
    • 汝何守株也如是 - Rhetorical question asking why the man is behaving in this manner, with '守株' being a metaphor for waiting for something that is unlikely to happen again.
    • 生时，其母并生一蛇，乃送山中 - Describes the birth of a snake along with the person, with '并生' indicating simultaneous birth.
    • 蜿蜒磕头血流而去 - Describes the snake's behavior, with '蜿蜒' meaning to move in a winding manner and '磕头' meaning to bow repeatedly.
    • 诛豪族三百余家，不顾妻子 - Describes the execution of 300+ families, with '不顾妻子' indicating that the action was carried out without regard for personal ties.
    • 置生徒一百二十人，隔纱窗授业 - Describes the setup of a school with 120 students, with '隔纱窗' indicating a physical barrier between the teacher and students.
    • 逾城突围，且战且前 - Describes a daring escape, with '逾城' meaning to scale the city walls and '且战且前' indicating fighting while advancing.
    • 犬马犹为其主，况于人乎 - Rhetorical question emphasizing loyalty, with '犬马犹为其主' meaning even animals are loyal to their masters.
    • 得行槊者高突鹁雏 - Describes the capture of a specific person, with '行槊' meaning to wield a spear and '高突鹁雏' being a name or identifier.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 后汉 - Refers to the Eastern Han Dynasty (25–220 CE), a significant period in Chinese history.
    • 窦武 - A prominent figure during the Eastern Han Dynasty, known for his political influence.
    • 郅都 - A famous general and statesman during the Western Han Dynasty, known for his strict justice.
    • 韦逞母宗氏 - Refers to Wei Cheng's mother, a respected scholar during the Eastern Han Dynasty.
    • 荀菘 - A figure during the Eastern Han Dynasty, known for his daughter's bravery.
    • 契丹何力 - A notable figure from the Xianbei tribe, known for his military prowess.
    • 太守 - A local administrative official during the Han Dynasty, responsible for governing a region.
    • 右骁卫将军 - A military title indicating a high-ranking officer in the imperial army.
    • 白雀城 - A location in the context of military campaigns during the Tang Dynasty.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The narrative sequence must be read as a continuous story.
    • The sentence structure is elliptical, with '后日日' indicating a repetitive action.
    • The rhetorical question is elliptical, with '守株' being a metaphorical reference.
    • The sentence is elliptical, with '并生' indicating simultaneous birth.
    • The sentence is elliptical, with '不顾妻子' indicating a lack of personal consideration.
    • The sentence is elliptical, with '隔纱窗' indicating a physical barrier.
    • The sentence is elliptical, with '且战且前' indicating a continuous action.
    • The sentence is elliptical, with '况于人乎' indicating a comparison.

    Proper Nouns

    • 窦武 - A prominent figure during the Eastern Han Dynasty.
    • 郅都 - A famous general and statesman during the Western Han Dynasty.
    • 韦逞母宗氏 - Refers to Wei Cheng's mother, a respected scholar during the Eastern Han Dynasty.
    • 荀菘 - A figure during the Eastern Han Dynasty, known for his daughter's bravery.
    • 契丹何力 - A notable figure from the Xianbei tribe, known for his military prowess.
    • 右骁卫将军 - A military title indicating a high-ranking officer in the imperial army.
    • 白雀城 - A location in the context of military campaigns during the Tang Dynasty.

    Translation

    Once upon a time, a man was out traveling when he encountered a rabbit running along and hitting a tree, which caused it to die. He took the rabbit. Later, he neglected his family and livelihood, sitting daily beneath the tree, waiting for the rabbit to reappear. Someone who passed by saw him and asked, "Why are you waiting for the rabbit like this?"

    During the Later Han Dynasty, when Dou Wu was born, his mother also gave birth to a snake, which was then sent to the mountains. When Dou Wu's mother died, a large snake appeared beside her coffin, bowing repeatedly with blood flowing from its mouth before departing.

    Zhi Du served as the governor of Jinan and executed more than 300 families of the powerful clans, without regard for his own family.

    During the Later Han Dynasty, Wei Cheng's mother, Zong Shi, extensively studied the classics and established a school with 120 students, teaching them through a gauze window.

    Xun Song had a daughter named Guan. When Xun Song was the governor of Xiangyang, he was besieged by Du Lu. Xun Song sought help from General Shi Lian, the Southern Pacification Commander. At the age of 13, Guan led more than a thousand brave warriors, scaled the city walls to break through the siege, and fought their way forward to seek aid from Shi Lian. Shi Lian lent her troops to rescue Xun Song, and the bandits fled in disarray.

    Qidu Helì, a chieftain and governor of the Western tribes, was granted the title of Right Guard General and established many military achievements early in his career. When he was sent to campaign against the Liao, he reached Baique City, where he was surrounded by the enemy. He suffered a severe wound from an enemy spear in his waist. The emperor ordered that, upon capturing the city, anyone who wielded a spear should be brought to him. Emperor Taizong handed over a man named Gao Tu Bochu, ordering Helì to kill him. Helì said, "Even dogs and horses remain loyal to their masters; how much more so humans? He was acting on behalf of his master, using a spear to stab me, which shows his bravery. We have never met, so how could he be my enemy?" Thus, he spared him. [74]

    郄超有旷世之度，每有寒素后进，力引拔之。死日，为其作诔者四千余人。
    　　鲁肃以义气周急为意。周瑜为居巢长，居母丧，过肃求粮。时肃有米两囤，各三千斛，指一囤与瑜。瑜奇之，遂定交。卒霸吴。
    　　晋王浑妻钟氏，名琰，有藻鉴。生女子淑，求夫，有兵家之子甚俊，欲妻之。令与群吏处，琰自帷中窃视之，曰：“此人才足拔萃，然地寒寿促，不足展其器。”遂止。其人数月卒。
    　　伊尹负鼎以干汤，汤令调味甚甘，得进见。汤问之，答曰：“使臣调国亦如是。”遂以为相，果成王道。
    　　玄宗幸蜀，裴士淹从驾。马上以商较当时卿相，士淹曰：“姚元之如何？”上曰：“才而健者也。“宋如何？”上曰：“贤而泥者也。”论及数十人，皆当其目。末曰：“李林甫如何？”上曰：“妒贤嫉能，古今无比。”士淹曰：“若陛下知之，何委用如此之深也！”上俯首而无言。 [75]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [75]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 有旷世之度 - Phrase meaning 'possesses a rare and exceptional demeanor,' emphasizing exceptional character.
    • 力引拔之 - Means 'to exert effort in promoting them,' indicating active support for others' advancement.
    • 为其作诔者四千余人 - Hyperbolic expression indicating that a large number of people wrote eulogies for him, emphasizing his influence and reputation.
    • 以义气周急为意 - Means 'to prioritize helping those in urgent need with a sense of righteousness,' highlighting a moral commitment.
    • 指一囤与瑜 - Means 'to point to one of the two piles and give it to Zhou Yu,' indicating a generous and decisive act.
    • 妒贤嫉能，古今无比 - Means 'envies the talented and fears the capable, unparalleled in ancient and modern times,' highlighting extreme jealousy and malice.
    • 若陛下知之，何委用如此之深也 - Rhetorical question meaning 'If the emperor knew this, why would he entrust him so deeply?' questioning the emperor's judgment.
    • 俯首而无言 - Means 'lowered his head and remained silent,' indicating a moment of reflection or disappointment.
    • 幸蜀 - Means 'visited Shu,' referring to Emperor Xuanzong's journey to Sichuan during the An Lushan Rebellion.
    • 从驾 - Means 'followed the emperor,' indicating service to the emperor.
    • 商较当时卿相 - Means 'compared the current high officials,' indicating a discussion of contemporary figures.

    Xie Chao possessed a rare and exceptional demeanor. Whenever there were humble and newly emerging individuals, he exerted effort in promoting them. On the day of his death, over four thousand people wrote eulogies for him.

    Lu Su prioritized helping those in urgent need with a sense of righteousness. When Zhou Yu was serving as the magistrate of Juchao and was in mourning for his mother, he passed by Lu Su seeking grain. At that time, Lu Su had two piles of grain, each containing three thousand shi. He pointed to one pile and gave it to Zhou Yu. Zhou Yu was impressed by this and thus established a friendship with him. Lu Su eventually became the dominant figure in Wu.

    The wife of Jin's Prince Wenhun, Lady Zhong, whose name was Yan, was known for her discernment. Her daughter, named Shu, was seeking a husband. There was a son of a military official who was exceptionally talented, and she wished to marry him. She instructed him to interact with a group of officials, while she secretly observed him from behind a curtain. She said, "This person has the potential to stand out, but his circumstances are poor, and his life is short, which will not allow him to fully display his abilities." She then stopped the marriage. That man died a few months later.

    Yi Yin carried a tripod to seek an audience with King Tang. King Tang ordered him to prepare a dish, which turned out to be very delicious. Impressed, King Tang granted him an audience. When asked, Yi Yin replied, "If I were to govern the country, I would do so in the same manner." He was then appointed as chancellor and successfully established the Way of the King.

    Emperor Xuanzong of Tang visited Shu, and Pei Shiyuan followed the emperor. On the way, they compared the current high officials. Pei Shiyuan asked, "How is Yao Yuanzhi?" The emperor replied, "He is both talented and vigorous." He then asked, "How is Song Qi?" The emperor replied, "He is virtuous but stubborn." They discussed several others, and the emperor provided fitting evaluations for each. Finally, Pei Shiyuan asked, "How is Li Linfu?" The emperor replied, "He envies the talented and fears the capable, unparalleled in ancient and modern times." Pei Shiyuan said, "If the emperor knew this, why would he entrust him so deeply?" The emperor lowered his head and remained silent. [75]

    晋杜预拜荆州，贺客皆集，羊与裴楷后至⊥坐连床，怒曰：“杜预以连榻待客。”拂衣而出。
    　　武宗朝宰相李德裕奢侈极，每食一杯羹，费钱约三万，杂宝贝珠玉雄黄朱砂煎汁为之。至三煎，即弃其滓于沟中。
    　　汉昭帝纳上官桀孙女，其父名安，策立为后，方年六岁。
    　　唐萧颖士，开元中，年十九岁，擢进士第。儒、释、道三教，无不该博。然性褊躁，忿戾无比。常使一佣仆，曰杜亮。每一决责，便至疮痍，养平复，为其指使如故。人有劝亮曰：“子，佣夫也。何不适善主，而自苦若是”答曰：“愚岂不知但爱其才，慕其博奥。”以此恋恋不能而卒至于死也。 [76]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [76]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 连床 - Metaphor for close companionship or intimacy; often translated as 'sharing a bed' or 'sitting together on the same bed'.
    • 拂衣而出 - Gesture of anger or indignation; often translated as 'brushing off one's clothes and leaving'.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋杜预 - Du Yu, a prominent official and military leader of the Jin dynasty.
    • 荆州 - A major province in ancient China, known for its strategic importance.
    • 羊祜 - Yang Hu, a famous general and statesman of the Jin dynasty.
    • 裴楷 - Pei Kai, a scholar and statesman of the Jin dynasty.
    • 武宗朝 - The reign of Emperor Wuzong of the Tang dynasty.
    • 李德裕 - Li Deyu, a powerful chancellor known for his political and literary achievements.
    • 汉昭帝 - Emperor Zhao of Han, the ninth emperor of the Western Han dynasty.
    • 上官桀 - Shangguan Jie, a high-ranking official and military leader of the Han dynasty.
    • 唐萧颖士 - Xiao Yingshi, a renowned scholar and poet of the Tang dynasty.
    • 开元中 - The Kaiyuan period, a golden age of the Tang dynasty under Emperor Xuanzong.
    • 儒、释、道三教 - Confucianism, Buddhism, and Taoism, the three major philosophical and religious traditions in Chinese history.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋杜预 - Du Yu, a prominent official and military leader of the Jin dynasty.
    • 荆州 - A major province in ancient China.
    • 羊祜 - Yang Hu, a famous general and statesman of the Jin dynasty.
    • 裴楷 - Pei Kai, a scholar and statesman of the Jin dynasty.
    • 武宗朝 - The reign of Emperor Wuzong of the Tang dynasty.
    • 李德裕 - Li Deyu, a powerful chancellor known for his political and literary achievements.
    • 汉昭帝 - Emperor Zhao of Han, the ninth emperor of the Western Han dynasty.
    • 上官桀 - Shangguan Jie, a high-ranking official and military leader of the Han dynasty.
    • 唐萧颖士 - Xiao Yingshi, a renowned scholar and poet of the Tang dynasty.
    • 开元中 - The Kaiyuan period, a golden age of the Tang dynasty under Emperor Xuanzong.
    • 儒、释、道三教 - Confucianism, Buddhism, and Taoism, the three major philosophical and religious traditions in Chinese history.

    During the Jin dynasty, Du Yu was appointed as the governor of Jing Province. All the guests came to congratulate him, but Yang Hu and Pei Kai arrived later. They sat together on the same bed, and Yang Hu became angry, saying, "Du Yu treats his guests with such intimacy." He then brushed off his clothes and left.

    During the reign of Emperor Wuzong, the Chancellor Li Deyu was extremely extravagant. Each time he ate a bowl of soup, it cost about thirty thousand coins, made from a mixture of precious stones, pearls, jade, cinnabar, and realgar. After three boils, he would discard the residue into the gutter.

    Emperor Zhao of Han took the granddaughter of Shangguan Jie as his wife. Her father was named An, and she was made empress at the age of six.

    During the Kaiyuan period, Xiao Yingshi, at the age of nineteen, was selected as a Jinshi (imperial scholar). He was well-versed in Confucianism, Buddhism, and Taoism, but he had a narrow and hasty temper, and was extremely irritable. He often employed a hired servant named Du Liang. Every time he disciplined him, it would result in injuries. After healing, he continued to serve as usual. Someone advised Du Liang, "You are a hired servant. Why don't you find a good master instead of suffering like this?" Du Liang replied, "I am not stupid; I only admire his talent and his extensive knowledge." He was so attached that he eventually died. [76]

    吴诸葛恪妻晨起对妆台，一婢侍后，忽跃身触栋，张目大叫曰：“室家被害。”有顷，家人归曰：“恪为孙峻所杀。”
    　　陈仲弓《异闻记》曰：“张广定者，遭乱避地，有一女子，四岁，不能走，又不忍弃之，乃悬笼于古冢中，意谓他日得骸骨。及三年，归引取之，见其尚活。问之，女答曰：“食尽则馁，见其旁有物，引颈呼吸，则效之，故能活。”广定入冢视之，乃一龟也。陈之言，固当不妄。
    　　玄宗偶与宁王博，召太真妃立观，俄而风冒妃帔，覆乐人贺怀智巾帻，香气馥郁不灭。后幸蜀归，怀智以其巾进于上，上执之潸然而泣，曰：“此吾在位时，西国有献香三丸，赐太真，谓之瑞龙脑。” [77]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [77]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 忽跃身触栋 - Sudden and unexpected action, meaning 'suddenly jumped and hit the beam.'
