    • 张目大叫曰 - Vivid description of opening eyes wide and shouting loudly, emphasizing dramatic nature.
    • 室家被害 - Cryptic phrase meaning 'the household is in danger' or 'the family is threatened.'
    • 有顷，家人归曰 - Indicates a short while later, the family members returned and said...
    • 及三年，归引取之 - Indicates after three years, the person returned to retrieve the girl.
    • 见其旁有物，引颈呼吸，则效之 - Describes a sequence of actions: seeing something nearby, extending the neck to breathe, and imitating it.
    • 故能活 - This is why she survived.
    • 俄而风冒妃帔 - Suddenly, the wind blew over Consort Yang's veil.
    • 覆乐人贺怀智巾帻 - It covered the headgear of the musician He Huizhi.
    • 香气馥郁不灭 - The fragrance was strong and lingering.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 吴诸葛恪 - Refers to Zhuge Ke, a historical figure from the state of Wu during the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 孙峻 - Refers to Sun Jun, another historical figure from the state of Wu.
    • 陈仲弓 - Refers to Chen Zhonggong, a historian and author of 'Yiwen Ji.'
    • 张广定 - Refers to Zhang Guangding, a historical figure who encountered chaos and had to flee.
    • 玄宗 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a famous emperor known for his cultural and artistic achievements.
    • 宁王 - Refers to Prince Ning, a member of the imperial family.
    • 太真妃 - Refers to Consort Yang, also known as Yang Guifei, a beloved concubine of Emperor Xuanzong.
    • 贺怀智 - Refers to He Huizhi, a musician in the imperial court.
    • 西国 - Refers to a Western country, likely referring to a foreign nation that presented gifts to the Tang court.

    Proper Nouns

    • 吴诸葛恪 - Zhuge Ke of Wu.
    • 孙峻 - Sun Jun.
    • 陈仲弓 - Chen Zhonggong.
    • 张广定 - Zhang Guangding.
    • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong.
    • 宁王 - Prince Ning.
    • 太真妃 - Consort Yang.
    • 贺怀智 - He Huizhi.
    • 西国 - A Western country.

    In the state of Wu, Zhuge Ke's wife woke up early and sat in front of her makeup table. A servant stood behind her, when suddenly she jumped up and hit the beam, opening her eyes wide and shouting loudly, saying, "The household is in danger." A short while later, the family members returned and said, "Ke was killed by Sun Jun."

    Chen Zhonggong, in his work "Yiwen Ji," recorded the following: "Zhang Guangding, who encountered chaos and had to flee, found a four-year-old girl who could not walk and was unwilling to abandon her. He placed her in a basket and hung it in an ancient tomb, intending to retrieve bones in the future. After three years, he returned to retrieve the girl and found that she was still alive. When he asked her, she replied, 'When food ran out, I became hungry. I saw something nearby, extended my neck to breathe, and imitated it, so I survived.' Zhang Guangding entered the tomb to see the object and found that it was a turtle. Chen Zhonggong's words are undoubtedly true."

    Emperor Xuanzong happened to play a game of Go with Prince Ning and summoned Consort Yang to stand and watch. Suddenly, the wind blew over Consort Yang's veil, covering the headgear of the musician He Huizhi. The fragrance was strong and lingering. After the emperor's journey to Shu, he returned, and He Huizhi presented the headgear to the emperor. The emperor held it and wept, saying, "This was the time when a Western country presented three pieces of incense, which were given to Consort Yang and called 'Rui Longnao.'" [77]

    晋荀爽女适阴瑜，周岁，瑜卒。爽以女才高气逸，愍其少寡，欲夺志再嫁郭奕，遣所亲人问之。女私挟刃至，爽夺之。其后广集亲族，设大宴，方合，令奕突出见之。女令四角备烛，与奕相见。奕但危坐。即令备浴，女遣二侍者出家以取他物，乃刺臂血书扇曰：“以尸还阴氏。”自缢而死。
    　　昔者愚公居山之阴，而出入有阻，乃勖励子孙移之。山神见曰：“山极崇高，汝何可移”公曰：“吾生有子，子复有孙，子子孙孙，誓而移之，何为不可”于是神命夸娥氏为移之。
    　　秦于骊山之下，坑儒士二百四十人，焚诗书，皆用李斯之计，欲愚黔首焉。
    　　赫连勃勃，本号屈子，自改其姓云赫连勃勃，言辉赫与天连。杀人，积其头为京观，谓之髑髅台。蒸土筑城，锥刺入，即杖筑者死；刺之不入，即锻锥者死。其造器，射入甲者，杀铠匠；不入者，杀弓匠。莫知所措。 [78]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [78]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晉荀爽女適陰瑜 - Standard biographical formula meaning 'Jin Xunshuang's daughter married Yin Yu.'
    • 愍其少寡 - Means 'pitied her for being young and widowed.'
    • 欲奪志再嫁郭奕 - Means 'wanted to change her mind and have her remarry Guo Yi.'
    • 遣所亲人问之 - Means 'sent his close relatives to ask her.'
    • 女私挟刃至 - Means 'the woman secretly carried a knife with her.'
    • 爽夺之 - Means 'Xunshuang took it from her.'
    • 广集亲族，设大宴 - Means 'gathered many relatives and set up a grand banquet.'
    • 方合，令奕突出见之 - Means 'just as they were about to start, he ordered Guo Yi to suddenly appear before her.'
    • 女令四角备烛 - Means 'the woman ordered candles to be placed at the four corners.'
    • 与奕相见 - Means 'to meet Guo Yi.'
    • 但危坐 - Means 'he only sat upright.'
    • 即令备浴 - Means 'immediately ordered a bath to be prepared.'
    • 女遣二侍者出家以取他物 - Means 'the woman sent two maidservants out of the house to fetch other things.'
    • 乃刺臂血书扇曰 - Means 'then she pricked her arm and wrote with her blood on a fan, saying...'
    • 以尸还阴氏 - Means 'to return her body to the Yin family.'
    • 自缢而死 - Means 'hanged herself and died.'
    • 昔者愚公居山之阴 - Refers to the ancient fable 'The Foolish Old Man Removes the Mountains,' where the old man lives in the shadow of the mountains.
    • 勖励子孙移之 - Means 'encouraged his descendants to move it.'
    • 山神见曰 - Means 'the mountain god said.'
    • 山极崇高 - Means 'the mountain is extremely high.'
    • 汝何可移 - Means 'how can you move it?'
    • 吾生有子，子复有孙 - Means 'I have children, and they will have grandchildren.'
    • 誓而移之 - Means 'swear to move it.'
    • 何为不可 - Means 'why not?'
    • 于是神命夸娥氏为移之 - Means 'then the god commanded the Qu'e clan to move it.'
    • 秦于骊山之下，坑儒士二百四十人 - Means 'Qin buried 240 scholars alive at Lishan.'
    • 焚诗书，皆用李斯之计 - Means 'burned the *Shi* and *Shu* (Books), all according to Li Si's plan.'
    • 欲愚黔首焉 - Means 'to make the common people ignorant.'
    • 赫连勃勃，本号屈子 - Means 'Helian Bobo, originally known as Qushizi.'
    • 自改其姓云赫连勃勃 - Means 'changed his surname to Helian Bobo, saying 'bright and connected with heaven.'
    • 杀人，积其头为京观 - Means 'killed people, piling their heads into a *jingguan* (trophy heap).'
    • 谓之髑髅台 - Means 'called it the Skull Tower.'
    • 蒸土筑城 - Means 'boiled clay to build a city.'
    • 锥刺入，即杖筑者死 - Means 'if a spearhead penetrated, the builder was beaten to death.'
    • 刺之不入，即锻锥者死 - Means 'if it did not penetrate, the spearhead maker was beaten to death.'
    • 其造器，射入甲者，杀铠匠 - Means 'in making weapons, if an arrow penetrated armor, the armor maker was killed.'
    • 不入者，杀弓匠 - Means 'if it did not penetrate, the bow maker was killed.'
    • 莫知所措 - Means 'no one knew what to do.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋荀爽 - Refers to Xunshuang of the Jin dynasty, a historical figure known for his influence and actions.
    • 阴瑜 - Refers to Yin Yu, the husband of Xunshuang's daughter.
    • 郭奕 - Refers to Guo Yi, a man to whom Xunshuang wanted to have his daughter remarry.
    • 愚公 - Refers to the character in the ancient fable 'The Foolish Old Man Removes the Mountains,' symbolizing perseverance and determination.
    • 夸娥氏 - Refers to the Qu'e clan, a legendary group of strong men in Chinese mythology.
    • 秦 - Refers to the Qin dynasty, known for its harsh policies and the burning of books.
    • 李斯 - Refers to Li Si, a prominent statesman and advisor to the First Emperor of Qin, known for his role in the burning of books and the burying of scholars.
    • 赫连勃勃 - Refers to Helian Bobo, a historical figure who founded the Xia dynasty and was known for his brutal and tyrannical rule.
    • 骊山 - Refers to Lishan, a famous mountain in Shaanxi province, known for its historical significance during the Qin dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋荀爽 - Historical figure, Xunshuang of the Jin dynasty.
    • 阴瑜 - Historical figure, Yin Yu.
    • 郭奕 - Historical figure, Guo Yi.
    • 愚公 - Fable character, the Foolish Old Man.
    • 夸娥氏 - Mythological group, the Qu'e clan.
    • 秦 - Dynasty, the Qin dynasty.
    • 李斯 - Historical figure, Li Si.
    • 赫连勃勃 - Historical figure, Helian Bobo.
    • 骊山 - Geographical location, Lishan mountain.

    The daughter of Xunshuang of the Jin dynasty married Yin Yu. After a year, Yin Yu died. Xunshuang, considering his daughter's high talent and spirited nature, pitied her for being young and widowed, and wished to change her mind and have her remarry Guo Yi. He sent his close relatives to ask her. The woman secretly carried a knife with her, and Xunshuang took it from her. Later, he gathered many relatives and set up a grand banquet. Just as they were about to start, he ordered Guo Yi to suddenly appear before her. The woman ordered candles to be placed at the four corners and met Guo Yi. Guo Yi only sat upright. Xunshuang immediately ordered a bath to be prepared. The woman sent two maidservants out of the house to fetch other things, and then she pricked her arm and wrote with her blood on a fan, saying, "Return my body to the Yin family." She hanged herself and died.

    In ancient times, there was a man named Foolish Old Man who lived in the shadow of the mountains, and his entrance and exit were blocked. He encouraged his descendants to move the mountains. The mountain god said, "The mountain is extremely high. How can you move it?" The old man replied, "I have children, and they will have grandchildren. Generation after generation, we swear to move it. Why not?" Thus, the god commanded the Qu'e clan to move the mountains.

    During the Qin dynasty, 240 scholars were buried alive at Lishan. The Shi and Shu (Books) were burned, all according to Li Si's plan, to make the common people ignorant.

    Helian Bobo, originally known as Qushizi, changed his surname to Helian Bobo, saying, "bright and connected with heaven." He killed people, piling their heads into a jingguan (trophy heap), calling it the Skull Tower. He boiled clay to build a city. If a spearhead penetrated, the builder was beaten to death; if it did not penetrate, the spearhead maker was beaten to death. In making weapons, if an arrow penetrated armor, the armor maker was killed; if it did not penetrate, the bow maker was killed. No one knew what to do. [78]

    梁周兴嗣为散骑常侍，聪明多才思。武帝出千言，无章句，令嗣次之，因成《千字文》。归而两目俱丧。及死，开视之，心如掬燥泥。
    　　刘幽求自朝邑尉为中书舍人，三日内拜相。
    　　太宗谓虞世南一人有五绝：一曰博闻，二曰德行，三曰书翰，四曰词藻，五曰忠直。图形凌烟，寿年八十一终。
    　　公孙瓒既杀刘虞，则见有胜己者皆杀之。男子年七岁者，不许入城。传达皆用妇人，令语音雄者宣扬其命。
    　　袁绍逼急，乃先缢其妻儿姊妹，然后自赴火而死。
    　　魏武残人性命，重伎艺。有一歌儿性甚慧，而声响入云。操爱其声未忍杀，乃于群妾中求得二人，声如歌者，密令教授，数月乃成。听之，立杀其前者。
    　　范粲，字承明。时齐王芳被废，承明哭甚恸，因不仕。景王辅政，召之不赴，称疾，佯狂不言，足不履地，人不闻音。家有婚姻，咨访之，合意者，即色不变；否者，即卧寝不安。家人以此候其旨于所寝之车。 [79]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [79]

    Proper Nouns

    • 梁周兴嗣 - A historical figure, specifically a person named Liang Zhou Xingsi.
    • 武帝 - Refers to Emperor Wu, a ruler in Chinese history.
    • 刘幽求 - A historical figure, specifically a person named Liu Youqiu.
    • 虞世南 - A historical figure, specifically a person named Yu Shiniu.
    • 公孙瓒 - A historical figure, specifically a person named Gong Sun Zan.
    • 袁绍 - A historical figure, specifically a person named Yuan Shao.
    • 魏武 - Refers to Cao Cao, a prominent warlord and statesman in Chinese history.
    • 范粲 - A historical figure, specifically a person named Fan Can.
    • 齐王芳 - Refers to Prince Fang of Qi, a member of the imperial family.
    • 景王 - Refers to Sima Shi, a regent during the Three Kingdoms period.

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 聪明多才思 - Intelligent and full of talent and thought.
    • 武帝出千言，无章句，令嗣次之，因成《千字文》 - Emperor Wu produced a thousand words without chapters or sentences, ordered Zhou Xingsi to arrange them, and thus created the Thousand Character Classic.
    • 归而两目俱丧 - Upon returning, both his eyes were lost.
    • 及死，开视之，心如掬燥泥 - Upon death, upon opening it, the heart was like scooping dry mud.
    • 刘幽求自朝邑尉为中书舍人，三日内拜相 - Liu Youqiu, from the position of magistrate of Chaoyi, became a Secretary of the Central Secretariat and was appointed chancellor within three days.
    • 太宗谓虞世南一人有五绝 - Emperor Taizong said that Yu Shiniu has five excellences.
    • 一曰博闻，二曰德行，三曰书翰，四曰词藻，五曰忠直 - The first is broad knowledge, the second is virtue and conduct, the third is calligraphy and writing, the fourth is rhetoric and style, the fifth is loyalty and integrity.
    • 图形凌烟，寿年八十一终 - Had his image painted in the Hall of Bright Clouds and lived to the age of eighty-one and died.
    • 公孙瓒既杀刘虞，则见有胜己者皆杀之 - After killing Liu Yu, then saw those who were superior to him and killed them all.
    • 男子年七岁者，不许入城 - Men aged seven were not allowed to enter the city.
    • 传达皆用妇人，令语音雄者宣扬其命 - To convey, all used women, ordered those with strong voices to proclaim his commands.
    • 袁绍逼急，乃先缢其妻儿姊妹，然后自赴火而死 - In dire straits, then first hanged his wife, children, and sisters, then threw himself into the fire and died.
    • 魏武残人性命，重伎艺 - Cao Cao was cruel to human life but valued skills.
    • 有一歌儿性甚慧，而声响入云 - There was a singing boy who was very intelligent and whose voice could reach the clouds.
    • 操爱其声未忍杀，乃于群妾中求得二人，声如歌者，密令教授，数月乃成 - Cao Cao loved his voice and did not dare to kill him, then sought out two women among his concubines whose voices were like his, secretly ordered them to teach him, and after several months they succeeded.
    • 听之，立杀其前者 - After hearing it, immediately killed the previous ones.
    • 范粲，字承明 - Fan Can, style name Chengming.
    • 时齐王芳被废，承明哭甚恸，因不仕 - When Prince Fang of Qi was deposed, Chengming wept bitterly and therefore did not take office.
    • 景王辅政，召之不赴，称疾，佯狂不言，足不履地，人不闻音 - When Sima Shi assisted in governance, when summoned did not respond, claimed illness, pretended madness and did not speak, feet did not touch the ground, people did not hear his voice.
    • 家有婚姻，咨访之，合意者，即色不变；否者，即卧寝不安 - When there were marriages in the family, consulted him, if they agreed, his expression did not change; if they disagreed, he would not sleep peacefully.
    • 家人以此候其旨于所寝之车 - Family members gauged his wishes from the carriage where he slept.

    Liang Zhou Xingsi served as a Scattered Rider and Attendant, intelligent and full of talent and thought. Emperor Wu produced a thousand words without chapters or sentences, ordered Zhou Xingsi to arrange them, and thus created the Thousand Character Classic. Upon returning, both his eyes were lost. Upon death, upon opening it, the heart was like scooping dry mud.

    Liu Youqiu, from the position of magistrate of Chaoyi, became a Secretary of the Central Secretariat and was appointed chancellor within three days.

    Emperor Taizong said that Yu Shiniu has five excellences: the first is broad knowledge, the second is virtue and conduct, the third is calligraphy and writing, the fourth is rhetoric and style, the fifth is loyalty and integrity. Had his image painted in the Hall of Bright Clouds, lived to the age of eighty-one and died.

    After killing Liu Yu, then saw those who were superior to him and killed them all. Men aged seven were not allowed to enter the city. To convey, all used women, ordered those with strong voices to proclaim his commands.

    In dire straits, then first hanged his wife, children, and sisters, then threw himself into the fire and died.

    Cao Cao was cruel to human life but valued skills. There was a singing boy who was very intelligent and whose voice could reach the clouds. Cao Cao loved his voice and did not dare to kill him, then sought out two women among his concubines whose voices were like his, secretly ordered them to teach him, and after several months they succeeded. After hearing it, immediately killed the previous ones.

    Fan Can, style name Chengming. When Prince Fang of Qi was deposed, Chengming wept bitterly and therefore did not take office. When Sima Shi assisted in governance, when summoned did not respond, claimed illness, pretended madness and did not speak, feet did not touch the ground, people did not hear his voice. When there were marriages in the family, consulted him, if they agreed, his expression did not change; if they disagreed, he would not sleep peacefully. Family members gauged his wishes from the carriage where he slept. [79]

    唐狄仁杰为安抚使，除去淫祠一千二百所。
    　　张巡守宁陵，事急心孤，每战，喊一声，即雁数行飞逆。
    　　阮籍居母丧，有吊客至，籍哭，即呕血，毁瘠骨立。
    　　时苗为寿春令，谒治中蒋济，济醉，不见之。归而刻木为人，书曰：“酒徒蒋济”，以弓矢射之。牧长闻之，不能制。
    　　季孙相鲁，家无衣帛之妾，枥无食粟之马。
    　　哀牢夷，其先有妇人名沙壹，居哀牢山，捕鱼水中，触沈木若有感，因妊孕，十月而生十子。今西南夷，其裔也。
    　　唐高祖起义并州，弟三女柴绍妻聚兵、杜间以应高祖。高祖登位后，封平阳公主，号“娘子军”，克著勋绩。获封邑不因夫子者，葬用卤簿，自此始。
    　　魏文侯问孤卷子曰：“父子兄弟臣贤，足恃乎”对曰：“父贤不过尧，而丹朱傲；子贤不过舜，而瞽瞍拘；兄贤不过舜，而象傲；弟贤不过旦，而管诛；臣贤不过汤武，而桀纣伐。君欲理国，贤可恃乎！” [80]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [80]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 除去淫祠一千二百所 - Removed 1,200 superfluous shrines; 淫祠 refers to unauthorized or excessive shrines, often built for local deities or spirits.
    • 每战，喊一声，即雁数行飞逆 - Every time he shouted, geese flew in rows against him; metaphor for the psychological impact of Zhang Xun's leadership, where his cries were so powerful that they could even affect birds.
    • 籍哭，即呕血，毁瘠骨立 - Jue cried, and vomited blood, becoming emaciated and standing like a skeleton; vividly describes the intense grief and physical toll of mourning.
    • 归而刻木为人，书曰：“酒徒蒋济”，以弓矢射之 - Returning home, he carved a figure out of wood, wrote 'drunkard Jiang Ji,' and shot it with an arrow; symbolic act of revenge and a critique of Jiang Ji's behavior.
    • 牧长闻之，不能制 - The local officials heard about it and could not control him; indicates the power and influence of the individuals involved.
    • 家无衣帛之妾，枥无食粟之马 - He had no concubines dressed in silk, and no horses fed on grain; emphasizes Duke Ji's frugality and moral integrity.
    • 其先有妇人名沙壹，居哀牢山 - Her ancestors were a woman named Sha Yi, who lived in Ailao Mountain; introduces the mythological origin of the Ailao tribe.
    • 今西南夷，其裔也 - The present-day southwestern barbarians are her descendants; connects the myth to the current population.
    • 获封邑不因夫子者，葬用卤簿，自此始 - The first case of a woman receiving a fiefdom without being a wife of a noble, and being buried with a full ceremonial escort; highlights a significant change in social norms.
    • 贤可恃乎！ - Can one rely on virtue?; rhetorical question challenging the assumption that virtue alone is sufficient for good governance.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 狄仁杰 - Di Renjie, a famous Tang Dynasty official known for his integrity and administrative skills.
    • 张巡 - Zhang Xun, a Tang Dynasty general who defended Ningling against rebel forces during the An Lushan Rebellion.
    • 阮籍 - Ruan Ji, a prominent figure of the Wei-Jin period, known for his philosophical and literary works.
    • 时苗 - Shi Miao, a historical figure known for his strict adherence to principles and his symbolic act of revenge.
    • 季孙 - Duke Ji of Lu, a ruler known for his frugality and moral conduct.
    • 哀牢夷 - An ancient ethnic group in Yunnan, China, known for their unique culture and mythology.
    • 唐高祖 - Li Yuan, the founder of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 魏文侯 - Duke Wen of Wei, a ruler of the state of Wei during the Warring States period, known for his reforms and cultural patronage.
    • 孤卷子 - Gu Jianzi, a minister or advisor to Huiwen.

    Proper Nouns

    • 狄仁杰 - Di Renjie, a Tang Dynasty official.
    • 张巡 - Zhang Xun, a Tang Dynasty general.
    • 阮籍 - Ruan Ji, a Wei-Jin philosopher and poet.
    • 时苗 - Shi Miao, a historical figure.
    • 季孙 - Duke Ji of Lu.
    • 哀牢夷 - Ailao tribe.
    • 唐高祖 - Li Yuan, founder of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 魏文侯 - Duke Wen of Wei.
    • 孤卷子 - Gu Jianzi, a minister or advisor to Huiwen.

    During the Tang Dynasty, Di Renjie served as a pacification commissioner and removed 1,200 superfluous shrines.

    Zhang Xun, while defending Ningling, was in a desperate situation and felt isolated. Every time he shouted, geese flew in rows against him.

    Ruan Ji, during his mother's mourning period, cried so intensely that he vomited blood and became emaciated, standing like a skeleton.

    Shi Miao, while serving as the magistrate of Shouchun, visited Jiang Ji, the governor of the region, but Jiang was drunk and did not receive him. Upon returning home, Shi carved a figure out of wood, wrote "drunkard Jiang Ji" on it, and shot it with an arrow. The local officials heard about this and could not control him.

    Duke Ji of Lu, as chancellor of the state of Lu, had no concubines dressed in silk and no horses fed on grain, emphasizing his frugality and moral integrity.

    The Ailao tribe, whose ancestors were a woman named Sha Yi who lived in Ailao Mountain, caught fish in the water and was touched by a submerged log, which led to her pregnancy. She gave birth to ten sons after ten months. The present-day southwestern barbarians are her descendants.

    Emperor Gaozu of Tang raised an army in Bingzhou, and his sister-in-law, Lady Chai, gathered troops in the regions of Jie and Du to support him. After Emperor Gaozu ascended the throne, he bestowed the title of Princess of Pingyang on her, known as the "Lady General," and she achieved notable military accomplishments. She was the first woman to receive a fiefdom without being a wife of a noble and was buried with a full ceremonial escort, setting a precedent.

    Duke Wen of Wei asked Gu Jianzi, "If one's father, son, brother, and minister are all virtuous, can one rely on them?" Gu replied, "Even if one's father is as virtuous as Yao, his son Dan Zhu was arrogant; even if one's son is as virtuous as Shun, his father Gushu was imprisoned; even if one's brother is as virtuous as Shun, his younger brother Xiang was arrogant; even if one's younger brother is as virtuous as Dan, his elder brother Guan was executed; even if one's minister is as virtuous as Tang and Wu, they were overthrown by Jie and Zhou. Can one rely on virtue alone to govern the state?" [80]

    常有疾，老子曰：“先生疾甚，能无教语弟子乎！”乃大其口示老子，曰：“舌存乎！”曰：“存。岂非柔耶”“齿亡乎”曰：“亡。岂非刚耶”枞曰：“天下事尽矣。”
    　　子产闻妇人哭，使人执而拘之。果手刃其夫者。御者问曰：“何以知之”子产曰：“夫人所亲也，有病则忧，临死则哀。今夫巳死，不哀而惧，是以知有奸也。”
    　　隋朝徐德言妻陈氏，叔宝妹。因惧乱不能相保，德言乃破一镜分之，以为他年不知存亡，但端午日各持其半镜于市内卖之，以图相合。至期适市，果有一破镜。德言乃题其背曰：“镜与人俱去，镜归人不归。无复嫦娥影，空余半月辉。”时陈氏为杨素所爱。见之，乃命德言对饮，三人环坐，令陈氏赋诗一章，即还之。陈氏诗曰：“今日何迁次新官对旧官。笑啼俱不敢，方验作人难。”素感之，乃还德言。 [81]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [81]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 常有疾 - Chang Bo is ill, where '常' is a proper name, and '有疾' means 'is ill.'
    • 老子曰 - Laozi said, where '老子' refers to Laozi, the founder of Daoism.
    • 能无教语弟子乎 - Can you not teach your disciples? This is a rhetorical question implying that Chang Bo should impart wisdom before his death.
    • 大其口示老子 - He opened his mouth wide for Laozi to see. This gesture is symbolic and can be interpreted as a metaphor for revealing something important.
    • 岂非柔耶 - Is it not soft? This rhetorical question implies that the tongue's softness is its strength.
    • 齿亡乎 - Are your teeth gone? This rhetorical question implies that the teeth's hardness is their weakness.
    • 天下事尽矣 - All things in the world are complete. This statement suggests that the lesson of flexibility and adaptability is universal.
    • 子产闻妇人哭 - Zichan heard a woman crying. '子产' refers to Zichan, a statesman of the State of Zheng.
    • 使人执而拘之 - He had someone arrest her. This indicates a formal action taken by Zichan.
    • 果手刃其夫者 - It turned out she was the one who killed her husband. This reveals the outcome of the investigation.
    • 御者问曰 - The driver asked, where '御者' refers to the charioteer or driver.
    • 何以知之 - How did you know? This question seeks the reasoning behind Zichan's conclusion.
    • 夫人所亲也 - She is the one who is close to him. This refers to the woman's relationship with her husband.
    • 有病则忧 - When he is sick, she worries. This describes the typical reaction of a loving wife.
    • 临死则哀 - When he dies, she mourns. This describes the natural response to a loved one's death.
    • 不哀而惧 - She does not mourn but fears. This indicates a deviation from the expected behavior.
    • 是以知有奸也 - Therefore, I knew there was something wrong. This explains Zichan's reasoning.
    • 隋朝徐德言妻陈氏 - In the Sui Dynasty, Xu Deyan's wife Chen Shi. This introduces the historical context and characters.
    • 叔宝妹 - Sister of Shubao, where '叔宝' refers to Emperor Wen of Sui, whose sister was Chen Shi.
    • 惧乱不能相保 - Afraid of chaos and unable to protect each other. This describes the fear of separation during turbulent times.
    • 破一镜分之 - He broke a mirror and divided it. This act symbolizes a promise to reunite in the future.
    • 以为他年不知存亡 - To ensure they would know each other's fate in the future. This explains the purpose of breaking the mirror.
    • 端午日各持其半镜于市内卖之 - On the fifth day of the fifth month, they each held half of the mirror and sold it in the market. This sets up the reunion plan.
    • 以图相合 - To try to reunite. This describes the goal of the plan.
    • 至期适市 - On the appointed day, he went to the market. This describes Xu Deyan's action.
    • 果有一破镜 - He found a broken mirror. This describes the unexpected discovery.
    • 德言乃题其背曰 - Xu Deyan then wrote on the back of it. This describes his action.
    • 镜与人俱去 - The mirror and the person went together. This line reflects the separation.
    • 镜归人不归 - The mirror returns, but the person does not. This line reflects the current situation.
    • 无复嫦娥影 - There is no longer the shadow of Chang'e. This metaphor suggests the loss of the beloved.
    • 空余半月辉 - Only the half-moon's light remains. This metaphor suggests the remaining hope of reunion.
    • 时陈氏为杨素所爱 - At that time, Chen Shi was loved by Yang Su. This provides the background of Chen Shi's situation.
    • 见之，乃命德言对饮 - When she saw it, she ordered Xu Deyan to drink with her. This describes the meeting.
    • 三人环坐 - They sat in a circle. This describes the seating arrangement.
    • 令陈氏赋诗一章 - She ordered Chen Shi to compose a poem. This describes the request.
    • 即还之 - And then returned it to him. This describes the return of the mirror.
    • 陈氏诗曰 - Chen Shi's poem said, where '陈氏' refers to Chen Shi.
    • 今日何迁次 - Today, what has changed? This line reflects the unexpected turn of events.
    • 新官对旧官 - A new official faces an old official. This metaphor suggests the change in status.
    • 笑啼俱不敢 - Neither smiling nor crying, she dared not. This describes her emotional state.
    • 方验作人难 - Only now does she realize how difficult it is to be a person. This line reflects her realization of the complexities of human life.
    • 素感之，乃还德言 - Yang Su was moved by this and returned the mirror to Xu Deyan. This describes the outcome of the encounter.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 常 - A historical figure, possibly a philosopher or sage.
    • 老子 - Laozi, the founder of Daoism, a central figure in Chinese philosophy.
    • 子产 - Zichan, a statesman of the State of Zheng, known for his wisdom and governance.
    • 隋朝 - The Sui Dynasty, a short-lived but significant dynasty in Chinese history.
    • 徐德言 - Xu Deyan, a historical figure from the Sui Dynasty.
    • 陈氏 - Chen Shi, Xu Deyan's wife.
    • 叔宝 - Emperor Wen of Sui, whose sister was Chen Shi.
    • 杨素 - Yang Su, a powerful general and statesman of the Sui Dynasty.
    • 端午日 - The fifth day of the fifth month of the lunar calendar, a traditional Chinese festival.
    • 嫦娥 - Chang'e, a mythological figure associated with the moon and often used in poetry to symbolize beauty and longing.

    Chang Bo fell ill, and Laozi said, "Master, your illness is severe. Can you not teach your disciples?" Chang Bo then opened his mouth wide for Laozi to see and said, "Is my tongue still there?" Laozi replied, "Yes, it is." Chang Bo asked, "Is it not soft?" Laozi replied, "Yes, it is." Chang Bo then asked, "Are my teeth gone?" Laozi replied, "Yes, they are." Chang Bo asked, "Is it not hard?" Laozi replied, "Yes, it is." Chang Bo said, "All things in the world are complete."

    Zichan heard a woman crying and had someone arrest her. It turned out she was the one who killed her husband. The driver asked, "How did you know?" Zichan replied, "She is the one who is close to him. When he is sick, she worries; when he dies, she mourns. But now that he is dead, she does not mourn but fears. Therefore, I knew there was something wrong."

    In the Sui Dynasty, Xu Deyan's wife Chen Shi was the sister of Emperor Wen of Sui. Afraid of chaos and unable to protect each other, Xu Deyan broke a mirror and divided it, so that in the future, they would know each other's fate. They agreed to meet on the fifth day of the fifth month, each holding half of the mirror and selling it in the market, in hopes of reuniting. On the appointed day, Xu Deyan went to the market and found a broken mirror. He then wrote on the back of it, "The mirror and the person went together; the mirror returns, but the person does not. There is no longer the shadow of Chang'e; only the half-moon's light remains." At that time, Chen Shi was loved by Yang Su. When she saw the mirror, she ordered Xu Deyan to drink with her. They sat in a circle, and she ordered Chen Shi to compose a poem. After the poem was written, she returned the mirror to Xu Deyan. Chen Shi's poem said, "Today, what has changed? A new official faces an old official. Neither smiling nor crying, she dared not. Only now does she realize how difficult it is to be a person." Yang Su was moved by this and returned the mirror to Xu Deyan. [81]

    昔宇宙初开之时，只有女娲兄妹二人在昆仑山，而天下未有人民，议以为夫妻，又自羞耻。兄即与其妹上昆仑山，兄曰：“天若遣我兄妹二人为夫妻而烟悉合；若不使，烟散。”于是烟即合，其妹即来就兄，乃结草为扇，以障其面。今时人取妇执扇，象其事也。
    　　羊角哀、左伯陶二人为友而贤。俱诣道途，其造风雨，粮尽，计不俱存。角哀乃并粮与伯陶，得济；角哀入空树中饿死。
    　　刘仁轨为相，其从父、昆弟皆为北海县邑吏，人有劝曰：“若与君相同籍，而独苦差科。”答曰云云。 [82]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [82]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 昔宇宙初开之时 - Refers to the time when the universe first began, a common theme in Chinese creation myths.
    • 女娲兄妹二人 - Refers to the mythological figures Nüwa and her brother, who are central to Chinese creation stories.
    • 议以为夫妻 - Suggests a discussion or agreement to become husband and wife.
    • 自羞耻 - Indicates feeling ashamed or embarrassed.
    • 若遣我兄妹二人为夫妻而烟悉合 - A conditional statement where the smoke merging symbolizes their acceptance of the union.
    • 若不使，烟散 - Another conditional statement where the smoke dispersing indicates rejection.
    • 结草为扇，以障其面 - Describes the act of making a fan from grass to cover their faces, a symbolic gesture of modesty.
    • 今时人取妇执扇，象其事也 - Suggests that people today use fans when taking a wife, symbolizing the same modesty.
    • 羊角哀、左伯陶二人为友而贤 - Refers to two friends, Yang Jue and Zuo Botao, who are described as virtuous.
    • 俱诣道途 - Indicates that they both traveled together.
    • 其造风雨 - Suggests that they encountered a storm or adverse weather conditions.
    • 计不俱存 - Implies that they decided they could not both survive.
    • 角哀乃并粮与伯陶 - Indicates that Yang Jue gave his food to Zuo Botao.
    • 得济 - Means to survive or make it through.
    • 入空树中饿死 - Describes Yang Jue entering an empty tree and dying of starvation.
    • 刘仁轨为相 - Refers to Liu Rengui, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 其从父、昆弟皆为北海县邑吏 - Indicates that his relatives, including his father and brothers, were local officials in Beihai County.
    • 若与君相同籍，而独苦差科 - Suggests that if they were registered under the same household, he would be the only one suffering from the labor duties.
    • 答曰云云 - Indicates a response, but the content is not specified.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 女娲 - A mythological figure in Chinese culture known for creating humans and repairing the sky.
    • 昆仑山 - A mythical mountain in Chinese mythology, often associated with the abode of immortals.
    • 羊角哀、左伯陶 - Historical figures known for their friendship and virtue.
    • 刘仁轨 - A prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his administrative skills and integrity.
    • 北海县邑吏 - Local officials in Beihai County, indicating a specific administrative context.
    • 差科 - Refers to labor duties or taxes imposed on the population.

    Proper Nouns

    • 女娲 - The mythological figure Nüwa.
    • 昆仑山 - The mythical mountain Kunlun.
    • 羊角哀 - The historical figure Yang Jue.
    • 左伯陶 - The historical figure Zuo Botao.
    • 刘仁轨 - The historical figure Liu Rengui.
    • 北海县邑吏 - Local officials in Beihai County.

    In the beginning, when the universe was just starting, only Nüwa and her brother were on Mount Kunlun, and there were no people in the world. They discussed becoming husband and wife but felt ashamed. Her brother then took his sister to Mount Kunlun and said, "If heaven ordains that we, my brother and sister, should become husband and wife, the smoke will merge; if not, it will disperse." The smoke merged, and her sister came to join her brother. They then made a fan from grass to cover their faces. Today, when people take a wife, they hold a fan, symbolizing this event.

    Yang Jue and Zuo Botao were two virtuous friends. They traveled together and encountered a storm, running out of food. They decided they could not both survive. Yang Jue gave his food to Zuo Botao, allowing him to survive, while Yang Jue entered an empty tree and died of starvation.

    Liu Rengui served as a high-ranking official. His father and brothers were all local officials in Beihai County. Someone advised him, "If you are registered under the same household as your relatives, you alone will suffer from the labor duties." He responded accordingly. [82]

    汉张良，字子房。少时行至圯桥，忽于桥上见老父堕履桥下，顾良曰：“可取履。”良欲殴，视为老，俯为下履。老人曰：“孺子可教。明日早为期。”良往，已在桥。谓良：“与我期，何后也明日复来。”良于是夜半往之，少顷，父至，怀中取书一帙付良，曰：“读之，王者师。”因使去，戒良曰：“后十年济北谷城相见，即我也。”良佐汉王，为帝师。后往济北谷城问，人曰：“此黄石公庙。”即立祠。
    　　汉武帝迁淮南厉王于蜀巴，道病死。人歌曰：“一尺布，尚可缝；一斗米，尚可舂。兄弟二人不相容。”
    　　《韩子》：齐王好竽，每欲听，必三百人齐吹之，南郭先生，不知竽者。滥求百人中，吹竽食禄。宣王薨后，王即位，曰：“寡人好听竽，欲一一吹之。”南郭先生惭而退逃。今冒禄者，亦多如此。

    　　●补佚
    　　晋武帝哭羊祜，冬月涕泗交下，凝须为冰。 [83]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [83]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 孺子可教 - Phrase used to indicate that someone has potential and can be taught.
    • 凝须为冰 - Metaphorical expression indicating extreme coldness and emotional intensity, often used to describe intense grief.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 张良 - Zhang Liang, a prominent advisor to Liu Bang during the early Han dynasty.
    • 圯桥 - Yi Bridge, where Zhang Liang met the old man who gave him the *Ba Jia* (Nine Strategies) book.
    • 老父 - Respectful term for an elderly man, specifically referring to the old man who appeared to Zhang Liang.
    • 济北谷城 - Jibei Gucheng, a place in the state of Qi where Zhang Liang later met the old man again, who revealed himself to be the Yellow Emperor.
    • 淮南厉王 - Liu An, a prince of the Han dynasty who was exiled and died in exile.
    • 武帝 - Emperor Wu, referring to Emperor Wu of Han, known for his military campaigns and cultural achievements.
    • 南郭先生 - Mr. Nanguo, a character in the story of 'The Musician of Nanguo,' who pretended to be a skilled musician.
    • 宣王 - King Xuan, a historical figure in the state of Qi.
    • 立祠 - Established a temple, often for deities or revered figures.
    • 补佚 - The act of supplementing or completing missing parts of a text or story.
    • 晋武帝 - Emperor Wu of Jin, the first emperor of the Jin dynasty.
    • 羊祜 - Yang Hu, a historical figure and advisor to Emperor Wu of Jin.

    Proper Nouns

    • 子房 - Zifang, Zhang Liang's courtesy name.
    • 老父 - Old father, a respectful term for an elderly man.
    • 孺子 - Child, a term used to address someone respectfully.
    • 宣王 - King Xuan, a historical figure.
    • 立祠 - Established a temple, an action.
    • 补佚 - Supplementing omissions, an action.

    Zhang Liang, courtesy name Zifang, was a young man when he walked to Yi Bridge. Suddenly, he saw an old man fall off the bridge, and the old man looked at Zhang Liang and said, "Can you fetch my shoes?" Zhang Liang wanted to beat him but, seeing that he was old, knelt down to fetch the shoes for him. The old man said, "You can be taught. Meet me tomorrow at dawn." Zhang Liang went, but the old man was already on the bridge. He told Zhang Liang, "We had an appointment, why were you late? Come back tomorrow." Zhang Liang then went at midnight, and after a short while, the old man arrived, took a book from his chest and gave it to Zhang Liang, saying, "Read this, it will make you a teacher to kings." He then sent Zhang Liang away and warned him, "In ten years, we will meet again in Jibei Gucheng, that will be me." Zhang Liang assisted King Han, becoming the emperor's teacher. Later, when he went to Jibei Gucheng to inquire, people said, "This is the Temple of the Yellow Emperor." He then established a temple there.

    Emperor Wu of Han exiled King Liu An of Huainan to Shu and Ba, where he died on the way. People sang, "A foot of cloth, still can be sewn; a measure of rice, still can be pounded. Brothers cannot coexist."

    According to Han Feizi: King Qi loved to listen to the yu (a type of wind instrument), and whenever he wanted to listen, he would have three hundred people play it together. Mr. Nanguo, who did not know how to play the yu, joined the group of a hundred and pretended to play it to receive俸禄 (salary). After King Xuan died, King Qi ascended the throne and said, "I love to listen to the yu, and I want each person to play it individually." Mr. Nanguo was ashamed and fled. Today, those who receive俸禄 without genuine skill are also many like this.

    Supplementing Omissions
    Emperor Wu of Jin cried over Yang Hu, and in winter, his tears and snot flowed together, freezing on his mustache. [83]

    《东方朔内传》云：秦并六国，太白星窃织女侍儿梁玉清、卫承庄，逃入卫城少仙洞，四十六日不出。天帝怒，命五岳搜捕焉。太白归位。卫承庄逃焉。梁玉清有子名休，玉清谪于北斗下，常舂；其子乃配于河伯，骖乘行雨。子休每至少仙洞，耻其母淫奔之所，辄回驭，故此地常少雨焉。
    　　淮南有居客卢婴者，气质文学，俱为郡中绝。人悉以卢三郎呼之。但甚奇蹇，若在群聚中，主人必有横祸，或小儿堕井，幼女入火，既久有验。人皆捐之。时元伯和为郡守，始至，爱其材气，特开中堂设宴，众客咸集。食毕，伯和戏问左右曰：“小儿堕井乎！”曰：“否。”“小女入火乎！”曰：“否。”伯和谓坐客曰：“众君不胜故也。”顷之合饮，群客相目，惴惴然。是日，军吏围宅，擒伯和弃市。时节度使陈少游甚异之，复见其才貌，谓曰：“此人一举，非摩天不尽其才。”即厚与金帛宠荐之。行至潼关，西望烟尘，有东驰者曰：“朱Г作乱，上幸奉天县矣。” [84]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [84]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 《东方朔内传》云 - A phrase meaning 'The *Inner Biography of Dongfang Shuo* says,' introducing a citation from a classical text.
    • 太白星窃织女侍儿梁玉清、卫承庄 - A mythological reference to the star Venus stealing two maidservants named Liang Yuqing and Wei Chengzhuang.
    • 四十六日不出 - Literally 'did not emerge for forty-six days,' referring to the duration of the star's absence.
    • 天帝怒，命五岳搜捕焉 - Means 'The Heavenly Emperor was angered and ordered the Five Sacred Mountains to search for them,' a mythological event.
    • 太白归位 - Means 'Venus returned to its proper place,' indicating the resolution of the myth.
    • 耻其母淫奔之所 - Means 'shamed by the place where his mother had an illicit affair,' referring to Liang Yuqing's infamy.
    • 辄回驭 - Means 'would always turn back,' describing the rain god's behavior.
    • 故此地常少雨焉 - Means 'thus this place often lacks rain,' summarizing the consequence of the rain god's actions.
    • 淮南有居客卢婴者 - Means 'In Huainan, there was a guest named Lu Ying,' introducing a new character.
    • 气质文学，俱为郡中绝 - Means 'His character and literary talent were both outstanding in the prefecture,' praising his qualities.
    • 甚奇蹇 - Means 'very strange and unlucky,' describing his peculiar fate.
    • 众君不胜故也 - Means 'It is because you cannot bear it,' a sarcastic response to the guests' unease.
    • 节度使陈少游甚异之 - Means 'Governor Chen Shaoyou was greatly surprised by this,' noting the official's reaction.
    • 此人一举，非摩天不尽其才 - Means 'This person, once he rises, will not stop until he reaches the heavens,' a metaphor for his potential.
    • 行至潼关，西望烟尘 - Means 'When he reached Tongguan, he saw smoke and dust to the west,' indicating the approach of an army.
    • 朱Г作乱，上幸奉天县矣 - Means 'Zhu Ci has rebelled, and the emperor has fled to Fanyang County,' reporting a historical event.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 《东方朔内传》 - A classical text about the legendary figure Dongfang Shuo, known for his wit and wisdom.
    • 太白星 - The planet Venus, often associated with beauty and femininity in Chinese mythology.
    • 织女 - The weaver girl, a figure from the Chinese legend of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl.
    • 五岳 - The Five Sacred Mountains in Chinese mythology, representing the five cardinal directions.
    • 淮南 - A historical region in China, often associated with scholars and philosophers.
    • 节度使 - A military governor during the Tang Dynasty, a key political figure.
    • 朱Г - A historical rebel leader during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 奉天县 - A county in China, where the emperor fled during the rebellion.

    Proper Nouns

    • 《东方朔内传》 - The *Inner Biography of Dongfang Shuo*.
    • 太白星 - Venus.
    • 织女 - The weaver girl.
    • 五岳 - The Five Sacred Mountains.
    • 淮南 - The historical region of Huainan.
    • 卢婴 - The character Lu Ying.
    • 朱Г - The rebel leader Zhu Ci.
    • 奉天县 - Fanyang County.

    The Inner Biography of Dongfang Shuo states: During the Qin dynasty's unification of the six states, the star Venus stole two maidservants of the weaver girl, Liang Yuqing and Wei Chengzhuang, and fled to the Lesser Fairy Cave in Wei City, where they remained for forty-six days without emerging. The Heavenly Emperor was angered and ordered the Five Sacred Mountains to search for them. Venus returned to its proper place. Wei Chengzhuang escaped. Liang Yuqing had a son named Xi. Liang Yuqing was demoted to the North Star, where she often ground grain; her son was paired with the River God and served as his charioteer, driving rain. Whenever Xi passed by the Lesser Fairy Cave, he was ashamed of the place where his mother had an illicit affair, and would always turn back, thus this place often lacks rain.

    In Huainan, there was a guest named Lu Ying, whose character and literary talent were both outstanding in the prefecture. However, he was very strange and unlucky. Whenever he was in a gathering, the host would inevitably suffer misfortune, such as a child falling into a well or a young girl entering a fire. This had been verified many times, and people all abandoned him. At the time, Yuan Bohe was the prefect, and when he first arrived, he admired Lu Ying's talent and opened the central hall for a banquet, where all the guests gathered. After the meal, Yuan Bohe joked and asked the attendants, "Did a child fall into a well?" They replied, "No." "Did a young girl enter a fire?" They replied, "No." Yuan Bohe then told the guests, "You cannot bear it." Soon, they began to drink together, and the guests looked at each other uneasily. That day, the military officials surrounded the house and captured Yuan Bohe, who was executed in the marketplace. Governor Chen Shaoyou was greatly surprised by this, but also saw his talent and appearance, and said to him, "This person, once he rises, will not stop until he reaches the heavens." He generously gave him gold and silk and recommended him. When they reached Tongguan, they saw smoke and dust to the west. A rider came from the east and said, "Zhu Ci has rebelled, and the emperor has fled to Fanyang County." [84]

    扬州西灵塔，中国之尤峻峙者。唐武宗末，拆寺之前一年，有淮南词客刘隐之薄游明州，梦中如泛海，见塔东渡海，时见门僧怀信居塔三层，凭阑与隐之言，曰：“暂送塔过东海，旬日而还。”数日，隐之归扬州，即访怀信。信曰：“记海上相见时否？”隐之了然省记。数夕后，天火焚塔俱尽，白雨如泻。旁有草堂，一无所损。 [85]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [85]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 峻峙 - Compound term meaning 'tall and majestic,' used to describe the tower's impressive height and appearance.
    • 拆寺之前一年 - Literally 'the year before the destruction of temples,' referring to the period just before the Tang Dynasty's anti-Buddhist campaign.
    • 薄游 - Means 'to travel lightly' or 'to make a brief trip,' indicating a casual or short visit.
    • 明州 - An ancient name for Ningbo in Zhejiang Province.
    • 夢中如泛海 - A vivid metaphor meaning 'in a dream, as if sailing the sea,' describing the dream experience.
    • 門僧 - Refers to a monk living in the temple, specifically one who lives near the entrance.
    • 憑欄與隱之言 - Means 'leaning on the railing and speaking with Duan Yin,' indicating a conversation while standing by a railing.
    • 暫送塔過东海 - Means 'temporarily sending the tower across the East Sea,' a metaphorical description of the dream.
    • 旬日而还 - Means 'returning within ten days,' indicating a short period.
    • 天火焚塔俱尽 - Means 'the tower was completely destroyed by a heavenly fire,' describing the event.
    • 白雨如泻 - A vivid metaphor meaning 'white rain like pouring,' describing the intense rainfall.
    • 草堂 - Refers to a small, simple house or hut, often used in a literary context.
    • 一无所损 - Means 'not a single thing was damaged,' indicating that the grass hut remained intact.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 扬州 - Refers to Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
    • 西灵塔 - Refers to the Xiling Pagoda, a famous pagoda in Yangzhou.
    • 唐武宗末 - Refers to the end of Emperor Wuzong's reign, during the Tang Dynasty, a period marked by significant political and religious changes.
    • 淮南词客刘隐之 - Refers to Liu Yin, a poet from Huainan, who visited Mingzhou.
    • 门僧怀信 - Refers to Monk Huai Xin, a monk living in the temple.
    • 天火焚塔 - Refers to the destruction of the pagoda by a natural disaster, which is a significant event in the narrative.
    • 白雨如泻 - A vivid description of a natural phenomenon, often used in classical Chinese literature to evoke a sense of grandeur or intensity.

    Proper Nouns

    • 扬州 - Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
    • 西灵塔 - Xiling Pagoda, a famous pagoda in Yangzhou.
    • 唐武宗 - Emperor Wuzong of Tang, the ruler during the anti-Buddhist campaign.
    • 淮南词客刘隐之 - Liu Yin, a poet from Huainan.
    • 明州 - Ningbo, a city in Zhejiang Province.
    • 门僧怀信 - Monk Huai Xin, a monk living in the temple.
    • 草堂 - A small, simple house or hut.

    The Xiling Pagoda in Yangzhou is particularly tall and majestic in China. At the end of Emperor Wuzong's reign, in the year before the temples were to be destroyed, a poet from Huainan named Liu Yin made a brief trip to Mingzhou. In a dream, he felt as if he were sailing the sea and saw the pagoda crossing the East Sea. He occasionally saw a monk named Huai Xin living in the third story of the pagoda, leaning on the railing and speaking with Liu Yin, saying, "I am temporarily sending the pagoda across the East Sea and will return within ten days." A few days later, when Liu Yin returned to Yangzhou, he immediately visited Huai Xin. Huai Xin asked, "Do you remember when we met by the sea?" Liu Yin clearly recalled the encounter. A few nights later, the pagoda was completely destroyed by a heavenly fire, with white rain falling like a deluge. Nearby, a small hut remained undamaged. [85]

    陈霸先未贵时，有直阁吏史溥，梦有人朱衣执玉简，自天而降。简上金字书曰：“陈氏五世，三十四年。”及后主降隋，史溥尚在。
    　　李源，洛城北惠林寺住。以其父忄登为禄山所害，誓不履人事，不婚，不役僮仆。暮春之际，荫树独处，有一少年，挟弹而至，源爱其风秀，与之驯狎，问其氏行，但曰“武十三”。其依阿，不甚显扬。讯其所居，或东、或西、或南、或北不定。源叔父为福建观察使，源修觐礼；武生亦云有事东去。同舟共载，行及宋之谷熟桥，携手登岸，武曰：“与子诀矣。”源惊讯之，即曰：“某非世人也。为国掌阴兵百有余年，凝结此形。 [86]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [86]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 未貴時 - Means 'when not yet honored' or 'before becoming prominent.'
    • 直閣吏史溥 - Refers to a low-ranking official named Shi Pu who worked in the imperial palace.
    • 朱衣執玉簡 - Describes a person wearing a red robe holding a jade tablet, often associated with divine or official figures.
    • 陳氏五世，三十四年 - Indicates a prophecy about the Chen dynasty lasting five generations and thirty-four years.
    • 誓不履人事 - Means 'swore never to engage in worldly affairs.'
    • 不婚，不役僮仆 - Means 'did not marry and did not use servants.'
    • 暮春之际 - Refers to the late spring season.
    • 荫树独处 - Means 'shaded by trees, sitting alone.'
    • 挟弹而至 - Means 'arrived with a slingshot.'
    • 风秀 - Means 'graceful and refined.'
    • 驯狎 - Means 'to become familiar and friendly.'
    • 氏行 - Refers to family name and personal name.
    • 武十三 - The name of the young man, possibly indicating his age or a nickname.
    • 依阿 - Means 'ambiguous' or 'vague.'
    • 修觐礼 - Means 'to perform the ritual of visiting one's uncle.'
    • 同舟共载 - Means 'traveled together on the same boat.'
    • 谷熟桥 - Refers to a specific bridge in Caozhou.
    • 凝结此形 - Means 'solidified into this form.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 陈霸先 - A historical figure who founded the Chen dynasty (557-589 CE).
    • 隋 - The Sui dynasty, which succeeded the Chen dynasty.
    • 李源 - A historical figure who lived during the Tang dynasty.
    • 洛城北惠林寺 - Refers to the Huilin Temple in Luoyang, a city in Henan Province.
    • 福建观察使 - A high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty responsible for overseeing the Fujian region.
    • 武十三 - A mysterious figure who appears to be a supernatural being.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • This sentence describes a dream where Shi Pu sees a figure descending from heaven.
    • The jade tablet is inscribed with golden characters.
    • This prophecy is ambiguous in its exact interpretation.
    • These statements describe Li Yuan's commitment to a life of seclusion.
    • This sentence describes a serene scene where a young man arrives.
    • The young man's identity is vague and mysterious.
    • The young man's residence is described as uncertain.
    • This sequence describes their journey and the young man's departure.
    • The young man reveals his supernatural nature.

    Proper Nouns

    • 陈霸先 - The founder of the Chen dynasty.
    • 隋 - The Sui dynasty.
    • 李源 - A historical figure who lived during the Tang dynasty.
    • 洛城北惠林寺 - The Huilin Temple in Luoyang.
    • 福建观察使 - A high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty.
    • 武十三 - A mysterious figure who appears to be a supernatural being.
    • 谷熟桥 - A specific bridge in Caozhou.

    Before the Tsin dynasty, there was a low-ranking official named Shi Pu who worked in the imperial palace. He had a dream in which a figure wearing a red robe and holding a jade tablet descended from the sky. The tablet was inscribed with golden characters that read, "The Tsin dynasty will last for five generations and thirty-four years." When the last ruler of the Tsin dynasty surrendered to the Sui dynasty, Shi Pu was still alive.

    Li Yuan lived in the Huilin Temple in the northern part of Luoyang. After his father, Li Deng, was killed by An Lushan, Li Yuan swore never to engage in worldly affairs, did not marry, and did not use servants. During the late spring, he sat alone under the shade of trees when a young man arrived with a slingshot. Li Yuan admired his graceful and refined demeanor and became familiar and friendly with him. When he asked the young man's family name and personal name, the young man only replied, "Wu Shisan." The young man's identity was vague and ambiguous. When Li Yuan inquired about his residence, the young man replied that it was sometimes east, sometimes west, sometimes south, and sometimes north, with no definite location. Li Yuan's uncle was the governor of Fujian, and Li Yuan went to pay his respects. The young man, Wu Shisan, also said he had business to attend to in the east. They traveled together on the same boat and reached the Gu Shu Bridge in Caozhou. They disembarked hand in hand, and Wu Shisan said, "Farewell, my friend." Li Yuan was surprised and asked him, and the young man replied, "I am not an ordinary mortal. I have been in charge of the hidden armies of the state for over a hundred years and have solidified into this form." [86]

    今夕，托质于张氏为男子，十五得明经，后终邑令。”又云：“子之禄亦薄，年登八十，朝廷当以谏议大夫征。后二年，当卒矣。我后七年，复与君相见。”言讫，抵村户，执手分袂。既而张氏举家惊喜，新妇诞一男。源累载放迹闽南，及还，省前事，复诣村户。见一童儿形貌类武者，乃呼曰：“武十三相识耶！”答曰：“李七健乎！”其后宪宗读国史，感叹李忄登、卢奕之事，有荐源名，遂以谏议大夫征。不起。明年，源卒于惠林寺。张终于宣州广德县令。 [87]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [87]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 今夕 - Means 'this night' or 'tonight.'
    • 托质于张氏为男子 - Literally means 'entrusted my body to the Zhang family as a man,' implying a reincarnation or transformation into a male member of the Zhang family.
    • 得明经 - Refers to passing the imperial examination in the Confucian classics.
    • 终邑令 - Means 'to end up as a county magistrate.'
    • 子之禄亦薄 - Means 'your salary is also meager.'
    • 年登八十 - Means 'to reach the age of eighty.'
    • 朝廷当以谏议大夫征 - Means 'the court will appoint you as a Censorate Advisor.'
    • 复与君相见 - Means 'to meet you again.'
    • 言讫 - Means 'after speaking.'
    • 抵村户 - Means 'arrived at the village gate.'
    • 执手分袂 - Means 'took hands and parted.'
    • 举家惊喜 - Means 'the whole family was surprised and delighted.'
    • 诞一男 - Means 'gave birth to a boy.'
    • 放迹闽南 - Means 'lived in exile in Minnan.'
    • 诣村户 - Means 'went to the village gate.'
    • 形貌类武者 - Means 'looked like the person named Wu.'
    • 李七健乎 - Means 'Is Li Qi well?'
    • 不起 - Means 'did not accept the position.'
    • 卒于惠林寺 - Means 'died at Huilin Temple.'
    • 终于宣州广德县令 - Means 'died as the magistrate of Guangde County, Xuanchou Prefecture.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 明经 - Refers to the imperial examination in the Confucian classics, which was a key pathway to officialdom in ancient China.
    • 谏议大夫 - A high-ranking official position in the Tang Dynasty, responsible for advising the emperor and participating in policy-making.
    • 宪宗 - Emperor Xianzong of Tang, who ruled from 805 to 820 CE.
    • 李忄登、卢奕 - Historical figures known for their loyalty and integrity during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 惠林寺 - A Buddhist temple, indicating the place of death for the subject.
    • 宣州广德县 - A specific location in ancient China, indicating the place of death for the subject.

    Proper Nouns

    • 张氏 - The Zhang family.
    • 武者 - The person named Wu.
    • 李七 - Li Qi.
    • 宪宗 - Emperor Xianzong of Tang.
    • 李忄登 - A historical figure known for his loyalty and integrity.
    • 卢奕 - A historical figure known for his loyalty and integrity.
    • 源 - The narrator's name.
    • 惠林寺 - A Buddhist temple.
    • 宣州广德县 - A specific location in ancient China.

    Tonight, I was reborn into the Zhang family as a man, and at fifteen, I passed the imperial examination in the Confucian classics, eventually becoming a county magistrate. It was also said, "Your salary will also be modest. When you reach eighty, the court will appoint you as a Censorate Advisor. After two more years, you will pass away. I will meet you again in seven years." After speaking, I arrived at the village gate, took hands, and parted ways. Soon after, the entire Zhang family was surprised and delighted, as the new wife gave birth to a son. For many years, I lived in exile in Minnan, and upon returning, I recalled the previous events and visited the village gate again. I saw a child whose appearance resembled Wu, and I called out, "Are you Wu the Thirteenth?" He replied, "Is Li Qi well?" Later, Emperor Xianzong read the national history and was moved by the stories of Li Deng and Lu Yi. Someone recommended my name, and I was appointed as a Censorate Advisor. I declined the position. The following year, I died at Huilin Temple. Zhang, on the other hand, died as the magistrate of Guangde County, Xuanchou Prefecture. [87]

    宝历二年，崔从镇淮南。五月三日，瓜步镇申浙右试竞渡船十艘，其三船平没于金山下，一百五十人俱溺死。从见申纸叹愤。时军司马皇甫曙入启事，与从同异之。座有宋生归儒者语曰：“彼之祸不及怪也，此亦有之，人数相类，但其死不同耳。”浃日，有大宴，陈于广场，百戏俱呈。俄暴风雨，庭前戏者并马数百匹，系在庑下，迅雷一震，马皆惊奔，大庑数十间平塌。凡居其下者俱压死。公令较其数，与浙右无一人差焉。
    　　管宁死辽东三十七年，归柩而阻海风，同行数十船俱没。惟宁船望见火光，投之，得岛屿。及上岸，无火亦无人。玄晏先生以为积善之感。 [88]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [88]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 平沒於金山下 - A poetic way of describing the sinking of ships under the Jade Mountain.
    • 一百五十人俱溺死 - Emphasizes the collective tragedy of 150 people all drowning.
    • 迅雷一震 - A sudden thunderclap, emphasizing the unexpectedness of the event.
    • 馬皆驚奔 - Describes the chaotic reaction of horses panicking and running.
    • 大庑數十間平塌 - A vivid description of the destruction of dozens of large buildings collapsing flatly.
    • 與浙右無一人差焉 - Confirms that there was not a single difference with the Zhejiang incident.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 寶歷二年 - A specific year in the reign of Emperor Xianzong of Tang (822–824).
    • 宋生歸儒者 - Song Sheng, a scholar, where '歸儒者' suggests a return to or adherence to Confucian learning.
    • 管寧 - A historical figure known for his integrity and virtue, often cited in moral teachings.
    • 遼東 - A region in northeastern China, historically significant for its isolation and cultural development.
    • 玄晏先生 - A title for a respected scholar or philosopher, often used to denote someone with deep moral or philosophical insight.

    Proper Nouns

    • 寶歷二年 - The specific year 824 in the reign of Emperor Xianzong of Tang.
    • 崔從 - Cui Cong, a Tang Dynasty official.
    • 瓜步鎮 - A specific town or district in Huainan.
    • 浙右 - The region west of Zhejiang.
    • 金山 - A specific mountain, likely referring to a landmark in the area.
    • 宋生歸儒者 - Song Sheng, a scholar.
    • 管寧 - A historical figure known for his integrity and virtue.
    • 遼東 - A region in northeastern China.
    • 玄晏先生 - A title for a respected scholar or philosopher.

    In the second year of Baoli (824), Cui Cong governed Huainan. On the third day of May, the Guabu town submitted a report about ten trial dragon boats from Zhejiang. Three of these boats sank flatly under the Jade Mountain, and all one hundred and fifty people aboard drowned. Cui Cong saw the report and sighed in anger. At the time, the military司马皇甫曙 entered to present a report, and he agreed or disagreed with Cui Cong. At the gathering, Song Sheng, a scholar, said, "The disaster of those people was not extraordinary; this also happened, and the numbers were similar, but their deaths were different." The next day, there was a grand feast held in the square, with all kinds of performances displayed. Suddenly, a violent storm struck, and hundreds of horses tied under the eaves of the hall panicked and ran. Dozens of large buildings collapsed flatly. All those beneath them were crushed to death. The official ordered the numbers to be compared, and there was not a single difference with the Zhejiang incident.

    Thirty-seven years after Guan Ning's death in Liaodong, his coffin was returning when it was blocked by sea winds, and dozens of ships traveling together all sank. Only Guan Ning's ship saw the firelight, and they threw themselves into it and landed on an island. Upon reaching the shore, there was no fire and no one there. Xuan Yan Master believed it was the result of accumulated virtue. [88]

    宋则家奴执弩弦断，误杀其子。则不之罪。
    　　陈子昂，蜀射洪人。十年居京师，不为人知。时东市有卖胡琴者，其价百万，日有豪贵传视，无辨者。子昂突出于众，谓左右：“可辇千缗市之。”众咸惊，问曰：“何用之”答曰：“余善此乐。”或有好事者曰：“可得一闻乎！”答曰：“余居宣阳里。”指其第处。”并具有酒，明日专候。不唯众君子荣顾，且各宜邀召闻名者齐赴，乃幸遇也。”来晨，集者凡百余人，皆当时重誉之士。子昂大张宴席，具珍羞。食毕，起捧胡琴，当前语曰：“蜀人陈子昂有文百轴，驰走京毂，碌碌尘土，不为人所知。此乐，贱工之役，岂愚留心哉！”遂举而弃之。舁文轴两案，遍赠会者。会既散，一日之内，声华溢都。时武攸宜为建安王，辟为记室。后拜拾遗。归觐，为段简所害。
    　　蜀将关羽善抚士卒而轻士大夫，张飞敬礼士大夫而轻卒伍，二将俱不得其中，亦不得其死。 [89]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [89]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 不之罪 - Archaic expression meaning 'did not punish him'; '之' is a pronoun referring back to the servant.
    • 不为人知 - Idiom meaning 'not known by anyone'; emphasizes the lack of recognition or fame.
    • 百万 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'a million'; indicates a very high price.
    • 突出于众 - Idiom meaning 'stood out among the crowd'; emphasizes bold action.
    • 辇千缗 - '辇' (辇) is a formal term for bringing something, and '千缗' (thousand strings of cash) is a unit of currency.
    • 善此乐 - '善' (good at) indicates proficiency in playing the lute.
    • 可得一闻乎 - '可得' (can get) is a polite way of asking for permission to hear the lute.
    • 大张宴席 - Describes holding a grand banquet.
    • 具珍羞 - Describes preparing precious delicacies.
    • 举而弃之 - '举' (throw) is a strong verb indicating the action of throwing away the lute.
    • 舁文轴两案 - '舁' (carry) and '遍赠' (distribute to all) describe the action of distributing the scrolls.
    • 声华溢都 - '声华' (reputation) and '溢都' (spread throughout the capital) describe the impact of the event.
    • 辟为记室 - '辟' (appoint) and '记室' (secretary) describe the official position.
    • 归觐，为段简所害 - '归觐' (return to visit) and '为...所害' (be harmed by) describe the sequence of events.
    • 不得其中 - Means 'did not find the middle ground'; describes the inability to balance contrasting behaviors.
    • 不得其死 - Means 'did not die a good death'; describes the unfortunate outcome of their actions.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 宋则 - Refers to Song Ze, a historical figure from the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 陈子昂 - Refers to Chen Zi'ang, a famous poet and scholar from the Tang Dynasty.
    • 蜀射洪 - Refers to Shehong, a county in Sichuan Province, where Chen Zi'ang was born.
    • 武攸宜 - Refers to Wu Youyi, a military general and politician during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 建安王 - Refers to the title of a nobleman during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 段简 - Refers to Duan Jian, a historical figure who harmed Chen Zi'ang.
    • 关羽 - Refers to Guan Yu, a famous general from the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 张飞 - Refers to Zhang Fei, another famous general from the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 三国有名将 - Refers to the Three Kingdoms period, a historical era in Chinese history.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The structure '不之罪' is an archaic way of expressing 'did not punish him', where '之' is a pronoun referring back to the servant.
    • The idiom '不为人知' emphasizes the lack of recognition or fame.
    • '百万' (million) is a hyperbolic expression indicating a very high price.
    • The idiom '突出于众' emphasizes Chen Zi'ang's bold action.
    • '辇' (辇) is a formal term for bringing something, and '千缗' (thousand strings of cash) is a unit of currency.
    • '善' (good at) indicates Chen Zi'ang's proficiency in playing the lute.
    • '可得' (can get) is a polite way of asking for permission to hear the lute.
    • '大张宴席' (held a grand banquet) and '具珍羞' (prepared precious delicacies) describe the lavishness of the event.
    • '举' (throw) is a strong verb indicating the action of throwing away the lute.
    • '舁' (carry) and '遍赠' (distribute to all) describe the action of distributing the scrolls.
    • '声华' (reputation) and '溢都' (spread throughout the capital) describe the impact of the event.
    • '辟' (appoint) and '记室' (secretary) describe the official position.
    • '归觐' (return to visit) and '为...所害' (be harmed by) describe the sequence of events.
    • '不得其中' (did not find the middle ground) and '不得其死' (did not die a good death) describe the consequences of their actions.

    Song Ze's servant, while handling a crossbow, accidentally broke the string and mistakenly killed his own son. Song Ze did not punish him.

    Chen Zi'ang, from Shehong in Shu, lived in the capital for ten years without gaining recognition. At that time, there was a merchant selling a lute in the eastern market, priced at a million coins. Each day, the wealthy and influential would come to view it, but none could discern its value. Chen Zi'ang stood out among the crowd and said to those around him, "Bring a thousand strings of cash to buy it." Everyone was startled and asked, "What will you use it for?" He replied, "I am skilled in this music." Someone who was fond of such things asked, "Can I hear it once?" He answered, "I live in Xuan Yang Li." He pointed to his residence. They prepared wine and waited exclusively the next day. Not only would the distinguished gentlemen be honored by their presence, but each should also invite and summon well-known people to join, which would be a fortunate encounter. The next morning, over a hundred people gathered, all of whom were highly respected scholars of the time. Chen Zi'ang held a grand banquet and prepared precious delicacies. After the meal, he stood up, holding the lute, and said to those present, "Chen Zi'ang of Shu has a hundred scrolls of writing, but he has been running around in the capital, mired in dust, and has not been recognized by anyone. This music, a task for lowly craftsmen, is not something I would pay attention to!" He then threw the lute away. He carried two cases of scrolls and distributed them to all attendees. After the gathering dispersed, within a single day, his reputation spread throughout the capital. At that time, Wu Youyi was the Marquis of Jian'an, and he appointed Chen Zi'ang as a secretary. Later, Chen Zi'ang was promoted to the position of Censor. When he returned to visit his family, he was harmed by Duan Jian.

    General Guan Yu of Shu was good at treating his soldiers but looked down on scholars, while General Zhang Fei respected scholars but looked down on soldiers. Both generals did not find the middle ground and did not die a good death. [89]

    周世，东海之上，有勇士甾丘，以勇闻于天下。过神泉，令饮马。其仆曰：“饮马于此者，马必死。”丘曰：“以丘之言，饮之。”其马果死。丘乃去衣拔剑而入。三日三夜，杀二蛟一龙而出。雷神随而击之，十日十夜，眇其左目。要离闻而往见之。丘出送有丧者，要离往见丘于墓所，曰：“雷神击子，十日十夜，眇子左目。夫天怨不旋日，人怨不旋踵。子至今弗报，何也？”叱之而去。墓上振愤者不可胜数。要离归，谓人曰：“甾丘，天下勇士也。今日我辱之于众人之中，必来杀我。暮无闭门，寝无闭户。”丘至夜半果来。拔剑柱颈曰：“子有死罪三：辱我于众人之中，死罪一也；暮无闭门，死罪二也；寝不闭户，死罪三也。”要离曰：“子待我一言而后杀也。子来不谒，一不肖也；拔剑不刺，二不肖也；刃先词后，三不肖也。子能杀我者，是毒药之死耳。”丘收剑而去，曰：“嘻！天下所不若者，唯此子耳。” [90]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [90]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 以勇闻于天下 - Famous for his bravery throughout the world.
    • 雷神随而击之 - The thunder god followed and struck him.
    • 天怨不旋日，人怨不旋踵 - Heaven's wrath does not turn in a single day, human resentment does not turn in a single step, indicating swift retribution.
    • 辱之于众人之中 - Insulted him in front of many people.
    • 拔剑柱颈 - Pulled out his sword and held it against his neck.
    • 死罪三 - Three capital offenses.
    • 子能杀我者，是毒药之死耳 - If you can kill me, it would be like dying from poison.
    • 天下所不若者 - No one in the world can surpass him.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 周世 - The Zhou Dynasty, a major period in Chinese history.
    • 东海之上 - The eastern sea, which could be the East China Sea or the Yellow Sea.
    • 甾丘 - Zi Qiu, a historical figure known for his bravery.
    • 要离 - Yao Li, a historical figure known for his bravery and loyalty.
    • 雷神 - The thunder god, a figure in Chinese mythology.
    • 龙 - The dragon, a mythical creature in Chinese culture.
    • 蛟 - A type of mythical creature, often described as a water dragon or serpent.
    • 眇其左目 - Blinded his left eye.
    • 要离闻而往见之 - Yao Li heard about it and went to see him.
    • 丘出送有丧者 - Zi Qiu went out to escort someone who had lost a relative.
    • 丘收剑而去 - Zi Qiu sheathed his sword and left.
    • 嘻 - An interjection expressing surprise or admiration.

    Proper Nouns

    • 周世 - The Zhou Dynasty.
    • 东海之上 - The eastern sea.
    • 甾丘 - Zi Qiu.
    • 要离 - Yao Li.
    • 雷神 - The thunder god.
    • 龙 - The dragon.
    • 蛟 - The mythical creature.
    • 眇其左目 - Blinded his left eye.
    • 要离闻而往见之 - Yao Li heard about it and went to see him.
    • 丘出送有丧者 - Zi Qiu went out to escort someone who had lost a relative.
    • 丘收剑而去 - Zi Qiu sheathed his sword and left.
    • 嘻 - An interjection expressing surprise or admiration.

    In the Zhou Dynasty, on the eastern sea, there was a brave warrior named Zi Qiu, who was famous for his bravery throughout the world. Passing by a sacred spring, he ordered his horse to drink from it. His servant said, "If a horse drinks from here, it will surely die." Zi Qiu replied, "According to my words, let it drink." Indeed, his horse died. Zi Qiu then stripped off his clothes and drew his sword, entering the spring. For three days and three nights, he killed two dragons and one serpent before emerging. The thunder god followed and struck him, blinding his left eye over ten days and nights. Yao Li heard about this and went to see him. Zi Qiu was escorting someone who had lost a relative, and Yao Li visited him at the burial site, saying, "The thunder god struck you, for ten days and nights, blinding your left eye. Heaven's wrath does not turn in a single day, human resentment does not turn in a single step. Why have you not avenged yourself until now?" He scolded him and left. There were countless people on the grave who were stirred up with anger. Yao Li returned and told others, "Zi Qiu is a brave warrior in the world. Today, I insulted him in front of many people, so he will surely come to kill me. Do not close the door at dusk, and do not lock the door at night." At midnight, Zi Qiu indeed came. He drew his sword and held it against his neck, saying, "You have three capital offenses: insulting me in front of many people, the first; not closing the door at dusk, the second; not locking the door at night, the third." Yao Li said, "You wait for me to say something before killing me. You did not greet me when you came, the first unbecoming act; you did not stab me with your sword, the second unbecoming act; you put the blade before the words, the third unbecoming act. If you can kill me, it would be like dying from poison." Zi Qiu sheathed his sword and left, saying, "Ah! No one in the world can surpass this person." [90]

    晋桓石虔有材干，し捷绝伦。随父豁在荆州，于猎围中，见猛虎被数箭而伏。诸督将素知其勇，戏令拔箭。石虔因急往，拔一箭，猛虎踞跃，石虔亦跳，高于猛兽。复拔一箭而归。时人有患疾者，谓曰：“桓石虔来。”以怖之，病者多愈。
    　　北齐将彭乐勇猛无双。时神武率乐等十余万人，于沙苑与字文护战。时乐饮酒，乘醉深入。被刺，肝肚俱出，内之不尽，截去之。复入战。护兵遂败，相枕籍死者三万余人。
    　　唐朝承周隋离乱，乐悬散失，独无征音。国姓所阙，知者不敢言达其事。天后末，御史大夫李嗣真密求之不得，一旦秋爽，闻砧声者在今弩营，是当时英公宅。又数年，无由得之。其后徐敬业反，天后潴其宫。嗣真乃求得丧车一镡，入振之于东南隅，果有应者。遂掘之，得石一段。裁为四具，补乐悬之阙。后享宗庙郊天，挂иね者，乃嗣真所得也。 [91]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [91]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • し捷绝伦 - Compound adjective meaning 'swift and agile beyond comparison,' emphasizing exceptional speed and agility.
    • 拔箭 - Literally 'pull out arrows,' but in this context, it refers to removing arrows from a tiger.
    • 踞跃 - Compound verb meaning 'to sit upright and leap,' describing the tiger's sudden movement.
    • 被数箭而伏 - Literally 'covered with several arrows and lying down,' but it means the tiger was lying still after being shot.
    • 内之不尽 - Literally 'not able to put it all back in,' referring to the difficulty of putting the liver and intestines back inside the body.
    • 截去之 - Literally 'cut it off,' but it means cutting off the protruding organs.
    • 復入战 - Literally 'returned to battle,' but it means rejoining the fight.
    • 相枕籍死者三万余人 - Literally 'lying on top of each other dead over 30,000 people,' but it means that over 30,000 people were killed and their bodies were piled up.
    • 独无征音 - Literally 'only lacking the note of Zheng,' but it means the music system was missing the note of Zheng.
    • 国姓所阙 - Literally 'the national surname is lacking,' but it means the surname of the ruling family is missing from the music system.
    • 知者不敢言达其事 - Literally 'those who know do not dare to speak of it,' but it means that knowledgeable people were afraid to discuss the issue.
    • 徐敬业反 - Literally 'Xu Jingye rebelled,' but it means Xu Jingye staged a rebellion.
    • 天后潴其宫 - Literally 'Empress Dowager flooded his palace,' but it means Empress Dowager Wu Zetian destroyed Xu Jingye's palace.
    • 裁为四具 - Literally 'cut into four parts,' but it means the stone was divided into four pieces.
    • 补乐悬之阙 - Literally 'to fill the gaps in the music system,' but it means to complete the music system.
    • 享宗庙郊天 - Literally 'worship at the ancestral temple and the sky,' but it means performing religious ceremonies.
    • 挂いね者 - Literally 'those who hang the i-ne,' but it means the people who play the i-ne instrument.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋桓石虔 - Huan Shiqian, a historical figure known for his bravery and agility.
    • 北齐将彭乐 - Le, a historical figure known for his extraordinary bravery.
    • 神武 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu of Northern Qi, a famous ruler.
    • 宇文护 - Yuwen Hu, a historical figure who was a general and regent during the Northern Zhou dynasty.
    • 唐朝 - The Tang Dynasty, a significant period in Chinese history.
    • 周隋离乱 - Refers to the period of chaos following the fall of the Northern Zhou and Sui dynasties.
    • 乐悬 - Refers to the ancient Chinese music system.
    • 征音 - One of the seven tones in the ancient Chinese music scale.
    • 天后 - Refers to Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
    • 李嗣真 - Li Sizhen, a historical figure known for his contributions to music and literature.
    • 徐敬业 - Xu Jingye, a historical figure who staged a rebellion against the Tang Dynasty.
    • 弩营 - Refers to the camp where archers and crossbowmen were stationed.
    • 英公宅 - Refers to the residence of a nobleman named Ying Gong.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋桓石虔 - Huan Shiqian, a historical figure.
    • 北齐将彭乐 - Le, a historical figure.
    • 神武 - Emperor Gaozu of Northern Qi.
    • 宇文护 - Yuwen Hu, a historical figure.
    • 唐朝 - Tang Dynasty.
    • 周隋离乱 - Period of chaos following the fall of the Northern Zhou and Sui dynasties.
    • 乐悬 - Ancient Chinese music system.
    • 征音 - One of the seven tones in the ancient Chinese music scale.
    • 天后 - Empress Wu Zetian.
    • 李嗣真 - Li Sizhen, a historical figure.
    • 徐敬业 - Xu Jingye, a historical figure.
    • 弩营 - Archer's camp.
    • 英公宅 - Residence of Ying Gong.

    晋桓石虔有材干，し捷绝伦。随父豁在荆州，于猎围中，见猛虎被数箭而伏。诸督将素知其勇，戏令拔箭。石虔因急往，拔一箭，猛虎踞跃，石虔亦跳，高于猛兽。复拔一箭而归。时人有患疾者，谓曰：“桓石虔来。”以怖之，病者多愈。

    Huan Shiqian of Jin was exceptionally skilled and agile beyond compare. Following his father, Hua, in Jingzhou, he once saw a fierce tiger lying down, covered with several arrows. The various commanders, knowing of his bravery, playfully ordered him to pull out the arrows. Huan Shiqian hurriedly went forward, pulled out one arrow, and as the tiger leaped up, he also jumped, higher than the beast itself. He then pulled out another arrow and returned. At that time, there was a person suffering from an illness who was told, "Huan Shiqian is coming." Frightened by this, many of the sick people recovered.

    北齐将彭乐勇猛无双。时神武率乐等十余万人，于沙苑与字文护战。时乐饮酒，乘醉深入。被刺，肝肚俱出，内之不尽，截去之。复入战。护兵遂败，相枕籍死者三万余人。

    Le of Northern Qi was unmatched in his bravery. At that time, Emperor Gaozu led Le and over ten thousand men to fight against Yuwen Hu at Shayan. Le was drinking wine and, in a drunken state, advanced deep into the enemy lines. He was stabbed, and his liver and intestines came out; he couldn't put them all back in, so he had to cut them off. He then returned to battle. Yuwen Hu's troops were defeated, and over 30,000 people died, their bodies piled up.

    唐朝承周隋离乱，乐悬散失，独无征音。国姓所阙，知者不敢言达其事。天后末，御史大夫李嗣真密求之不得，一旦秋爽，闻砧声者在今弩营，是当时英公宅。又数年，无由得之。其后徐敬业反，天后潴其宫。嗣真乃求得丧车一镡，入振之于东南隅，果有应者。遂掘之，得石一段。裁为四具，补乐悬之阙。后享宗庙郊天，挂иね者，乃嗣真所得也。

    During the Tang Dynasty, following the chaos of the late Zhou and Sui dynasties, the music system (乐悬) was scattered and lost, and only the note of Zheng was missing. The surname of the ruling family was also absent from the music system, and those who knew about it dared not speak of it. At the end of Empress Wu's reign, the Grand Censor Li Sizhen secretly searched for it but could not find it. One crisp autumn day, the sound of a mallet striking was heard in the current archer's camp, which was the residence of Ying Gong at the time. After several more years, there was no way to obtain it. Later, Xu Jingye rebelled, and Empress Wu destroyed his palace. Li Sizhen then found a part of a funeral carriage and inserted it into the southeast corner, and果然有回应的. They then dug and found a piece of stone. It was cut into four parts and used to complete the music system. Later, during the worship at the ancestral temple and the sky, those who played the i-ne instrument were those obtained by Li Sizhen. [91]

    鲍瑗家多丧及病，淳于智为筮之。卦成云：“宜入市门数十步，有一人持荆马鞭，便就买取，悬东北桑树上。无病，三年当得财。”如其言。后穿井得钱及铜器二十万。
    　　唐李佐，山东名族。少时因安史之乱，失其父。后佐进士擢第，有令名，官为京兆少尹。阴求其父。有识者告后，往迎之于鬻凶器家。归而奉养，如是累月。一旦，父召佐，谓曰：“汝孝行绝世。然吾三十年在此党中，昨从汝来，未与流辈谢绝。汝可具大猪五头，白醪数斛，蒜齑数瓮，薄饼十拌，开设中堂，吾与群党一守申款，则无恨矣。”佐恭承其教，数日乃具。父散召两市善薤歌者百人至。初则列坐堂中，久乃杂讴，及暮皆醉。众扶佐父登榻，而薤歌一声，凡百齐和。俄然相扶父出，不知所在。行路观者亿万。明日，佐弃家人入山，数日而卒。 [92]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [92]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 为筮之 - Divination for someone.
    • 卦成云 - The divination is complete; says.
    • 宜入市门数十步 - Should enter the city gate a few dozen steps.
    • 悬东北桑树上 - Hang on a mulberry tree in the northeast.
    • 无病，三年当得财 - No illness, will gain wealth in three years.
    • 擢第 - Pass the imperial examination with distinction.
    • 阴求其父 - Secretly search for his father.
    • 鬻凶器家 - Households that sell funerary items.
    • 如是累月 - Like this for several months.
    • 行路观者亿万 - Millions of passersby and spectators.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 淳于智 - Historical figure known for divination skills.
    • 安史之乱 - The An Lushan Rebellion (755-763 AD), a major uprising during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 山东名族 - Refers to the aristocratic families of the Shandong region, known for their prominence and influence.
    • 京兆少尹 - An official title in the Tang Dynasty, equivalent to a deputy governor of the capital region.

    Proper Nouns

    • 鲍瑗 - Historical figure.
    • 淳于智 - Historical figure known for divination.
    • 李佐 - Historical figure.
    • 安史之乱 - The An Lushan Rebellion.
    • 山东名族 - Refers to the aristocratic families of the Shandong region.
    • 京兆少尹 - An official title in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 鬻凶器家 - Refers to households that sell funerary items.

    The Bao family had many funerals and illnesses, so Chunyu Zhi performed divination for them. The divination was complete, and he said: "You should go to the city gate and take a few dozen steps forward. There will be a person holding a willow branch and a horsewhip. You should buy it from them and hang it on a mulberry tree in the northeast. You will not fall ill, and in three years, you will gain wealth." As predicted, later when they dug a well, they found twenty thousand coins and copper vessels.

    During the Tang Dynasty, there was Li Zuo, a member of a prominent family from the Shandong region. In his youth, during the An Lushan Rebellion, he lost his father. Later, Li Zuo passed the imperial examination with distinction, gained a good reputation, and became the Deputy Governor of the capital region. He secretly searched for his father. An informed person told him later, and he went to the household that sold funerary items to welcome his father back. Upon returning, he took care of his father for several months. One day, his father called Li Zuo and said, "Your filial piety is unparalleled. However, I have been in this group for thirty years. Yesterday, I came with you, but I have not yet bid farewell to my peers. You should prepare five large pigs, several dou of white wine, several jars of garlic paste, and ten batches of thin pancakes. Set up the main hall, and I will express my feelings with the group for a moment, and then we will have no regrets." Li Zuo followed his father's instructions and prepared everything over several days. His father then summoned a hundred skilled wailing singers from the two markets. At first, they sat in the main hall, and after a while, they began to sing various songs. By evening, everyone was drunk. The crowd helped Li Zuo's father up onto a couch, and upon hearing a single wail, all a hundred joined in. Suddenly, they helped his father out, and he disappeared. Passersby and spectators numbered in the millions. The next day, Li Zuo left his family and went into the mountains, where he died a few days later. [92]

    唐长庆、太和中，王初、王哲，俱中科名。其父仲舒显于时。二子初宦，不为秘书省官，以家讳故也。既而私相议曰：“若遵典礼避私讳，而吾昆弟不得为中书舍人、中书侍郎、列部尚书。乃相与改讳，只言仲字可矣。”又为宣武军掌书记。识者曰：“二子逆天忤神，不永。”未几相次殒谢。
    　　至德元年，史思明未平，卫州有妇人侯四娘等三人，刺血谒于军前，愿入义营讨贼。
    　　王凝之妻谢道韫。王献之与客谈义不胜，道韫遣婢白曰：“请与小郎解围。”乃施青绫步障自蔽，与客谈⊥不能屈。
    　　北齐侍御史李广，博览群书，修史。夜梦一人曰：“我心神也。君役我太苦，辞去。”俄而广疾卒。 [93]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [93]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 俱中科名 - Means 'both passed the imperial examinations,' indicating success in the civil service exams.
    • 其父仲舒顯于時 - Means 'their father, Zhongshu, was prominent in his time,' highlighting his status and influence.
    • 二子初宦，不為秘书省官，以家讳故也 - Means 'the two sons, when they first entered officialdom, did not serve in the Secretariat because of a family taboo.'
