    • 若遵典礼避私讳，而吾昆弟不得为中书舍人、中书侍郎、列部尚书 - Means 'if we follow the ritual to avoid private taboos, then my brothers and I cannot become Secretariat Attendants, Secretariat Attendants-in-Waiting, or Secretaries of the Six Ministries.'
    • 乃相与改讳，只言仲字可矣 - Means 'so we agreed to change the taboo, only using the character 'Zhong' is acceptable.'
    • 又为宣武军掌书记 - Means 'he also served as the Chief Clerk of the Xuanwu Army.'
    • 识者曰：“二子逆天忤神，不永。” - Means 'those who understood said, 'These two sons are defying heaven and offending the spirits, they will not live long.''
    • 未几相次殒谢 - Means 'not long after, they died one after another.'
    • 至德元年，史思明未平，卫州有妇人侯四娘等三人，刺血谒于军前，愿入义营讨贼 - Means 'In the first year of the Tian De era, before Shi Siming was defeated, three women from Weizhou, including Hou Siniang, cut their blood to offer to the army, volunteering to join the righteous camp to fight the rebels.'
    • 王献之与客谈义不胜，道韫遣婢白曰：“请与小郎解围。”乃施青绫步障自蔽，与客谈，不能屈 - Means 'When Wang Xizhi was discussing principles with guests and could not win the argument, Xie Daoyun sent a maid to say, 'Please let me help my younger brother out of this.' She then set up an embroidered screen to shield herself and continued the discussion, but could not be persuaded.'
    • 夜梦一人曰：“我心神也。君役我太苦，辞去。”俄而广疾卒 - Means 'One night, he dreamed of a person who said, 'I am a spirit. You have overworked me, so I am leaving.' Soon after, Li Guang died of illness.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 唐长庆、太和中 - Specific years in the Tang Dynasty, highlighting the political and social context.
    • 中书舍人、中书侍郎、列部尚书 - High-ranking positions in the Tang Dynasty bureaucracy, emphasizing the importance of these roles.
    • 家讳 - A cultural practice where certain names or characters are avoided due to their association with deceased family members.
    • 至德元年 - The first year of the Tian De era, a period of significant political turmoil.
    • 史思明 - A powerful rebel leader during the An Lushan Rebellion.
    • 宣武军 - A military unit in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 王凝之妻谢道韫 - Xie Daoyun, known for her intelligence and literary talent.
    • 王献之 - Wang Xizhi, a renowned calligrapher and scholar.
    • 北齐 - A dynasty that ruled northern China from 550 to 577.
    • 侍御史 - A high-ranking official responsible for judicial and administrative duties.

    Proper Nouns

    • 唐长庆、太和中 - Specific years in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 中书舍人、中书侍郎、列部尚书 - High-ranking positions in the Tang Dynasty bureaucracy.
    • 至德元年 - The first year of the Tian De era.
    • 史思明 - A powerful rebel leader.
    • 宣武军 - A military unit.
    • 王凝之妻谢道韫 - Xie Daoyun, a famous poet and calligrapher.
    • 王献之 - Wang Xizhi, a renowned calligrapher and scholar.
    • 北齐 - A dynasty that ruled northern China.
    • 侍御史 - A high-ranking official.
    • 李广 - A censor of the Northern Qi Dynasty.

    During the years of the Tang Dynasty, specifically during the reigns of Emperor Muzong (821-824) and Emperor Wenzong (827-840), Wang Chu and Wang Zhe both passed the imperial examinations. Their father, Zhongshu, was prominent in his time. When the two sons first entered officialdom, they did not serve in the Secretariat due to a family taboo. Later, they privately discussed, saying, "If we follow the ritual to avoid private taboos, then my brothers and I cannot become Secretariat Attendants, Secretariat Attendants-in-Waiting, or Secretaries of the Six Ministries." So they agreed to change the taboo, only using the character 'Zhong' is acceptable. He also served as the Chief Clerk of the Xuanwu Army. Those who understood said, "These two sons are defying heaven and offending the spirits, they will not live long." Not long after, they died one after another.

    In the first year of the Tian De era, before Shi Siming was defeated, three women from Weizhou, including Hou Siniang, cut their blood to offer to the army, volunteering to join the righteous camp to fight the rebels.

    Xie Daoyun, the wife of Wang Ningzhi, was a famous poet and calligrapher. When Wang Xizhi was discussing principles with guests and could not win the argument, Xie Daoyun sent a maid to say, "Please let me help my younger brother out of this." She then set up an embroidered screen to shield herself and continued the discussion, but could not be persuaded.

    Li Guang, a censor of the Northern Qi Dynasty, was well-versed in many books and compiled historical records. One night, he dreamed of a person who said, "I am a spirit. You have overworked me, so I am leaving." Soon after, Li Guang died of illness. [93]

    隋文帝未贵时，常舟行江中。夜泊中，梦无左手。及觉，甚恶之。及登岸，诣一草庵。中有一老僧，道极高。具以梦告之。僧起贺曰：“无左手者，独拳也。当为天子。”后帝兴建此庵为吉祥寺。居武昌下三十里。
    　　贞元中，吴郡进士李赤者，与赵敏之相同游闽。行及衢之信安，去县三十里，宿于馆厅。宵分，忽有一妇人入庭中。赤于睡中蹶起下阶，与之揖让。良久即上厅，开箧取纸笔，作一书与其亲，云：“某为郭氏所选为婿。”词旨重叠，讫，乃封于箧中。复下庭，妇人抽其巾缢之。敏之走出大叫，妇人乃收巾而走。及视其书，如赤梦中所为。明日，又偕行。南次建中驿，白昼又失赤。敏之即遽往厕，见赤坐于床，大怒敏之曰：“方当礼谢，为尔所惊。”浃日至闽，属寮有与赤游旧者，设宴饮次，又失赤。敏之疾索于厕，见赤僵仆于地，气已绝矣。 [94]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [94]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 常舟行江中 - Habitual activity of traveling by boat in the river.
    • 夜泊中 - Stopping during the night while traveling.
    • 夢無左手 - Dreaming of having no left hand, often interpreted as a sign of loss or change.
    • 覺，甚惡之 - Woke up and found it very ominous, indicating a negative reaction to the dream.
    • 登岸，诣一草庵 - Landing and going to a thatched hut, describing the action of leaving the boat and visiting a place.
    • 中有一老僧，道极高 - There was an old monk inside, whose Dao was very high, indicating a highly respected spiritual figure.
    • 具以梦告之 - Told him all about the dream, indicating a full disclosure.
    • 僧起贺曰 - The monk stood up and congratulated, indicating a positive response to the dream.
    • 无左手者，独拳也 - Having no left hand means being alone with a fist, a metaphorical interpretation of the dream.
    • 当为天子 - Will become an emperor, indicating a prediction of high status.
    • 兴建此庵为吉祥寺 - Built this hut into the Temple of Auspiciousness, indicating the transformation of a place.
    • 贞元中 - In the Zhengyuan period, referring to a specific era.
    • 吴郡进士李赤者 - The Jinshi candidate Li Chi from Wu County, indicating a scholar.
    • 与赵敏之相同游闽 - Traveled to Fujian together with Zhao Minzhi, indicating a shared journey.
    • 行及衢之信安 - Traveled to Xian'an in Qu County, indicating a specific location.
    • 去县三十里 - Thirty miles from the county, indicating distance.
    • 宿于馆厅 - Stayed in the hall, indicating a place of lodging.
    • 宵分 - Midnight, indicating a specific time.
    • 忽有一妇人入庭中 - Suddenly a woman entered the courtyard, indicating an unexpected event.
    • 赤于睡中蹶起下阶 - Li Chi woke up and jumped down the stairs, indicating a sudden action.
    • 与之揖让 - Bowed to her, indicating a formal greeting.
    • 良久即上厅 - After a long time, he went upstairs, indicating a delay.
    • 开箧取纸笔 - Opened the chest and took out paper and ink, indicating preparation.
    • 作一书与其亲 - Wrote a letter to his relatives, indicating a communication.
    • 词旨重叠 - The words and meanings were complex, indicating a detailed and layered message.
    • 讫，乃封于箧中 - Finished, then sealed it in the chest, indicating completion and storage.
    • 复下庭，妇人抽其巾缢之 - Returned to the courtyard, the woman tied the scarf around his neck, indicating a violent act.
    • 敏之走出大叫 - Zhao Minzhi ran out and shouted, indicating a reaction.
    • 妇人乃收巾而走 - The woman then took the scarf and ran away, indicating escape.
    • 及视其书 - When they saw the letter, indicating discovery.
    • 如赤梦中所为 - As Li Chi had done in his dream, indicating a connection to the dream.
    • 明日，又偕行 - The next day, they traveled together again, indicating continuation.
    • 南次建中驿 - Southeast to the Jianzhong Post, indicating a specific location.
    • 白昼又失赤 - During the day, Li Chi disappeared again, indicating another disappearance.
    • 敏之即遽往厕 - Zhao Minzhi immediately went to the toilet, indicating a hasty action.
    • 见赤坐于床 - Saw Li Chi sitting on the bed, indicating a surprising discovery.
    • 大怒敏之曰 - Angrily said to Zhao Minzhi, indicating a confrontation.
    • 方当礼谢 - Was about to show respect, indicating a planned action.
    • 为尔所惊 - Interrupted by you, indicating a disruption.
    • 浃日至闽 - Arrived in Fujian after a few days, indicating a journey.
    • 属寮有与赤游旧者 - Among the officials, there were those who had known Li Chi before, indicating acquaintances.
    • 设宴饮次 - During a banquet, indicating a social event.
    • 又失赤 - Li Chi disappeared again, indicating another disappearance.
    • 敏之疾索于厕 - Zhao Minzhi quickly searched the toilet, indicating a search.
    • 见赤僵仆于地，气已绝矣 - Saw Li Chi lying motionless on the ground, his breath already gone, indicating a fatal outcome.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 隋文帝 - Emperor Wen of Sui, the founder of the Sui Dynasty.
    • 草庵 - A simple, thatched hut, often associated with Buddhist monks or hermits.
    • 老僧，道极高 - A highly respected Buddhist monk.
    • 贞元 - The Zhengyuan period of the Tang Dynasty, a specific era.
    • 吴郡 - Wu County, a region in present-day Jiangsu Province.
    • 进士 - A scholar who has passed the imperial examinations.
    • 闽 - Fujian Province.
    • 衢之信安 - Xian'an in Qu County, a specific location.
    • 建中驿 - The Jianzhong Post, a specific location.
    • 属寮 - Officials or subordinates.
    • 与赤游旧者 - People who had known Li Chi before.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The sequence '隋文帝未贵时，常舟行江中。夜泊中，梦无左手。及觉，甚恶之。' must be read as a series of events: the emperor's habitual boat travel, his night stop, his dream, and his reaction.
    • The sequence '登岸，诣一草庵。中有一老僧，道极高。具以梦告之。僧起贺曰：“无左手者，独拳也。当为天子。”' must be read as a series of actions: landing, visiting a hut, meeting a monk, telling the dream, and receiving a prediction.
    • The sequence '兴建此庵为吉祥寺。居武昌下三十里。' must be read as a transformation and a location description.
    • The sequence '贞元中，吴郡进士李赤者，与赵敏之相同游闽。行及衢之信安，去县三十里，宿于馆厅。' must be read as a series of events: the time period, the scholars' journey, their stop, and their lodging.
    • The sequence '宵分，忽有一妇人入庭中。赤于睡中蹶起下阶，与之揖让。良久即上厅，开箧取纸笔，作一书与其亲，词旨重叠，讫，乃封于箧中。复下庭，妇人抽其巾缢之。敏之走出大叫，妇人乃收巾而走。及视其书，如赤梦中所为。' must be read as a series of events: the unexpected visit, the scholar's actions, the writing of the letter, the violent act, and the discovery.
    • The sequence '明日，又偕行。南次建中驿，白昼又失赤。敏之即遽往厕，见赤坐于床，大怒敏之曰：“方当礼谢，为尔所惊。”浃日至闽，属寮有与赤游旧者，设宴饮次，又失赤。敏之疾索于厕，见赤僵仆于地，气已绝矣。' must be read as a series of events: the continuation of the journey, another disappearance, the search, and the fatal outcome.

    Proper Nouns

    • 隋文帝 - Emperor Wen of Sui.
    • 草庵 - A thatched hut.
    • 老僧 - An old monk.
    • 贞元 - The Zhengyuan period.
    • 吴郡 - Wu County.
    • 进士 - A scholar who has passed the imperial examinations.
    • 闽 - Fujian Province.
    • 衢之信安 - Xian'an in Qu County.
    • 建中驿 - The Jianzhong Post.
    • 属寮 - Officials or subordinates.
    • 与赤游旧者 - People who had known Li Chi before.

    Emperor Wen of Sui, before he rose to power, often traveled by boat in the river. One night, as he anchored in the river, he dreamed of having no left hand. Upon waking, he found it very ominous. After landing, he went to a thatched hut. Inside, there was an old monk whose Dao was very high. Li Chi told him all about the dream. The monk stood up and congratulated, saying: "Having no left hand means being alone with a fist. You will become an emperor." Later, the emperor built this hut into the Temple of Auspiciousness, located thirty miles below Wuchang.

    In the Zhengyuan period, Jinshi candidate Li Chi from Wu County traveled to Fujian together with Zhao Minzhi. They traveled to Xian'an in Qu County, thirty miles from the county, and stayed in the hall. At midnight, suddenly a woman entered the courtyard. Li Chi woke up and jumped down the stairs, bowed to her. After a long time, he went upstairs, opened the chest and took out paper and ink, wrote a letter to his relatives, the words and meanings were complex, finished, then sealed it in the chest. Returned to the courtyard, the woman tied the scarf around his neck. Zhao Minzhi ran out and shouted, the woman then took the scarf and ran away. When they saw the letter, it was as Li Chi had done in his dream. The next day, they traveled together again, southeast to the Jianzhong Post, during the day, Li Chi disappeared again. Zhao Minzhi immediately went to the toilet, saw Li Chi sitting on the bed, angrily said to Zhao Minzhi: "Was about to show respect, interrupted by you." Arrived in Fujian after a few days, among the officials, there were those who had known Li Chi before, during a banquet, Li Chi disappeared again. Zhao Minzhi quickly searched the toilet, saw Li Chi lying motionless on the ground, his breath already gone. [94]

    唐大历元年，周智光为华州刺史，劫剥行侣，旋欲谋反。遣吏邵进，潜往京，伺朝廷御伐之意。进归，告曰：“朝廷无疑公之心。”光怒，以其叶朝廷而绐于己，遽命斩之。既而甚悔，速遣送其首付妻儿。妻即以针纫颈。俄顷复活，以药傅之。然犹惧智光。使人告光曰：“进本蒲人，今欲归葬。”光亦赙之。既至蒲，浃旬，其疮平愈。乃改姓他游。后三十年，崔为宋州牧，晨衙，有一人投刺，曰：“敕吏。”召见，讯其由。进曰：“明公昔为周智光从事。”因叙其本末。乃省悟，与缣帛。揖之而去。 [95]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [95]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 劫剥行侣 - Robbing and stripping travelers, a term that combines robbery and extortion.
    • 旋欲谋反 - Intending to rebel, where '旋' suggests a sudden or recent intention.
    • 潜往京 - Secretly going to the capital, indicating covert actions.
    • 伺朝廷御伐之意 - Observing the imperial court's intentions regarding punishment or military action.
    • 遽命斩之 - Ordering his immediate execution, '遽' emphasizes the suddenness.
    • 甚悔 - Deeply regretting, '甚' intensifies the degree of regret.
    • 速遣送其首付妻儿 - Quickly sending his head to his wife and children, a grim and ironic gesture.
    • 以针纫颈 - Stiching the neck with a needle, a gruesome detail.
    • 俄顷复活 - Reviving shortly after, '俄顷' means a short time.
    • 以药傅之 - Applying medicine to the wound, '傅' means to apply.
    • 惧智光 - Fearful of Zhiguang, '惧' conveys fear.
    • 使人告光曰 - Sending someone to inform Zhiguang, '使人' means sending a messenger.
    • 本蒲人 - Originally from Puzhou, a place name.
    • 归葬 - Returning to be buried, '归葬' means to return for burial.
    • 亦赙之 - Also providing a funeral gift, '赙' means to provide a funeral gift.
    • 浃旬 - Ten days, '浃旬' is a classical way of saying ten days.
    • 疮平愈 - The wound healed completely, '平愈' means to heal completely.
    • 改姓他游 - Changing his name and wandering elsewhere, '他游' means to wander elsewhere.
    • 晨衙 - Morning court session, a formal meeting.
    • 投刺 - Submitting a visiting card, a formal introduction.
    • 敕吏 - Imperial officer, a term of respect.
    • 明公昔为周智光从事 - You were once a subordinate of Zhou Zhiguang, '从事' means subordinate.
    • 因叙其本末 - Telling the whole story, '本末' means the beginning and end.
    • 省悟 - Realizing, '省悟' means to realize or understand.
    • 与缣帛 - Giving silk and cloth, '缣帛' refers to fine silk and cloth.
    • 揖之而去 - Bowing and leaving, '揖' means to bow.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 唐大历元年 - The first year of the Dali era during the Tang Dynasty, marking a specific historical period.
    • 华州 - A prefecture in the Tang Dynasty, located in present-day Shaanxi Province.
    • 刺史 - A high-ranking official responsible for both civil and military administration in a prefecture.
    • 谋反 - Rebellion, a serious crime punishable by death in imperial China.
    • 京 - The capital city, Chang'an (modern Xi'an) during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 朝廷 - The imperial court, the center of political power.
    • 御伐 - Punishment or military action, often used in the context of suppressing rebellion.
    • 周智光 - A historical figure known for his rebellious activities and eventual downfall.
    • 邵进 - A subordinate of Zhou Zhiguang who was sent on a secret mission.
    • 崔 - A historical figure who served as the governor of Songzhou.
    • 宋州 - A prefecture in the Tang Dynasty, located in present-day Henan Province.

    Proper Nouns

    • 唐大历元年 - The first year of the Dali era during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 周智光 - A historical figure known for his rebellious activities.
    • 华州 - A prefecture in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 邵进 - A subordinate of Zhou Zhiguang.
    • 京 - The capital city, Chang'an (modern Xi'an).
    • 崔 - A historical figure who served as the governor of Songzhou.
    • 宋州 - A prefecture in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 蒲 - A place name, likely referring to a region in Shanxi Province.

    In the first year of the Dali era of the Tang Dynasty, Zhou Zhiguang served as the governor of Huazhou. He robbed and stripped travelers, and soon intended to rebel. He sent his subordinate Shao Jin secretly to the capital to observe the imperial court's intentions regarding punishment or military action. Upon his return, Shao Jin reported, "The court has no doubt about your loyalty." Zhou Zhiguang became angry, believing that Shao Jin was flattering the court and deceiving him, and immediately ordered his execution. However, he soon deeply regretted this decision and quickly sent Shao Jin's head to his wife and children. His wife then stitched the neck with a needle. Shortly after, Shao Jin revived and applied medicine to the wound. Despite this, he remained fearful of Zhou Zhiguang. She sent someone to inform Zhou Zhiguang, saying, "Shao Jin is originally from Puzhou and now wishes to return for burial." Zhou Zhiguang also provided a funeral gift. After arriving in Puzhou, Shao Jin's wound healed completely within ten days. He then changed his name and wandered elsewhere. Thirty years later, Cui Sheng served as the governor of Songzhou. One morning, during a court session, a man submitted a visiting card and said, "Imperial officer." Cui Sheng summoned him and asked about his purpose. The man replied, "You were once a subordinate of Zhou Zhiguang." He then recounted the entire story. Cui Sheng realized the truth and gave him silk and cloth. The man bowed and left. [95]

    汉夏侯婴以功封滕公。及死将葬，未及墓，引车马踣地不前。使人掘之，得一石室。室中有铭曰：“佳城郁郁，三千年见白日。吁嗟！滕公居此室。”遂改卜焉。
    　　李灌者，不知何许人。性孤静。常次洪州建昌县，倚舟于岸。岸有小蓬室，下有一病波斯。灌悯其将尽，以汤粥给之。数日而卒。临绝，指所卧黑毡曰：“中有一珠，可径寸。”将酬其惠。及死，毡有微光溢耀。灌取视得珠。买棺葬之，密以珠内胡口中，植木志墓。其后十年，复过旧邑。时杨凭为观察使，有外国符牒，以胡人死于建昌逆旅，其粥食之家，皆被梏讯经年。灌因问其罪，囚具言本末。灌告县寮，偕往郭番伐树，树已合拱矣。发棺视死胡，貌如生，乃于口中探得一珠还之。其夕，棹舟而去，不知所往。 [96]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [96]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 以功封滕公 - Classical expression meaning 'bestowed the title of Duke of Teng for his merits.'
    • 引車馬踣地不前 - Literally 'the carriage and horses stumbled and could not proceed,' used to describe a supernatural or symbolic event.
    • 佳城郁郁 - Poetic phrase meaning 'the noble tomb is lush and flourishing.'
    • 三千年見白日 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to see the light of day after three thousand years,' symbolizing eternal life or immortality.
    • 性孤静 - Classical description of a person who is solitary and quiet.
    • 次洪州建昌县 - Means 'stopped at Jianchang County in Hongzhou,' indicating a geographical location.
    • 病波斯 - Refers to a sick Persian, indicating a foreigner or someone of Persian descent.
    • 引汤粥给之 - Means 'gave him soup and porridge,' describing acts of kindness.
    • 将卒 - Means 'on the verge of death.'
    • 植木志墓 - Means 'planted a marker at the tomb,' indicating a memorial.
    • 符牒 - Refers to official documents or letters.
    • 逆旅 - Means 'inn' or 'temporary lodging.'
    • 梏讯 - Means 'to be shackled and interrogated.'
    • 本末 - Means 'the beginning and end' or 'the whole story.'
    • 伐树 - Means 'to cut down the tree.'
    • 合拱 - Means 'as thick as two hands clasped together,' indicating a large tree.
    • 发棺视死胡 - Means 'opened the coffin to see the dead Persian,' describing a physical action.
    • 貌如生 - Means 'looked as if alive,' indicating a well-preserved body.
    • 探得一珠还之 - Means 'found a pearl and returned it,' describing a specific action.
    • 棹舟而去 - Means 'rowed the boat away,' indicating departure.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 汉夏侯婴 - Refers to Xiahou Ying, a general under Emperor Gaozu of Han.
    • 滕公 - The title bestowed upon Xiahou Ying, indicating his noble status.
    • 洪州建昌县 - Refers to Jianchang County in Hongzhou, a historical place name.
    • 波斯 - Refers to Persia, indicating a foreigner.
    • 杨凭 - Refers to Yang Ping, an official during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 观察使 - Refers to a high-ranking official in charge of a region during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 外国符牒 - Refers to official documents from foreign countries, indicating diplomatic or administrative matters.
    • 逆旅 - Refers to an inn or temporary lodging, indicating a place where travelers stay.
    • 梏讯 - Refers to the practice of shackling and interrogating suspects, indicating a form of punishment or investigation.

    Proper Nouns

    • 汉夏侯婴 - Refers to Xiahou Ying, a historical figure.
    • 滕公 - The title bestowed upon Xiahou Ying.
    • 洪州建昌县 - Refers to Jianchang County in Hongzhou.
    • 波斯 - Refers to Persia.
    • 杨凭 - Refers to Yang Ping, a historical figure.
    • 观察使 - Refers to a high-ranking official in charge of a region during the Tang Dynasty.

    Xiahou Ying, a general under Emperor Gaozu of Han, was bestowed the title of Duke of Teng for his merits. When he was about to be buried, his carriage and horses stumbled and could not proceed before reaching the tomb. Someone dug and found a stone chamber. Inside the chamber, there was an inscription that read: "The noble tomb is lush and flourishing, to see the light of day after three thousand years. Alas! Duke of Teng resides in this chamber." They then changed the burial site.

    Li Guan, whose origins are unknown, was a solitary and quiet person. He once stopped at Jianchang County in Hongzhou and docked his boat on the shore. There was a small thatched hut on the shore, and inside was a sick Persian. Li Guan pitied him as he was on the verge of death and gave him soup and porridge. After several days, the Persian died. On his deathbed, he pointed to the black felt he was lying on and said: "There is a pearl in the middle, about an inch in diameter." He wanted to repay Li Guan's kindness. After his death, there was a faint glow emanating from the felt. Li Guan took it and found the pearl. He bought a coffin to bury him, secretly placing the pearl in the Persian's mouth, and planted a marker at the tomb.

    Ten years later, he passed through the old county again. At that time, Yang Ping was the governor of the region, and there were official documents from foreign countries, stating that a Persian had died in a roadside inn in Jianchang County, and those who had fed him were being shackled and interrogated for a long time. Li Guan asked about the reason for their punishment, and the prisoner explained the whole story. Li Guan informed the county officials, and they went together to cut down the tree, which was as thick as two hands clasped together. They opened the coffin to see the dead Persian, who looked as if he were still alive. They found a pearl in his mouth and returned it. That night, Li Guan rowed his boat away, and no one knew where he went. [96]

    陇州吴山县，有一人乘白马夜行，凡县人皆梦之语曰：“我欲移居，暂假尔牛。”言讫即过。其夕，数百家牛及明皆被体汗流如水。于县南山曲出一湫，方圆百余步。里人以此湫因牛而迁，谓之“特牛湫”也。
    　　齐庄公时，有里征者，讼三年而狱不决。公乃使二人具一羊，诅于社。二子将羊而刺之，洒其血，羊起触二子，殪于盟所。
    　　梁夏侯为九列，家贫而好置乐。妓无衣装饰，客至，即令隔帘奏曲。时人以帘为夏侯妓衣。
    　　尉迟敬德善夺槊，齐王元吉亦善用槊，高祖于显德殿前试之，谓敬德曰：“闻卿善夺槊，令元吉执槊去刃。”敬德曰：“虽加刃，亦不能害。”于是加刃。顷刻之际，敬德三夺之，元吉大惭。
    　　李适之入仕，不历丞簿，便为别驾；不历两畿官，便为京兆尹；不历御史及中丞，便为大夫；不为两省给舍，便为宰相；不历刺史，便为节度使。
    　　贺知章乘醉赋诗，问左右曰：“纸多少”纸尽诗穷。 [97]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [97]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 凡县人皆梦之语曰 - An idiomatic expression meaning 'all the people in the county dreamed that he said.'
    • 暂假尔牛 - Means 'temporarily borrow your ox.'
    • 被体汗流如水 - Means 'sweat flowed like water all over their bodies.'
    • 谓之“特牛湫”也 - Means 'it is called 'Terniu Pool'.'
    • 里征者，讼三年而狱不决 - Means 'a man named Li Zheng, whose lawsuit lasted three years without resolution.'
    • 具一羊，诅于社 - Means 'prepared a sheep to curse at the altar.'
    • 洒其血，羊起触二子，殪于盟所 - Means 'they sprinkled its blood, the sheep got up and touched the two men, killing them at the site of the oath.'
    • 不历丞簿，便为别驾 - Means 'without serving as a county magistrate, he became a deputy governor.'
    • 不历两畿官，便为京兆尹 - Means 'without serving in the capital region, he became the governor of Jingzhao.'
    • 不历御史及中丞，便为大夫 - Means 'without serving as an inspector or vice-minister, he became a doctor (high-ranking official).'
    • 不为两省给舍，便为宰相 - Means 'without serving as a secretary in the two provinces, he became a chancellor.'
    • 不历刺史，便为节度使 - Means 'without serving as a prefect, he became a military governor.'
    • 乘醉赋诗，问左右曰 - Means 'drunk, he composed poetry and asked his attendants.'
    • 纸尽诗穷 - Means 'when the paper ran out, the poem ended.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 陇州吴山县 - Refers to Wushan County in Longzhou, a place in ancient China.
    • 特牛湫 - A pool named after an ox, with a local legend.
    • 齐庄公 - Refers to Duke Qi of Qi, a ruler during the Spring and Autumn period.
    • 里征 - A man named Li Zheng, involved in a legal dispute.
    • 社 - Refers to the ancestral altar or a place for public oaths.
    • 梁夏侯 - Refers to Xiahou Yi, a high-ranking official in the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 两畿官 - Refers to officials in the capital regions.
    • 京兆尹 - The highest administrative official in the capital region.
    • 御史及中丞 - Refers to inspectors and vice-ministers.
    • 两省给舍 - Refers to secretaries in the two provinces.
    • 宰相 - The highest-ranking minister in the imperial court.
    • 刺史 - Refers to prefects, local administrators.
    • 节度使 - Refers to military governors.
    • 李适之 - Refers to Li Shi, a high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty.
    • 贺知章 - Refers to He Zhizhang, a famous poet and official in the Tang dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 陇州吴山县 - Wushan County in Longzhou.
    • 特牛湫 - Terniu Pool.
    • 齐庄公 - Duke Qi of Qi.
    • 里征 - Li Zheng.
    • 社 - Ancestral altar or place for public oaths.
    • 梁夏侯 - Xiahou Yi.
    • 两畿官 - Officials in the capital regions.
    • 京兆尹 - Governor of Jingzhao.
    • 御史及中丞 - Inspectors and vice-ministers.
    • 两省给舍 - Secretaries in the two provinces.
    • 宰相 - Chancellor.
    • 刺史 - Prefects.
    • 节度使 - Military governors.
    • 李适之 - Li Shi.
    • 贺知章 - He Zhizhang.

    In Wushan County of Longzhou, there was a man riding a white horse who traveled at night. All the people in the county dreamed that he said, "I wish to move, and I will temporarily borrow your ox." After he spoke, he passed by. That night, hundreds of oxen from various households were drenched in sweat like water by the next morning. In the bend of the southern mountains of the county, a pool emerged, covering an area of more than a hundred steps. The locals believed that this pool had moved due to the oxen, and thus named it "Terniu Pool."

    During the reign of Duke Qi of Qi, there was a man named Li Zheng whose lawsuit lasted for three years without resolution. The duke then ordered two men to prepare a sheep to curse at the altar. The two men killed the sheep and sprinkled its blood, but the sheep suddenly got up and struck the two men, killing them at the site of the oath.

    Xiahou Yi, a high-ranking official, was poor but enjoyed setting up music. His courtesans lacked clothing and decorations; when guests arrived, he would have them play music behind a curtain. At the time, people referred to the curtain as Xiahou Yi's courtesans' attire.

    Yuchi Jingde was skilled at disarming spears, and Prince Yuanji of Qi was also skilled in using spears. Emperor Gaozu tested them in front of the Xiande Hall, saying to Jingde, "I have heard that you are good at disarming spears. Have Yuanji hold a spear without a blade." Jingde replied, "Even if there is a blade, I cannot harm him." So, a blade was added. In the blink of an eye, Jingde disarmed Yuanji three times, causing Yuanji great embarrassment.

    Li Shi entered officialdom without serving as a county magistrate, becoming a deputy governor; without serving in the capital regions, he became the governor of Jingzhao; without serving as an inspector or vice-minister, he became a high-ranking official; without serving as a secretary in the two provinces, he became a chancellor; without serving as a prefect, he became a military governor.

    He Zhizhang, drunk, composed poetry and asked his attendants, "How much paper do you have?" When the paper ran out, the poem ended. [97]

    长兴于相与举人裴丘友善。丘有一古镜，所常宝者。布素时，曾一照，分明见有朱衣吏导从。他皆类此。其镜旋亦坠矣。
    　　丞相邹平公段文昌，负才傲俗，落泊荆楚间。尝半酣，ヒ履于江陵大街往来，雨霁泥甚，街侧有大宅枕渠，公乘醉于渠上脱履濯足，旁若无人。自言：“我作江陵节度使，必买此宅。”闻者皆笑。其后果镇荆南，遂买此宅。
    　　崔圆微时，欲举进士，于魏县见李含章。云：“君合武举出身，官更不停，直至宰相。”开元二十三年，应将帅举，又于河南充乡贡进士。其日于福唐观试，遇敕下便于试场中召拜执戟，参谋河西军事。后官果不停，不逾二十年拜中书令、赵国公。又圆常作司勋员外，初释服，往见会昌寺克慎师，师笑云：“人皆自台入省，公乃自省入台。从此常合在枪槊中，后当大贵。”无何为刑部员外郎兼侍御史，充剑南节度。后入剑门，每行常有兵戈，未逾一年，便致勋业。崔入蜀，常自说其如此。 [98]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [98]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 長興於相與舉人裴丘友善 - 長興與相及舉人裴丘為友。
    • 曾一照，分明見有朱衣吏導從 - 曾一照，清楚地看到有穿紅衣的官吏引導。
    • 其鏡旋亦墜矣 - 鏡子很快就掉了下來。
    • 丞相鄒平公段文昌 - 鄒平公段文昌，丞相。
    • 負才傲俗 - 有才華而傲視世俗。
    • 落泊荊楚間 - 在荊楚地區流浪。
    • 嘗半酣，ヒ履於江陵大街往來 - 曾半醉時，在江陵的大街上來來往往。
    • 雨霁泥甚 - 雨後泥很厚。
    • 街側有大宅枕渠 - 街邊有座大宅靠近水渠。
    • 公乘醉於渠上脱履濯足，旁若無人 - 公乘醉時，在水渠上脫鞋洗腳，旁若無人。
    • 我作江陵節度使，必買此宅 - 我若為江陵節度使，必買此宅。
    • 其后果鎮荊南，遂買此宅 - 後來果然鎮守荊南，並買下此宅。
    • 崔圓微時，欲舉進士 - 崔圓在卑微時，想參加進士考試。
    • 於魏縣見李含章 - 在魏縣見到李含章。
    • 君合武舉出身，官更不停，直至宰相 - 你應該通過武舉出身，官運亨通，直至宰相。
    • 開元二十三年，應將帥舉 - 開元二十三年，應將帥舉。
    • 又於河南充鄉貢進士 - 又在河南充鄉貢進士。
    • 其日於福唐觀試 - 那天在福唐觀考試。
    • 遇敕下便於試場中召拜執戟，參謀河西軍事 - 遇到詔書便在試場中被召拜為執戟，參謀河西軍事。
    • 後官果不停，不逾二十年拜中書令、趙國公 - 後官運果然亨通，不到二十年便拜中書令、趙國公。
    • 又圓常作司勳員外 - 又常任司勳員外。
    • 初釋服，往見會昌寺克慎師 - 初釋服，去見會昌寺克慎師。
    • 人皆自臺入省，公乃自省入臺 - 人們通常從臺到省，而公則從省到臺。
    • 從此常合在槍槊中，後當大貴 - 從此常在槍槊中，後當大貴。
    • 無何為刑部員外郎兼侍御史 - 不久任刑部員外郎兼侍御史。
    • 充劍南節度 - 充任劍南節度。
    • 後入劍門，每行常有兵戈 - 後入劍門，每行常有兵戈。
    • 未逾一年，便致勳業 - 未逾一年，便致勳業。
    • 崔入蜀，常自說其如此 - 崔入蜀，常自說其如此。

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 長興 - 可能是一個名字，也可能是一個稱號。
    • 相 - 可能是一個名字，也可能是一個稱號。
    • 裴丘 - 一名舉子的名字。
    • 朱衣吏 - 穿紅衣的官吏，通常指高級官員。
    • 段文昌 - 唐朝宰相。
    • 鄒平公 - 一個稱號，可能指一位貴族或高級官員。
    • 荊楚 - 指荊楚地區，位於今天的湖北和湖南省。
    • 江陵 - 今天的荊州，湖北省。
    • 崔圓 - 唐朝高級官員。
    • 魏縣 - 今天的河北省魏縣。
    • 李含章 - 一名歷史人物。
    • 武舉 - 武舉，一種針對軍事人才的科舉考試。
    • 進士 - 科舉考試中成功的考生獲得的學位。
    • 開元二十三年 - 唐玄宗開元二十三年，即公元735年。
    • 河南 - 今天的河南省。
    • 福唐觀 - 考試舉行的地點。
    • 執戟 - 執戟，一種守衛或侍從。
    • 河西 - 河西走廊以西的地區，指今天的西域。
    • 中書令 - 秘書省的長官，高級官員。
    • 趙國公 - 一個貴族稱號。
    • 司勳員外 - 司勳部門的官員。
    • 會昌寺 - 一座寺廟，可能位於今天的河南省。
    • 克慎師 - 一位和尚，可能是一位受人尊敬的教師。
    • 劍南 - 秦嶺以南的地區，包括今天的四川省。
    • 劍門 - 劍門關，四川省的一個戰略要塞。
    • 蜀 - 今天的四川省。

    Changxing befriended the candidate Pei Qiu. Pei Qiu possessed an ancient mirror, which he highly valued. During his humble days, Changxing once looked at the mirror and clearly saw officials in red robes leading the way. Other incidents were similar. The mirror soon fell.

    Chancellor of Zouping, Duan Wenchang, was talented and disdainful of the common people. He wandered in the Jing and Chu regions. Once half-drunk, he walked on the streets of Jiangling, where the mud was very thick after the rain cleared. On the side of the street, there was a large house next to a canal. The official, drunk, took off his shoes and washed his feet on the canal, as if no one was around. He said, "If I become the military governor of Jiangling, I will definitely buy this house." Those who heard him laughed. Later, he indeed became the military governor of Jingnan and bought the house.

    Cui Yuan, in his humble days, wanted to take the imperial examination. In Weixian, he met Li Hanzhang, who said, "You should pass the martial examination. Your career will not stop, and you will eventually become a chancellor." In the 23rd year of Emperor Xuanzong's reign (735 AD), he took the examination for military leaders and was also selected as a provincial candidate for the imperial examination in Henan. That day, at the examination hall in Futang, he encountered an imperial decree and was summoned and appointed as a guard, serving as a military advisor in the Western Regions. Later, his career indeed did not stop, and within twenty years, he was appointed as Grand Secretary and Duke of Zhao. Later, he served as an official in the Department of Merit. After mourning, he went to see Master Keshen at Huichang Temple. The master smiled and said, "Everyone usually goes from the Ministry to the Secretariat, but you go from the Secretariat to the Ministry. From now on, you will often be in the midst of weapons, and you will eventually become very powerful." Soon, he became a Deputy Minister of Justice and a Censor, serving as the military governor of Jian Nan. Later, when entering Jianmen Pass, there were always weapons around. Within a year, he achieved great achievements. When Cui entered Shu, he often said that it was like this. [98]

    ●附录
    　　唐许敬宗奢豪，尝造飞楼七十间，令妓女走马于其上，以为戏乐。
    　　大历中，将作少匠韩晋卿女，适尚衣奉御韦隐。隐奉使新罗，行及一程，怆然有思，因就寝，乃觉其妻在帐外，惊问之。答曰：“愍君涉海，志愿奔而随之。人无知者。”隐即诈左右曰：“欲纳一妓，将侍枕席。”人无怪者。及归，已二年。妻亦随至。隐乃启舅姑，首其罪。而室中宛存焉。及相近，翕然合体。其从隐者，乃魂也。 [99]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [99]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 附录 - Refers to an appendix or supplementary material.
    • 尝造飞楼七十间 - Means 'once built seventy flying pavilions,' where '飞楼' refers to elevated or multi-tiered structures.
    • 令妓女走马于其上 - Describes a scene where courtesans were made to gallop on top of these structures for entertainment.
    • 以为戏乐 - Means 'for entertainment.'
    • 怆然有思 - Means 'felt sorrowful and reflective.'
    • 乃觉其妻在帐外 - Means 'he then felt his wife was outside the tent.'
    • 愍君涉海 - Means 'I pity you for crossing the sea.'
    • 志愿奔而随之 - Means 'and I wish to follow you.'
    • 人无知者 - Means 'no one knows about it.'
    • 乃诈左右曰 - Means 'he then deceived his attendants by saying.'
    • 欲纳一妓 - Means 'he wants to take in a courtesan.'
    • 将侍枕席 - Means 'to serve him in bed.'
    • 无怪者 - Means 'no one found it strange.'
    • 隐乃启舅姑 - Means 'Wei Yin then informed his in-laws.'
    • 首其罪 - Means 'confessing his sin.'
    • 而室中宛存焉 - Means 'but the room remained as it was.'
    • 及相近 - Means 'when they came close.'
    • 翕然合体 - Means 'they merged into one body.'
    • 其从隐者 - Means 'the one who followed him.'
    • 乃魂也 - Means 'was a spirit.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 唐 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty (618–907 CE), a period of great cultural and political achievement in Chinese history.
    • 许敬宗 - A prominent official during the Tang Dynasty known for his political influence and luxurious lifestyle.
    • 大历 - A reign title of Emperor Dezong of Tang, covering the years 766–779 CE.
    • 新罗 - An ancient Korean kingdom that was a significant cultural and political entity in East Asia during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 尚衣奉御 - A high-ranking official responsible for the emperor's clothing and attire.
    • 魂 - Refers to a spirit or ghost, a concept deeply rooted in Chinese beliefs about the afterlife and spiritual existence.

    Proper Nouns

    • 唐许敬宗 - Xu Jingzong, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 大历 - The reign title of Emperor Dezong of Tang.
    • 韩晋卿 - Han Jinqing, a craftsman during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 韦隐 - Wei Yin, a court official responsible for the emperor's clothing.
    • 新罗 - Silla, an ancient Korean kingdom.
    • 魂 - Spirit or ghost.

    Appendix

    During the Tang Dynasty, Xu Jingzong, known for his extravagance and luxury, once built seventy flying pavilions and had courtesans gallop on top of them for entertainment.

    In the middle of the Dali era, Han Jinqing's daughter, who had married Wei Yin, an official responsible for the emperor's clothing, accompanied him. Wei Yin was sent on a mission to Silla. After traveling one stage, he felt sorrowful and reflective. He then felt his wife was outside the tent and was surprised. He asked her, and she replied, "I pity you for crossing the sea, and I wish to follow you. No one knows about it." Wei Yin then deceived his attendants by saying, "I want to take in a courtesan to serve me in bed." No one found it strange. By the time he returned, two years had passed, and his wife also arrived. Wei Yin then informed his in-laws, confessing his sin. But the room remained as it was. When they came close, they merged into one body. The one who followed him was a spirit. [99]

    晋羊祜三岁时，乳母抱行，乃令于东邻树孔中探得金环。东邻之人云：“吾儿七岁堕井死。曾弄金环，失其处所。”乃验祜前身，东邻子也。
    　　唐敦煌李鹬，开元中，为邵州刺史，挈家之任，泛洞庭，时晴景，登岸，因鼻衄，血沙上，为江鼍所舐。俄然复生一鹬，其形体衣服言语，与其身无异。鹬之本身，为鼍法所制，絷于水中。其妻子家人迎奉鼍妖就任。州人亦不能觉悟。为郡几数年。因天下大旱，西江可涉，道士叶静能自罗浮山赴玄宗急诏，过洞庭，忽沙中见一人面缚，问曰：“君何为者”鹬以状对。静能书一符帖巨石上，石即飞起空中。鼍妖方拥案晨衙，为巨石所击，乃复本形。时张说为岳州刺史，具奏，并以舟楫送鹬赴郡，家人妻子乃信。今舟行者，相戒不沥血于波中，以此故也。 [100]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [100]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 乳母抱行 - Literal phrase meaning 'the wet nurse carried him,' implying the child was very young.
    • 东邻树孔中探得金环 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'found a golden ring in a hole in the neighbor's tree,' symbolizing an unusual and significant event.
    • 吾儿七岁堕井死 - Literal phrase meaning 'my son fell into a well and died at seven years old,' indicating a tragic event.
    • 曾弄金环，失其处所 - Literal phrase meaning 'he once played with a golden ring and lost its location,' suggesting the ring was significant to the child.
    • 验祜前身 - Phrase meaning 'verify his previous life,' indicating a belief in reincarnation.
    • 东邻子也 - Phrase meaning 'he was the son of the neighbor,' confirming the identity of the child in his previous life.
    • 泛洞庭 - Literal phrase meaning 'sail across Dongting Lake,' indicating a journey.
    • 鼻衄，血沙上 - Literal phrase meaning 'nosebleed, blood on the sand,' describing a physical symptom.
    • 为江鼍所舐 - Phrase meaning 'was licked by a river crocodile,' indicating a supernatural encounter.
    • 俄然复生一鹬 - Phrase meaning 'suddenly a new Ruyu was born,' describing a miraculous event.
    • 其形体衣服言语，与其身无异 - Phrase meaning 'its body, clothing, and speech were no different from his,' indicating a complete transformation.
    • 为鼍法所制，絷于水中 - Phrase meaning 'was bound by the crocodile's magic and tied in the water,' indicating a state of captivity.
    • 妻子家人迎奉鼍妖就任 - Phrase meaning 'his wife, children, and family welcomed the crocodile demon to take office,' indicating a state of acceptance.
    • 州人亦不能觉悟 - Phrase meaning 'the people of the state could not realize,' indicating a lack of awareness.
    • 天下大旱，西江可涉 - Phrase meaning 'a great drought, the West River could be crossed,' indicating a significant natural event.
    • 道士叶静能自罗浮山赴玄宗急诏 - Phrase meaning 'the Taoist Ye Jingneng from Luofu Mountain came to answer the urgent edict of Emperor Xuanzong,' indicating a religious figure responding to a royal command.
    • 忽沙中见一人面缚 - Phrase meaning 'suddenly saw a man bound face down in the sand,' indicating a supernatural encounter.
    • 可为师表 - Phrase meaning 'can serve as a teacher and model,' indicating a high status.
    • 无不利于事也 - Phrase meaning 'nothing is unhelpful in matters,' indicating a general statement of value.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋羊祜 - Refers to Yang Hu, a prominent official during the Jin Dynasty.
    • 乳母 - Refers to a wet nurse, a common practice in ancient China.
    • 东邻 - Refers to a neighbor, indicating a close community relationship.
    • 金环 - A golden ring, often symbolizing wealth or status.
    • 鼍 - A river crocodile, often associated with water spirits or demons in Chinese folklore.
    • 李鹬 - Refers to Li Ruyu, a historical figure during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 开元 - Refers to the Kaiyuan era, a period of prosperity during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 邵州 - Refers to a region in Hunan Province.
    • 洞庭 - Refers to Dongting Lake, one of the largest freshwater lakes in China.
    • 玄宗 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a famous emperor known for his cultural achievements.
    • 罗浮山 - Refers to Luofu Mountain, a sacred mountain in Guangdong Province.
    • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest, indicating a religious figure.
    • 叶静能 - Refers to Ye Jingneng, a famous Taoist priest during the Tang Dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋羊祜 - Yang Hu, a prominent official during the Jin Dynasty.
    • 东邻 - Neighbor, indicating a close community relationship.
    • 金环 - Golden ring, often symbolizing wealth or status.
    • 鼍 - River crocodile, often associated with water spirits or demons in Chinese folklore.
    • 李鹬 - Li Ruyu, a historical figure during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 开元 - Kaiyuan era, a period of prosperity during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 邵州 - Region in Hunan Province.
    • 洞庭 - Dongting Lake, one of the largest freshwater lakes in China.
    • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a famous emperor known for his cultural achievements.
    • 罗浮山 - Luofu Mountain, a sacred mountain in Guangdong Province.
    • 道士 - Taoist priest, indicating a religious figure.
    • 叶静能 - Ye Jingneng, a famous Taoist priest during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 张说 - Zhang Shuo, a prominent official during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 岳州 - Region in Hunan Province.

    During the Jin Dynasty, Yang Hu, when he was three years old, was carried by his wet nurse. She took him to a hole in a tree near their neighbor's house, where he found a golden ring. The neighbor said, "My son fell into a well and died at seven years old. He once played with a golden ring and lost its location." Upon investigation, it was discovered that Yang Hu's previous life was that of the neighbor's son.

    In the Tang Dynasty, during the Kaiyuan period, Li Ruyu, from Dunhuang, served as the governor of Shaozhou. He took his family with him on a journey across Dongting Lake. One day, while the weather was clear, he disembarked and suffered a nosebleed, with blood staining the sand. He was licked by a river crocodile. Suddenly, a new Ruyu was born, whose body, clothing, and speech were no different from his own. The original Ruyu was bound by the crocodile's magic and tied in the water. His wife, children, and family welcomed the crocodile demon to take office. The people of the state could not realize the truth. He remained in office for several years. During a great drought, the West River could be crossed. The Taoist priest Ye Jingneng from Luofu Mountain came to answer the urgent edict of Emperor Xuanzong, passing through Dongting Lake. He suddenly saw a man bound face down in the sand and asked, "What are you doing?" Ruyu explained the situation. Ye Jingneng wrote a talisman on a large stone, which flew into the air. The crocodile demon, who was conducting morning court proceedings, was struck by the stone and returned to its original form. At that time, Zhang Shuo was the governor of Yuezhou. He reported the incident and sent Ruyu to the prefecture with a boat. His family and children then believed the truth. Nowadays, travelers on boats warn each other not to spill blood into the water, due to this reason. [100]

    晋羊祜三岁时，乳母抱行，乃令于东邻树孔中探得金环。东邻之人云：“吾儿七岁堕井死。曾弄金环，失其处所。”乃验祜前身，东邻子也。
    　　唐敦煌李鹬，开元中，为邵州刺史，挈家之任，泛洞庭，时晴景，登岸，因鼻衄，血沙上，为江鼍所舐。俄然复生一鹬，其形体衣服言语，与其身无异。鹬之本身，为鼍法所制，絷于水中。其妻子家人迎奉鼍妖就任。州人亦不能觉悟。为郡几数年。因天下大旱，西江可涉，道士叶静能自罗浮山赴玄宗急诏，过洞庭，忽沙中见一人面缚，问曰：“君何为者”鹬以状对。静能书一符帖巨石上，石即飞起空中。鼍妖方拥案晨衙，为巨石所击，乃复本形。时张说为岳州刺史，具奏，并以舟楫送鹬赴郡，家人妻子乃信。今舟行者，相戒不沥血于波中，以此故也。

    During the Jin Dynasty, Yang Hu, when he was three years old, was carried by his wet nurse. She took him to a hole in a tree near their neighbor's house, where he found a golden ring. The neighbor said, "My son fell into a well and died at seven years old. He once played with a golden ring and lost its location." Upon investigation, it was discovered that Yang Hu's previous life was that of the neighbor's son.

    In the Tang Dynasty, during the Kaiyuan period, Li Ruyu, from Dunhuang, served as the governor of Shaozhou. He took his family with him on a journey across Dongting Lake. One day, while the weather was clear, he disembarked and suffered a nosebleed, with blood staining the sand. He was licked by a river crocodile. Suddenly, a new Ruyu was born, whose body, clothing, and speech were no different from his own. The original Ruyu was bound by the crocodile's magic and tied in the water. His wife, children, and family welcomed the crocodile demon to take office. The people of the state could not realize the truth. He remained in office for several years. During a great drought, the West River could be crossed. The Taoist priest Ye Jingneng from Luofu Mountain came to answer the urgent edict of Emperor Xuanzong, passing through Dongting Lake. He suddenly saw a man bound face down in the sand and asked, "What are you doing?" Ruyu explained the situation. Ye Jingneng wrote a talisman on a large stone, which flew into the air. The crocodile demon, who was conducting morning court proceedings, was struck by the stone and returned to its original form. At that time, Zhang Shuo was the governor of Yuezhou. He reported the incident and sent Ruyu to the prefecture with a boat. His family and children then believed the truth. Nowadays, travelers on boats warn each other not to spill blood into the water, due to this reason.
